inscription.head:
No. 1.
(A.R. No. 581 of 1925.)
ON A WHITE MARBLE PILLAR NEAR THE ENTRANCE INTO THE TEMPLE OF
RĀMALINGASVĀMI AT VĒLPŪRU, SATTENEPALLI TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p:
This is the only stone inscription of this dynasty so far found and it is damaged
and incomplete. Only the name of the family Vishṇukuṇḍi and that of a ruler
Mādhavavarmma are legible.

inscription.head:
No. 2.
(A.R. No. 839 of 1922.)
ON A SLAB BUILT INTO A PICKOTA TO THE EAST OF THE VILLAGE OF
DHARMAVARAM, ONGOLE TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
The year is lost.
inscription.p:
Damaged. Seems to record a gift made by Paṇḍaraṅgu, general of Guṇake-
nallaṇḍu. Refers to certain exploits of Paṇḍaraṅgu and also to a general Bejeyaṇḍu,
brother of Ayyaparāju.

l.te: • • • • rutaṁbu daṇḍanāthu nacalapuramu • • ccena calituṇḍu valla • • • • • •
• • • cōḍani jēkoni kāci bhūlōkaṁ beṟuṁga kiranapuramu dahiñcina • • •
baiki rāhavu • • ḍicina vallabhu kolvuna nella neṟuṁga kannarabhū[pā]-
lu • • • nnalarāju[vi]bhutōḍana daṟi • • • ya • naḍavi socce || • • • na

edition.p:
Note.—The greater portion of this inscription seems to be in Telugu verse but, as it is mostly
illegible, the verses cannot be reconstructed.

This inscription is published in Nellore Inscriptions, pp. 966 f. but the reading there is incorrect in
places.

inscription.head:
No. 3.
(A.R. No. 840 of 1922.)
ON A SLAB LEANING AGAINST THE EASTERN WALL OF THE MALLIKĀRJUNA
TEMPLE AT THE SAME VILLAGE.
The year is unknown.
inscription.p: Damaged. Refers to Paṇḍaraṅgu, Ayyama-Peggaḍa and Bejeyarāja.

l.te: bu sēsi ḍi • • • • guḍlu prākāraṁbu maṁṭa[pa]ṁbunu namayiṁcina
sa[ṁ]pannuṇḍu • maya bejeyarāju bejeyēśvara[ṁ]bu galasi sa¨rvva • • • •

• • •

edition.p:
Note.—This inscription seems to be in Telugu verse but, in its present incomplete form, the verses
cannot be reconstructed.
This record is published in Nellore Inscriptions, p. 968, but the reading there is incorrect in places.

inscription.head:
No. 4.
(A.R. No. 431 of 1915.)
ON A NANDI SLAB SET UP NEAR THE TEMPLE OF SŌMĒŚVARA AT ELŪRU, NARASARAOPETA TALUK,
SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 925.
inscription.p:
The characters appear too modern for the date given. Records the grant of land
to god Sōmanāthadēva by Parichchhēda Chikka-Bhīmarāja.

l.te: ṁ paradattāṁ vā yō harēti vaśuṁda-
na vāni pitṛpitāṁmahulu 60 vēlēṁḍlu na • ka •

inscription.head:
No. 5.
(A.R. No. 707 of 1920.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE TEMPLE OF ANANTABHŌGĒŚVARA, KOLLŪRU, TENALI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
18th year of Rājarāja.
inscription.p:
Records the gift of 7 rūka-māḍas by Bummana, son of Māviseṭṭi and Kosana-Sāni
for a perpetual lamp in the temple of Anantīśvaramahādēva of Kollūru.

l.te: lu 18 śrāhi caitra nu¨rddha sōmavārāna kollū-
pamunaku peṭṭina rūkamāḍalu 7 pa • vīniṁ jēkoni

inscription.head:
No. 6.
(A.R. No. 673 of 1920.)
ON A STONE LYING IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF SVAYAMBHUVĒŚVARA, VALIVĒRU,
SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Ś. 965.
inscription.p:
Records a gift of sheep for the upkeep of perpetual lamps in the temple of
Tripurushadēva in Valivēru in the Velanāṇḍu district, by general Tikkapayya, Aggiya-
Shaḍaṅgavibhaṭṭa and Maṇḍalika Gaṇḍaya.

inscription.head:
No. 7.
(A.R. No. 663 of 1920.)
ON A STONE BUILT INTO THE ROOF OF THE ŚIVA TEMPLE AT NANDIVELUGU,
SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Ś. 980 (37th year of Rājarāja.)
inscription.p:
Refers to the gift of goats by certain people for the upkeep of a perpetual lamp in
the temple of Appēśvaradēva, during the reign of Rājarāja.

inscription.head:
No. 8.
(A.R. No. 671 of 1920.)
ON A STONE LYING IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF SVAYAMBHŪDĒVA, VALIVĒRU,
SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Ś. 983 (41st year of Vishṇuvardhana.)
inscription.p:
States that Goṇḍava, daughter of Janniya-Bhaṭṭa gave fifty sheep for the upkeep
of a perpetual lamp in the temple of Traipurusha-Mahādēva of Valivēru in the
Velanāṇḍu six-thousand district.

inscription.head:
No. 9.
(A.R. No. 672 of 1920.)
ON A STONE LYING IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF SVAYAMBHŪDĒVA,
SAME VILLAGE—BELOW No. 671 OF 1920.
Ś. 983 (?)
inscription.p:
States that Pōtaya, son of Divākara-Peddēri and Savviya-Shaḍaṅgavi, son of
Kommiya-Shaḍaṅgavi, gave fifty sheep each for the upkeep of perpetual lamps in the
Vishṇu temple at Valivēru. No date is given but it is presumed that this inscription
belongs to the same year as No. 8.

inscription.head:
No. 10.
(A.R. No. 747 of 1920.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE MAṆḌAPA OF THE TEMPLE OF SŌMĒŚVARA, JUTTIGA,
TANUKU TALUK, WEST GODAVARI DISTRICT.
Ś. 1000 (3rd year of Vishṇuvardhana.)
inscription.p:
Records a gift made by Vēṅgi Bikirāju for the upkeep of a perpetual lamp in the
temple of Sōmēśvara. The gift seems to be of a kind of local tax (tīṟu).

edition.p: 1. The rest of the pillar is built into the wall.

inscription.head:
No. 11.
(A.R. No. 738 of 1920.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE TEMPLE OF SŌMĒŚVARA AT THE SAME VILLAGE.
(13th year of Vishṇuvardhana.)
inscription.p:
States that Vijayādityadēva raised a maṇḍapa called Karavālabhairava-maṇḍapa
in front of god Vāsukiravi-Sōmīśvara of Duttiga.

inscription.head:
No. 12.
(A.R. No. 737 of 1920.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAME TEMPLE.
(15th year of Vishṇuvardhana.)
inscription.p:
Records the gift of 50 inupa yeḍlu by Pōtiyabhaṭṭa for the upkeep of a
perpetual lamp in the temple of Vāsukiravi-Sōmēśvara.

l.te: mānika māneṇḍu sēsi neyi vūsi yā-

inscription.head:
No. 13.
(A.R. No. 748 of 1920.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE MAṆḌAPA OF THE SAME TEMPLE.
(17th year of Vishṇuvardhana.)
inscription.p:
States that Mudigoṇḍamadēvi made a gift of 50 inupa yeḍlu for the upkeep of
a perpetual lamp in the temple of Vāsukiravi-Sōmēśvara on the occasion of a lunar
eclipse.

edition.p: The rest is illegible.

inscription.head:
No. 14.
(A.R. No. 746 of 1920.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAME MAṆḌAPA.
(17th year of Vishṇuvardhana.)
inscription.p:
Registers a gift of 50 sheep for the upkeep of a perpetual lamp in the temple of
god Vāsukiravi-Sōmīśvara on the occasion of a lunar eclipse.

inscription.head:
No. 15.
(A.R. No. 731 of 1920.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAME MAṆḌAPA.
(17th year of Vishṇuvardhana.)
inscription.p:
States that Chōḍagaṅgadēva established a perpetual lamp in the temple of
Sōmīśvaradēva.

inscription.head:
No. 16.
(A.R. No. 833 of 1917.)
ON A SLAB NEAR THE GOVINDA-MAṬHA ON THE INDRAKĪLA HILL, BEZWADA, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
(17th year of Vishṇuvardhana.)
inscription.p:
States that one Chaṭṭapa consecrated the image of Pārthīśvara on the Indrakīla
hill by the grace of Vishṇuvardhana, son of Vikramāditya.

l.te:
pālaniyyō bhavadbhiḥ sa¨rvvā nētā nbhāvinaḥ
pā-

inscription.head:
No. 17.
(A.R. No. 337 of 1919.)
ON THE UPPER TIER IN THE EAST WALL OF THE TEMPLE OF BHĪMĒŚVARA, BHĪMAVARAM,
COCANADA TALUK, EAST GODAVARI DISTRICT.
([2]5th (?) year of Vishṇuvardhana.)
inscription.p:
Incomplete. Refers to the endowment of a perpetual lamp in the temple of
Chāḷukya-Bhīmēśvaramahādēva on the occasion of a lunar eclipse.

l.te:
svasti sa¨rvvalōkāśraya śrī viṣṇuva¨rddhanamahārājula prava¨rddhamāna vijayarājyasaṁvatsa
raṁbulu •

(incomplete.)

inscription.head:
No. 18.
(A.R. No. 336 of 1919.)
ON THE UPPER TIER IN THE EAST WALL OF THE TEMPLE OF BHĪMĒŚVARA, BHĪMAVARAM,
COCANADA TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
(26th year of Vishṇuvardhana.)
inscription.p: Incomplete.

l.te: svasti sa¨rvvalōkāśraya śrī viṣṇuva¨rddhanamahārājula prava¨rddhamāna vijayarājyasaṁvatsara-

inscription.head:
No. 19.
(A.R. No. 846 of 1917.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE TEMPLE OF JALADHĪŚVARASVĀMI, GHAṆṬASĀLA, DIVI TALUK,
KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. Seems to refer to a gift made to a Brāhman in Ghaṇṭaśāla.
Mentions Vishṇuvardhana-Mahārāja.

inscription.head:
No. 20.
(A.R. No. 529 of 1913.)
ON A STONE PLACED IN THE VERANDAH OF THE SUB-REGISTRAR'S OFFICE AT VINUKONDA,
VINUKONDA TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p:
States that the stone was set up by a local potentate named Paḍavalu Kattirāju,
who bore several birudas such as Immaḍigaṇḍa, Kalliga-Nārāyaṇa and [Gh]aṇḍa-
Śārdūla.

inscription.head:
No. 21.
(A.R. No. 379 of 1915.)
ON A BROKEN PILLAR IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF CHENNAKĒŚAVASVĀMI, KŌLALAPŪḌI,
NARASARAOPETA TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Date missing.
inscription.p: Damaged. Refers to a gift by somebody.

inscription.head:
No. 22.
(A.R. No. 536 of 1909.)
ON A MUTILATED SLAB DUG UP IN THE COURT-YARD OF THE TEMPLE OF MALLĒŚVARA,
BEZWADA, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Date missing.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. Refers to Pallakētabhūpāla of the Kāḍveṭṭi family. Incidentally
mentions many favours bestowed by god Mallēśvara on Arjuna, Mādhavavarmma
and Paṇḍitārādhya.

l.te: raṇītalē || tata svaputrēṇa hatē tiṁtriṇi jīvini nutē dha¨rmmasa-

edition.l:
• • • • • tapratāpa
(Below this the stone is broken)

inscription.head:
No. 23.
(A.R. No. 359 of 1920.)
ON A STONE LYING IN A FIELD NORTH-EAST OF KOṆḌUPALLI, GOOTY TALUK,
ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
(23rd year of Vijayāditya.)
inscription.p:
Refers to a gift of land made by some one while Vikramāditya Bali-Indra
Bāṇarāja of Bali-kula was governing Turumara province during the reign of
Vijayāditya.

l.te: ja paramēśvara bhaṭāra[ḷa]ku vijayarājyasa[ṁ]vatsaraṁbu ḷirubādi[mūṁ]ḍu prava-

inscription.head:
No. 24.
(A.R. No. 364 of 1920.)
ON A STONE LYING IN A FIELD TO THE NORTH-EAST OF THE VILLAGE, DIMMAGUDI,
SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
(27th year of Vikramāditya.)
inscription.p: Incomplete. Seems to refer to a gift made in the reign of Vikramāditya.

inscription.head:
No. 25.
(A.R. No. 396 of 1915.)
ON A MUTILATED SLAB IN A FIELD IN NĀDIṆḌLA, NĀRASARAOPETA TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
(Chāḷukya Vikrama year 46.)
inscription.p: Incomplete and damaged. Mentions the Mahāpradhā[na] Anantapāla.

edition.p:
Note.—The latter part of the inscription seems to be in Telugu verse but the verses cannot be
reconstructed as many words are missing.

inscription.head:
No. 26.
(A.R. No. 710 of 1920.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE TEMPLE OF ANANTABHŌGĒŚVARA, KOLLŪRU, TENALI TALUK,
SAME DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p:
Refers to the gift of the village of Mulkalapāḍu in the province of Ojīvi to god
Anantīśvara. The donor's name is lost. Refers to the Chāḷukya king Tribhuvana-
malladēva (Vikramāditya VI) who was ruling at Kalyāṇapura.

l.te: yatē krimiḥ || gā mēkāṁ ratnikā mēkāṁ

inscription.head:
No. 27.
(A.R. No. 596 of 1909.)
ON THE NĀGA PILLAR IN THE TEMPLE OF VĪRABHADRA OUTSIDE THE VILLAGE OF GURAZALA,
PALNAD TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. [10]51.
inscription.p:
States that a Brāhman named Dāra son of Kommana who was the head of
Kummunūru village and who claimed to be an incarnation of the serpent king Śēsha,
put up a nāga-stambha in front of the temple of Brahmā, Vishṇu, and Śiva built by
himself and that Bētabhūpa of the Haihaya family, a dependent of Bhūlōkamalla-
dēva (Sōmēśvara III) made a gift of 4 kharis of land to the said temple.

l.te: mi stasya tasya tadā phalaṁ || madvaṁśajā[ḥ] para-
ma dha¨rmma mimaṁ samastaṁ tēṣāṁ mayā
yaṁ dha¨rmmasētu nnṛpā[ṇāṁ] kālē kālē pālanī-

inscription.head:
No. 28.
(A.R. No. 331 of 1905.)
ROUND A PEDESTAL IN FRONT OF A JAINA IMAGE IN THE RUINED JAINA TEMPLE
RECENTLY DISCOVERED AT DĀNAVULAPĀḌU, JAMMALAMAḌUGU TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p:
States that a certain Śānti put up the śilā-pīṭha during the reign of Nitya-
varsha.

l.te:
svasti śrī nityava¨rṣa prabhuviśadayaśa[ḥ*] śrīsamutpattima[dviḥ] prōddāma • • • • •
• • • • • tassa¨rvvadōdāttasatvaḥ | śāntiḥ prācīkarat śrīsnapanavidhiṁ śilāpīṭha

vasvaṁ putrapautrikaṁ ||

inscription.head:
No. 29.
(A.R. No. 391 of 1904.)
ON A SLAB LYING BEHIND THE TEMPLE OF GŌPĀLAKRISHNASVĀMI AT MALEPADU,
PRODDATUR TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p:
States that during the reign of Nityavarsha, the queen of Vallavarāja and
others made a gift of land (?) capable of yielding a certain quantity of rice to the
one hundred and eight of Viripaṟiti. Mentions the names of several persons—male and
female.

inscription.head:
No. 30.
(A.R. No. 835 of 1917.)
ON THE WEST SIDE OF THE GORGE NEAR THE RĀMĀNUJA-MAṬHA ON THE INDRAKĪLA HILL,
BEZWADA, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p: Contains only two letters Śrī and Sā.

edition.p: Note.—The characters are big and archaic.

inscription.head:
No. 31.
(A.R. No. 830 of 1917.)
ON A BOULDER TO THE SOUTH OF PHULTA, BERHAMPORE TALUK, GANJAM DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p: Contains no historical information.

edition.p:
Note.—This inscription is engraved in big archaic characters. (Vide Annual Report on
Epigraphy for 1917—18.)

inscription.head:
No. 32.
(A.R. No. 693 of 1926.)
ON A BROKEN STONE CALLED RACHCHABANDA LYING IN THE MIDDLE OF THE VILLAGE OF
RAYAVARAM, SARVASIDDHI TALUK, VIZAGAPATAM DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p: Incomplete and unintelligible. Contains the name of god Narēndrēśvara.

edition.l:
• • • na narēndrēsvara
The rest is entirely illegible.

inscription.head:
No. 33.
(A.R. No. 459 of 1915.)
ON THE KIRĀTĀRJUNA PILLAR ON THE TOP OF THE INDRAKĪLA HILL,
BEZWADA, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p:
States that the inscribed slab was planted at the spot where Arjuna made tapas
on the Indrakīla hill for obtaining the Pāśupata weapon from god Śiva, by Pechcha-
vāḍa Trikōṭibōyi, son of Kaliyamabōyi who claims to be a descendant of the Yaksha
who guided Arjuna to that spot. (Published in the Journal of the Āndhra Sāhitya
Parishat, Vol. 7, pp. 227—231 and in A.S.R. 1915—16, pp. 98 f.)
As read from the bottom upwards.

l.te: jayaḥ svasti śrūyatē khalu dvaitavanē pāṇḍavā-
yiṣyati tvāmityādiṣṭavaniti sa ca yakṣaḥ śāpā-
kōṭibōyu dha¨rmmōyaṁ sthēyā ntāva dbhavē di-

edition.p: Note.—The characters are of the Chāḷukyan type.

inscription.head:
No. 34.
(A.R. No. 306 of 1920.)
ON A SLAB SET UP NEAR THE OLD VILLAGE SITE AT MAILAVARAM,
ONGOLE TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p:
Not very intelligible. Refers to the eldest son of some one, who died in the
Niḍumrāni battle and says that a certain Raṭṭoḍi gave some land to Bhaṭṭaraḷu.
Contains the archaic forms vānṟu and raṭṭoḍi.

inscription.head:
No. 35.
(A.R. No. 162 of 1913.)
ON A SLAB SET UP NEAR A TANK IN ILAPAKURRU, GUDIVADA TALUK, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. Refers to a gift of land and cows made by Kadaladēva son
of Badayāri.

inscription.head:
No. 36.
(A.R. No. 523 of 1925.)
ON A MARBLE SLAB FROM GHANTASALA PRESERVED IN THE
GOVERNMENT MUSEUM, MADRAS.
Undated.
inscription.p: Unintelligible.

edition.p: The central portion of the inscription containing one letter in each line is rubbed off.

inscription.head:
No. 37.
(A.R. No. 796 of 1922.)
ON A STONE LYING IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF SŌMĒŚVARA AT PĀLAPARRU,
BAPATLA TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p:
Damaged. Refers to a gift of land made by Nūgaparāju and the three-hundred
mahājanas.

inscription.head:
No. 38.
(A.R. No. 834 of 1917.)
ON THE FRONT OF THE SMALLER SHRINE IN THE CONSERVED RĀMĀNUJA-MAṬHA ON THE
INDRAKĪLA HILL, BEZWADA, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p: In big Chāḷukyan characters mostly illegible. Incomplete and fragmentary.

inscription.head:
No. 39.
(A.R. No. 837 of 1917.)
ON THE FRONT WALL AND TWO PILLARS ON THE SAME HILL.
Undated.
inscription.p:
The inscription is in Chāḷukyan characters. Seems to contain proper names of
persons. It is in three parts which are unconnected with one another.
A (on the front wall.)

śrīdharayya
B (on ·a pillar.)

śrī ·utpattipiḍu [Reading is śrī ·utpattipiḍu[gu]• The last letter, though not clear, can be read ṇ. ḷ. ṟ.]
C (on ·another pillar.)

inscription.head:
No. 40.
(A.R. No. 265 of 1922.)
BELOW THE IMAGE OF VISHṆU CARVED IN THE FIRST CAVE TEMPLE AT BHAIRAVAKOṆḌA,
UDAYAGIRI TALUK, NELLORE DISTRICT.
THIS AND THE NEXT SIXTEEN INSCRIPTIONS ARE ENGRAVED IN THE SAME SET OF CAVE TEMPLES AT
BHAIRAVAKOṆḌA.
Undated.
inscription.p: Nos. 40—55. These are in the nature of labels engraved before certain images.

inscription.head:
No. 41.
(A.R. No. 266 of 1922.)
ON THE TOP OF A NICHE CONTAINING A LINGA.
Undated.

No. 42.
(A.R. No. 267 of 1922.)
ON THE TOP OF THE SECOND NICHE CONTAINING A LINGA TO THE PROPER RIGHT OF THE
FIRST CAVE AT BHAIRAVAKOṆḌA.
inscription.p:
Very much damaged and the few letters which are illegible make no sense.
Undated.

inscription.head:
No. 43.
(A.R. No. 268 of 1922.)
IN FRONT OF THE TWO NICHES BELOW THE SECOND CAVE TEMPLE, BHAIRAVAKOṆḌA.
Undated.

No. 44.
(A.R. No. 269 of 1922.)
IN THE SAME PLACE AS No. 43.
Undated.

No. 45.
(A.R. No. 270 of 1922.)
AT THE TOP OF THE ENTRANCE INTO THE THIRD CAVE TEMPLE AT BHAIRAVAKOṆḌA.
Undated.

No. 46.
(A.R. No. 271 of 1922.)
ON THE BEAM OF THE VERANDAH OF THE FIFTH CAVE TEMPLE AT BHAIRAVAKOṆḌA.
Undated.

p.te: śrī tribhuvanādityan śrī maṟuvara triṇētrō • • ma·inṟu •

inscription.head:
No. 47.
(A.R. No. 272 of 1922.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE VERANDAH OF THE FIFTH CAVE TEMPLE AT BHAIRAVAKOṆḌA.
Undated.

No. 48.
(A.R. No. 273 of 1922.)
ON THE CAPITAL OF THE PILLAR IN THE VERANDAH OF THE FIFTH CAVE TEMPLE
AT BHAIRAVAKOṆḌA.
Undated.

No. 49.
(A.R. No. 274 of 1922.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE VERANDAH OF THE FIFTH CAVE TEMPLE AT BHAIRAVAKOṆḌA.
Undated.

No. 50.
(A.R. No. 275 of 1922.)
ON THE SAME PILLAR AS No. 49.
Undated.

No. 51.
(A.R. No. 276 of 1922.)
ON THE ROCK TO THE PROPER LEFT OF THE SIXTH CAVE TEMPLE AT BHAIRAVAKOṆḌA.
Undated.
inscription.p: Illegible.

inscription.head:
No. 52.
(A.R. No. 277 of 1922.)
ON THE BEAM OF THE VERANDAH OF THE SEVENTH CAVE TEMPLE AT BHAIRAVAKOṆḌA.
Undated.

No. 53.
(A.R. No. 278 of 1922.)
ON A BEAM IN THE VERANDAH OF THE SEVENTH CAVE TEMPLE AT BHAIRAVAKOṆḌA.
Undated.

No. 54.
(A.R. No. 279 of 1922.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE VERANDAH OF THE SEVENTH CAVE TEMPLE AT BHAIRAVAKOṆḌA.
Undated.

No. 55.
(A.R. No. 280 of 1922.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE VERANDAH OF THE SIXTH CAVE TEMPLE AT BHAIRAVAKOṆḌA.
Undated.

No. 56.
(A.R. No. 281 of 1922.)
ON THE TOP OF A NICHE CONTAINING A LINGA IN FRONT OF THE BHAIRAVA TEMPLE AT
BHAIRAVAKOṆḌA.
Undated.
inscription.p:
Localizes a stānam established by Lōkama, daughter of a certain Pōṟēri and
granddaughter of Gōvinda-Pōṟēri.

inscription.head:
No. 57.
(A.R. No. 711 of 1920.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE TEMPLE OF ANANTABHŌGĒŚVARA, KOLLŪRU, TENALI TALUK,
GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1000 (?) (9th year of Kulōttuṅga-Chōḍa.)
inscription.p:
Damaged. States that a certain Peggaḍa Chimmapa gave five gadyas for the
upkeep of a perpetual lamp in the temple of Anantī[śvara].

inscription.head:
No. 58.
(A.R. No. 444 of 1915.)
ON A NANDI PILLAR SET UP IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF ŚIVA, DEGARAMUDI,
NARASARAOPETA TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1002See Appendix II..
inscription.p:
States that the chief Nanni-chōḍa made a gift of the village of Ḍēṅgarabūḍi and
also a tank to the temple of Tribhuvana-Mallikārjuna-Mahādēva. The temple wor-
shippers of Ḍēṅgarabūḍi were exempted from payment of duty on the sale of houses
(ammubaḍi suṅkhamu.)

inscription.head:
No. 59.
(A.R. No. 730 of 1920.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE MAṆḌAPA OF THE TEMPLE OF SŌMĒŚVARA, JUTTIGA, TANUKU TALUK,
WEST GODAVARI DISTRICT.
Ś. 1[0]11 (20th year of Vishṇuvardhana.)
inscription.p:
Records the gift of a perpetual lamp by Vīrachōḍadēva to the temple of
Sōmīśvara of Duttika.

inscription.head:
No. 60.
(A.R. No. 732 of 1920.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE MAṆḌAPA IN THE SAME TEMPLE.
Ś. 1[0]13.
inscription.p:
Records the gift of a perpetual lamp by one Muppaṟiya to the temple of
Sōmīśvara at Duttika during the reign of Vishṇuvardhana-Mahārāja.

inscription.head:
No. 61.
(A.R. No. 674 of 1920.)
AT THE BOTTOM OF A STONE LYING IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF SVAYAMBHUVĒŚVARA,
VALIVĒRU, TENALI TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT (BELOW A.R. No. 673 of 1920.)
Ś. 1026.
inscription.p:
Records the gift of land to the mason (Kāse) who built the temple of Svayam-
bhūdēva of Valivēru, the mason undertaking to do repairs to the temple from time
to time.

inscription.head:
No. 62.
(A.R. No. 265 of 1905.)
ON A NANDI PILLAR PLANTED IN FRONT OF THE DARK ROOM IN THE TEMPLE OF
TRIPURĀNTAKĒŚVARA, TRIPURĀNTAKAM, MARKAPURAM TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
Vyaya = Ś. 1028 (?) (37th year of Kulōttuṅga-Chōḍa.)
inscription.p:
Records the gift of the villages of Palkūru and Bhōjani-Cheṟuvu in
Mārjavāḍa by Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Karikāla-Chōḍa to the temple of Tripurāntakadēva
and Chaṇḍīśvaradēva.

l.te: vrālu || svadattāṁ paradattāṁ vā yō harēti vasu-

inscription.head:
No. 63.
(A.R. No. 277 of 1905.)
ON A SLAB SET UP CLOSE TO THE PRĀKĀRA ON THE RIGHT SIDE OF THE BLOCKED UP WESTERN
ENTRANCE TO THE TEMPLE OF TRIPURĀNTAKĒŚVARA AT THE SAME VILLAGE.
Ś. 1028.
inscription.p:
Records the gift of the village Bēṭalapāḍu in Kammanāṇḍu by Velanāṭi
Gōṅkarāju to the temple of Tripurāntakadēva.

l.te: sapa • ravallabha bhayalōbhadu¨rlabha raṇāṁgarākṣasa ni • • • ya •

inscription.head:
No. 64.
(A.R. No. 567 of 1925.)
ON A BIG WHITE MARBLE PILLAR SET UP NEAR THE DHVAJASTAMBHA IN THE TEMPLE OF
RĀMALINGĒŚVARA, VELPURU, SATTENEPALLI TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1030.
inscription.p:
Records the gift of a perpetual lamp by Kōṭa Gōkarāju son of Bhīma to the
temple of Rāmēśvara of Vēlupūru.

inscription.head:
No. 65.
(A.R. No. 361 of 1915.)
ON A BROKEN PILLAR LYING NEAR THE HOUSE OF A KOMATI IN MUKTĪŚVARAM,
NARASARAOPETA TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1031.
inscription.p: Incomplete. Mentions the town of Vraddhana in Karmarāshṭra.

inscription.head:
No. 66.
(A.R. No. 575 of 1909.)
ON THE NĀGA PILLAR IN THE COURTYARD OF THE TEMPLE OF CHENNAKĒŚAVASVĀMĪ,
MACHERLA, PALNĀD TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1033.
inscription.p:
States that the chief Chāgi Bēta made a gift of two hundred nivartanas of land
in the village of Sabbeyapalli in the Palli-dēśa three-hundred, to the temple of
Ādityēśvara established by Āditya, son of Śrīdhara, in the capital Mahādēvītaṭāka.
Also mentions gifts made by several other persons to the same temple and a gift of land
to the architect Tippōju whose genealogy is given. Bēta was the son of Vīrakāma
and grandson of Bēta.

inscription.head:
No. 67.
(A.R. No. 262 of 1905.)
ON A NANDI PILLAR SET UP IN FRONT OF THE DARK ROOM (CHIKATI MIDDI) IN THE
TEMPLE OF TRIPURĀNTAKĒŚVARA, TRIPURĀNTAKAM, MĀRKAPURAM
TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
Ś. 1033 (43rd year of Kulōttuṅga-Chōḍa.)
inscription.p:
Records the gift of a perpetual lamp by Bhīmaya, the peggaḍa of Pottapi
Kāma-Chōḍa to the temple of Tripurāntakadēva.

inscription.head:
No. 68.
(A.R. No. 263 of 1905.)
ON A NANDI PILLAR SET UP IN FRONT OF THE DARK ROOM (CHIKATI MIDDI)
IN THE SAME TEMPLE.
Ś. 1033 (42nd year of Kulōttuṅga-Chōḍa.)
inscription.p:
Refers to the gift of 50 inupa yeḍlu for the upkeep of a perpetual lamp in the
temple of Tripurāntaka-Mahādēva by a certain person who was the son of Guṇḍaya.

inscription.head:
No. 69.
(A.R. No. 698 of 1926.)
ON A STONE BUILT OVER THE DOORWAY OF THE PĀRVATĪ SHRINE IN THE TEMPLE OF
RĀMĒŚVARA AT ACHANTA, NARASAPURAM TALUK, WEST GODAVARI DISTRICT.
Ś. 1034 (?)
inscription.p:
Damaged. Refers to grants of topes to the temples of Mallīśvara and Sūrīśvara.
Mentions Kōna Mummaḍi-Bhīmarāja and Lōkarāja.

inscription.head:
No. 70.
(A.R. No. 578 of 1925.)
ON THE LEFT SIDE OF THE SMALL WHITE MARBLE PILLAR SET UP NEAR THE
DHVAJASTAMBHA IN THE TEMPLE OF RĀMALINGASVĀMI AT VELPŪRU,
SATTENEPALLI TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1034 (?) (Chitrabhānu.)
inscription.p:
Damaged. Refers to a gift of six cows to the temple of [Rāmē]śvara during the
reign of Vishṇuvardhana-Mahārāja.

edition.p: Note.—The cyclic year Chitrabhānu tallies with Ś. 1024 and not with Ś. 1034.

inscription.head:
No. 71.
(A.R. No. 579 of 1925.)
ON THE RIGHT SIDE OF THE SMALL WHITE MARBLE PILLAR SET UP NEAR THE
DHVAJASTAMBHA, IN THE SAME TEMPLE.
Ś. 1034 (?) (Chitrabhānu.)
inscription.p:
Refers to the gift of six cows to the temple of Rāmēśvara of Vēlpūru by the son of
Bhīma-Nāyaka, during the reign of Vishṇuvardhana-Mahārāja.

edition.p: Note:—Chitrabhānu tallies with Ś. 1024 and not with Ś. 1034.

inscription.head:
No. 72.
(A.R. No. 576 of 1909.)
ON THE THIRD FACE OF THE NĀGA PILLAR IN THE COURTYARD OF THE TEMPLE OF
CHENNAKĒŚAVA, MACHERLA, PALNĀD TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1035.
inscription.p:
States that a certain Āditya put up a nāgastambha during the reign of Vīra-
Kāma, son of Mēḍāmbikā.

l.te: sē nāgadini • sitē ca guyugē mēḍāṁbikānaṁda-

inscription.head:
No. 73.
(A.R. No. 316 of 1915.)
ON A NĀGA PILLAR SET UP IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF CHANDRAŚĒKHARA,
TANGEDUMALLI, NARASARAOPETA TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1037 (Jaya.)
inscription.p:
States that Māra, son of Īśvara-Nāyaka, minister of the Chōḍa chief
Kannāradēva got two tanks excavated and the image of Sōmīśvara established in the
village of Koppa[ram] in Kammanāṇḍu and that Kannāradēva's brother Chōḍa-
Balli made a gift of the village of Biḍepalli to the said temple.

inscription.head:
No. 74.
(A.R. No. 327 of 1915.)
ON THE NĀGA PILLAR SET UP IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF KŌDANDARĀMASVĀMI, KOPPARAM,
SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Ś. 1037. (Jaya.)
inscription.p:
States that Kannara-Chōḍadēva's minister Māra, son of Īśvara-Nāyaka, got
two tanks dug and built the temple of Sōmīśvara in Koppara in Kamma-vishaya, that
Kannaradēva granted the village of Kopparam and his brother Chōḍa-Balli the village
of Bīḍepalli to the said temple and that Mallikārjuna of the Kālāmukha sect who
was a native of Prātūr was made the trustee (?) of the said temple and endowment.
Gives a detailed account of the endowments in the land assigned to different persons
engaged in the management of the temple.

l.te: ślōka || dāpayitvā taṭākau dvau dēvāya ta-
patēḥ kha 3 vrīhi kha 1 mā¨rddalika bō-

inscription.head:
No. 75.
(A.R. No. 365 of 1915.)
ON A SLAB SET UP NEAR THE TEMPLE OF PARASAVĒDĪŚVARA, CHENNUPALLI AGRAHARAM,
SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Ś. 10 • .
inscription.p:
States that a certain Sūraparāju, made a gift of land in Kāṭēpalli to god
Kommīśvara. Mentions Kannaradēva, the Chōḍa chief.

edition.l:
vacōḍamahārājulu tama •
ṣome lines ·are lost here•

inscription.head:
No. 76.
(A.R. No. 315 of 1915.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN A STREET OF MINNIKALLU, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p:
States that the Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Kannaradēva granted three puṭṭis of land
to god Mallikārjuna of Mrindikallu.

inscription.head:
No. 77.
(A.R. No. 875 of 1922.)
ON THE NORTHERN FACE OF THE BLACK STONE PLANTED OPPOSITE TO THE TEMPLE OF
CHENNAKĒŚAVASVĀMĪ, KOMMŪRU, BAPATLA TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1040 (Hēvilambi.)
inscription.p: Very much damaged. Refers to a gift to a temple of Mahādēva.

inscription.head:
No. 78.
(A.R. No. 817 of 1922.)
ON THE WESTERN FACE OF THE BLACK STONE PLANTED OPPOSITE TO THE SAME TEMPLE.
Ś. 1040 (Hēvilambi.)
inscription.p:
States that Kommūri Gōkiraḍḍi made a gift of six uttamagaṇḍa-māḍas for the
upkeep of a perpetual lamp in the temple of Agastīśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 79.
(A.R. No. 117 of 1917.)
ON A STONE SET UP NEAR THE MADIGAPALLI OF NŪTAKKI, GUNTUR TALUK,
GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1040.
inscription.p:
States that Kalugoṭṭumbaḷḷi Māvi-Nāyaka, son of Chāmī-Nāyaka, one of
the five hundred mahājanas of Malvāna founded the temple of Kēśavadēva at
Nūtakki and gave 25 inupa yeḍlu for a perpetual lamp in the said temple. Describes
the Malvāna five hundred as a sect of Brahmins originally coming from Māshapuri on
the west of the Gōdāvarī and connects them in some unintelligible way with the killing
of Rāvaṇa by Rāma.

inscription.head:
No. 80.
(A.R. No. 815 of 1922.)
ON THE WESTERN FACE OF THE BLACK STONE PLANTED OPPOSITE TO THE TEMPLE OF
CHENNAKĒŚAVASVĀMĪ AT KOMMŪRU, BAPATLA TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1041. (Vilambi.)
inscription.p:
Records the gift of six uttamagaṇḍa-māḍas by Nemmalūri Sūraya for the
upkeep of a perpetual lamp in the temple of Agastīśvara of Kommūru. The endow-
ment was to be administered by the three hundred Sānis of Kommūru.

inscription.head:
No. 81.
(A.R. No. 814 of 1922.)
ON A BLACK STONE PLANTED IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF CHENNAKĒŚAVASVĀMĪ AT
KOMMŪRU, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Ś. 1041. (Vilambi.)
inscription.p:
Records the grant of six uttamagaṇḍa-māḍas by a certain Appana for the
upkeep of a perpetual lamp in the temple of Agastīśvara of Kommūru.

inscription.head:
No. 82.
(A.R. No. 395 of 1915.)
ON A MUTILATED SLAB LYING IN A FIELD IN NĀDIṆḌLA, NARASARAOPETA TALUK
SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1046.
inscription.p:
States that Mokkōla Gōmanaseṭṭi, son of Gosaniseṭṭi, consecrated the image of
Viśvēśvara and built a temple for it at [Nā]diṇḍla. This inscription is in Telugu verse.

inscription.head:
No. 83.
(A.R. No. 728 of 1920.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE MAṆḌAPA OF THE TEMPLE OF SŌMĒŚVARA, JUTTIGA, TANUKU TALUK,
WEST GODAVARI DISTRICT.
Ś. 1047.
inscription.p:
States that Kolaniya Kettu-Gaṇḍa made a gift for the upkeep of a perpetual
lamp in the temple of Vāsukiravi-Sōmīśvara of Duttika. The gift is of manniya-tīṟu
which seems to be a local tax.

inscription.head:
No. 84.
(A.R. No. 683 of 1920.)
ON A SLAB BUILT INTO THE SHRINE UNDER THE DHVAJASTAMBHA IN THE TEMPLE OF
AGASTYĒŚVARA, IMANI, TENALI TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1047 (Viśvāvasu.)
inscription.p:
States that Ghaṇṭasāla Kāmaka made a gift of 50 sheep for the upkeep of a
perpetual lamp in the temple of Toṇḍīśvaradēva of Ivani.

l.te: mūḷlu 2 bhūmi na 4 yidha¨rmmuvu ·evvarainaṁ dappirēni bā-

inscription.head:
No. 85.
(A.R. No. 6 of 1908.)
ON A SLAB LYING IN A MOUND IN THE SAHEBU BAZAAR, SATTENEPALLI, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1049.
inscription.p:
States that a certain Ma[ṭṭe]-Nāyaka made a gift of land to the temple of
Trikōṭīśvara of Vellaṇṭūra and also to the Sthānapati of the temple.

l.te:
ttaṁ paradattaṁ jyā viharē[ta] vasuntharā ṣaṣṭi
¨rvva¨rṣa-

inscription.head:
No. 86.
(A.R. No. 50 of 1909.)
ON A PILLAR IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF RĀMALINGĒŚVARA, VĒMŪRU, TENALI TALUK,
SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1[0]5[0].
inscription.p:
Refers to a gift made by the Nāyakas of Vēmūru to god Rāmīśvara, for the
upkeep of a perpetual lamp.

inscription.head:
No. 87.
(A.R. No. 51 of 1909.)
ON A PILLAR IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF RĀMALINGĒŚVARA, VĒMŪRU,
SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Ś. 1[050] (?)
inscription.p:
Refers to a gift (of land) by Kulōttuṅga-chōḍa-Goṅka's general Eṟapōta-
Nāyaka for the upkeep of a perpetual lamp in the temple of Rāmēśvara-Mahādēva.

inscription.head:
No. 88.
(A.R. No. 820 of 1922.)
ON A STONE PLANTED OPPOSITE TO THE TEMPLE OF CHENNAKĒŚAVA AT KOMMŪRU,
BAPATLA TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1051.
inscription.p:
States that Rēkamadēvī the paramour of Permāḍidēva alias Kendūri Chōḍa-
mahārāja granted six uttamagaṇḍa-māḍas to the temple of Agastīśvara at
Kommūru.

inscription.head:
No. 89.
(A.R. No. 441 of 1915.)
ON THE NĀGA SLAB SET UP NEAR THE SHRINE OF VINĀYAKA, TUBADU,
NARASARAOPETA TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1054.
inscription.p:
States that Kāpe[ya]-Nāyaka, a general of Maṇḍarāju made a gift of 100
puṭṭis of land to god Bhīmēśvara of Dūṁbaṟuta and gives details of the distribution
of the land among the several servants of the temple. The latter portion says that
Kāpe[ya]-Nāyaka consecrated the image of Mahēśvara at Dūṁbaṟuta, built a temple for
it and endowed it with 100 puṭṭis of land. Probably both the gifts are identical.

inscription.head:
No. 90.
(A.R. No. 631 of 1920.)
ON A STONE CALLED PŌTURĀJU SET UP IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF PUTTĀLAMMA,
AMRITALŪRU, TENALI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1054.
inscription.p:
States that the Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Gōṅkaya Bōya (?) lord of the 480 villages
and servant of Rājēndra-Chōḍa made a gift of 50 sheep for the upkeep of a perpetual
lamp in the temple of Amritīśvara at Amutunūru.

inscription.head:
No. 91.
(A.R. No. 645 of 1920.)
ON THE BLACK GRANITE PILLAR SET UP IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF GŌKARNĒŚVARA,
DĀVULŪRU, TENALI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1054.
inscription.p:
States that a certain Komma-Nāyaka who was appointed Raḍḍi of Dāvulūru
in the Velanānḍu country by Velanāṇṭi Gōkarāja consecrated the image of Gōkēśvara
in the said village, built a temple over it and endowed it with land and 55 sheep for a
perpetual lamp.

inscription.head:
No. 92.
(A.R. No. 5 of 1908.)
ON A SLAB LYING IN A MOUND IN THE SAHEBU BAZAAR, SATTENEPALLI, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1055.
inscription.p: Incomplete. Refers to a gift made by a certain Vāsena.

inscription.head:
No. 93.
(A.R. No. 321 of 1919.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE MAṆḌAPA AT THE NORTHERN ENTRANCE INTO THE TEMPLE OF
MALLĒŚVARA AT BEZWADA, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Ś. 1055.
inscription.p: Incomplete and illegible. Refers to one Pēriseṭṭi.

inscription.head:
No. 94.
(A.R. No. 9 of 1908.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAHEBU BAZAAR, SATTENEPALLI, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1055.
inscription.p:
States that Vāsi-Nāyaka son of Prōli-Nāyaka made a gift of 55 sheep for a
perpetual lamp in the temple of Trikōṭīśvaradēva, the sheep to be taken care of by
Gāde-Bōya, son of Pēriseṭṭi.

inscription.head:
No. 95.
(A.R. No. 698 of 1920.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE TEMPLE OF ANANTABHŌGĒŚVARA, KOLLŪRU, TENALI TALUK,
SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1055.
inscription.p: Damaged. Refers to a gift of sheep for a perpetual lamp.

inscription.head:
No. 96.
(A.R. No. 716 of 1920.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE TEMPLE OF KĒŚAVASVĀMĪ, DUGGIRĀLA, GUNTUR TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1056.
inscription.p:
States that Bhīma, chief of Kaṇḍravāḍi, who was the son of Paṇḍāmbikā,
made a gift of the village of Duggaṟēlapūṇḍi to a number of Brahmins and invokes the
blessings of Śēsha and other seven great Nāgas on the chief.

inscription.head:
No. 97.
(A.R. No. 350 of 1915.)
ON THE NANDI FILLAR IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF RĀMALINGASVĀMĪ, SĀTULŪRU,
NARASARAOPETA TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1057.
inscription.p:
States that Kulōttuṅga-chōḍa-Goṅka made a gift of land to god Rāmēśvara of
Chāṁchalūru in Koṇḍapaḍumaṭi country and gives details of the distribution of the
land among the several servants of the temple and refers also to other gifts made by
other persons to the same temple.

l.te: tta vā yō harēti vasuṁdhara ṣa-

inscription.head:
No. 98.
(A.R. No. 119 of 1917.)
ON A NANDI NĀGA PILLAR SET UP IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF MALLIKĀRJUNA, PĀMULAPĀḌU,
GUNTUR TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p:
Refers to a gift of land made by Velanāti Rājēndra-Chōḍa and others to the
temple at Pāmulapāḍu in the Koṇḍapaḍumaṭi country.

l.te:
pāmulapāṭiṁ damavrittipai dēvaraku bolliraḍḍi

edition.p:
Note:—The impression of Face B is missing. Consequently the reading given in the
departmental transcript is adopted.

inscription.head:
No. 99.
(A.R. No. 140 of 1913.)
ON A SLAB PRESERVED IN THE COLLECTOR'S OFFICE AT GUNTUR, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1059.
inscription.p: Incomplete and damaged. Mentions a certain Rēkāsāni.

inscription.head:
No. 100.
(A.R. No. 699 of 1920.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE TEMPLE OF ANANTĪŚVARA, KOLLŪRU, TENALI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1059(?)
inscription.p:
Damaged. Seems to refer to a gift of five Rājanārāyaṇa-nishkas by a certain
Dāmāmbā for a perpetual lamp in the temple of Anantīśvara of Kollūru.

inscription.head:
No. 101.
(A.R. No. 697 of 1920.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE TEMPLE OF ANANTABHŌGĒŚVARA,
KOLLŪRU, TENALI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1059 (4th year of Kulōttuṅga-chōḍa II.)
inscription.p:
Damaged and incomplete. Refers to a gift of five Rājanārāyaṇa-gadyāṇas
by a certain lady for a perpetual lamp in the temple of Anantīśvara of Kollūru.

inscription.head:
No. 102.
(A.R. No. 116 of 1917.)
ON A STONE LYING NEAR THE ENTRANCE TO THE TEMPLE OF ŚAKTĪŚVARA, NŪTAKKI,
GUNTUR TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1059 (4th year of Kulōttuṅga-chōḍa II).
inscription.p:
Incomplete and damaged. Refers to a gift of land by Kaṇḍravāṭi Bhīmarāju
to god Śaktīśvara of Nūteki (Nūtakki).

l.te:
gu srāhi dakṣiṇāyana nimittamuna śrīmanma
hā-

inscription.head:
No. 103.
(A.R. No. 404 of 1915.)
ON A PILLAR STANDING NEAR THE TEMPLE OF RĀMASVĀMĪ, KĀVŪRU,
NARASARAOPETA TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 10[59].
inscription.p:
Damaged. States that a certain Raḍḍi built a temple to god Sōmēśvara of
Gōniyapūṇḍi and endowed it with a grant of land and also granted 55 sheep for
a perpetual lamp.

edition.l:
lu 55 vīniṁ jēkoni kro • • kāma
(The rest is illegible.)

inscription.head:
No. 104.
(A.R. No. 10 of 1908.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAHEBU BAZAAR, SATTENEPALLI, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1060.
inscription.p:
Refers to the gift of 55 sheep by Koṇḍapa-Nāyaka son of Maṭṭe-Nāyaka for a
perpetual lamp in the temple of Trikōṭīśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 105.
(A.R. No. 11 of 1908.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAHEBU BAZAAR AT THE SAME VILLAGE.
Ś. 1060.
inscription.p:
Refers to the gift of 55 sheep by Pedda Koṇḍapa-Nāyaka, son of Nāvi-Nāyaka
for a perpetual lamp. The name of the temple is not mentioned.

inscription.head:
No. 106.
(A.R. No. 332 of 1915.)
ON A NĀGA PILLAR SET UP IN A GROVE IN LINGAMGUNTA, NARASARAOPETA TALUK,
SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 106.
inscription.p: Damaged. Refers to a gift by a certain Kāśa-Nāyaka. Mentions Gōkarāja.

inscription.head:
No. 107.
(A.R. No. 393 of 1915.)
ON A PILLAR SET UP IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF VEMULAMMA, NĀDIṆḌLA,
SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Ś. 1061.
inscription.p:
Refers to a gift of 55 sheep by Manma-Maṇḍayarāja, a subordinate of
Kulōttuṅga-chōḍa for a perpetual lamp in the temple of Karṇamōṭī of Nādiṇḍla. It
also refers to certain lands assigned to dancing girls and some other servants of the
temple. Contains also a not quite intelligible reference to a duty on marriages
performed in Nādiṇḍla to be paid to the temple. Maṇḍa was the son of Buddha, son
of Maṇḍa, chief of Giripaschima country.

inscription.head:
No. 108.
(A.R. No. 705 of 1920.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE TEMPLE OF ANANTABHŌGĒŚVARA, KOLLŪRU,
TENALI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 10[62] [8th (?) year of Kulōttuṅga-Chōḍa II.]
inscription.p:
States that Indulasāni Peḍudama (?) daughter of Kosanāsāni made a
gift of 5 Rājanārāyaṇa-gadyāṇas for a perpetual lamp in the temple of Anantīśvara
of Kollūru.

inscription.head:
No. 109.
(A.R. No. 646 of 1920.)
ON THE NORTHERN FACE OF THE BLACK GRANITE PILLAR SET UP IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF
GŌKARṆĒŚVARA, ḌĀVULŪRU, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Ś. 1063.
inscription.p:
States that Rājēndra-Chōḍa's servant Kommaya-Nāyaka the headman of
Kuṟumgallu in Kaṇḍravāṭi consecrated the image of Gōkēśvara in Ḍāvulūru and
built a temple and maṇḍapa for it.

inscription.head:
No. 110.
(A.R. No. 743 of 1920.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE MAṆḌAPA OF THE TEMPLE OF SŌMĒŚVARA, JUTTIGA,
TANUKU TALUK, WEST GODAVARI DISTRICT.
Ś. 1063 (1[5th] year of Vishṇuvardhana ?)
inscription.p:
States that Prōli Pāva, a dancing girl attached to the temple of Vāsukiravi
Sōmēśvara of Duttika, made a gift of 50 inupa-yeḍlu for a perpetual lamp in the said
temple. The sthānapati, the three hundred sānis and the nibandhakartas are enjoined
to see that the gift is properly administered.

l.te: ka māneṁḍu sēsi neyi vōsi yāca-

inscription.head:
No. 111.
(A.R. No. 676 of 1920.)
ON A STONE IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF SVAYAMBHUVĒŚVARA, VALIVĒRU,
TENALI TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1064.
inscription.p:
States that one Eṟiya Bōya established a perpetual lamp in the temple of
Bhaḍāri (?) of Valivēru.

inscription.head:
No. 112.
(A.R. No. 647 of 1920.)
ON THE WESTERN FACE OF THE BLACK GRANITE PILLAR SET UP IN FRONT OF THE
TEMPLE OF GŌKARṆĒŚVARA, ḌĀVULŪRU, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Ś. 1065.
inscription.p:
States that Aytama, wife of Aytama Nāyaka who was the Raḍḍi of
Kuṟuṅgallu in Īvani-Kaṇḍravāḍi and Ḍāvulūru in Velanāṇḍu and a trusted
servant of Velanāṭi Rājēndra-Chōḍa, gave 55 sheep for a perpetual lamp in the
temple of Gōkēśvara at Ḍāvulūru.

l.te: 55 vīni jēkoni tenāli nakka nāve bōyini

inscription.head:
No. 113.
(A.R. No. 38 of 1912.)
ON A PILLAR LYING NEAR THE GODOWN IN THE MUNICIPAL MUSEUM AT RAJAHMUNDRY,
EAST GODAVARI DISTRICT.
Ś. 1065.
inscription.p:
Damaged and illegible. Refers to a gift made by a certain chief named Rāya
to the temple of Vīrabhadrēśvara of Paṭṭisam.

l.te: ṇitē rāyābhidhānō nṛpaḥ puṇyānāṁ nidhi ru-

inscription.head:
No. 114.
(A.R. No. 848 of 1917.)
ON A WHITE MARBLE PILLAR IN THE TEMPLE OF JALADHĪŚVARA AT GHAṆṬAŚĀLA,
DIVI TALUK, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Ś. 1066.
inscription.p:
Somewhat damaged. Refers to the gift of two perpetual lamps to god
Jaladhīśvara of Ghaṇṭaśāla by a certain Kommana who is stated to belong to
Dhanada-kula.

l.te:
kṣi yanaṁgā dagu[pama] • • • nupu

inscription.head:
No. 115.
(A.R. No. 848 of 1917.)
ON A WHITE MARBLE PILLAR SET UP IN THE SAME TEMPLE.
Ś. 1066. (13th year of Kulōttuṅga-Chōḍa II.)
inscription.p:
States that Kōṭa Kommiseṭṭi a Vaiśya of the Nābela-gōtra entrusted to
the three hundred sānis of Ghaṇṭaśāla alias Chōḍa-Pāṇḍyapuram the sum of
10 Rājarāja-māḍas for maintaining two perpetual lamps in the temple of Jaladhīśvara
in the said town.

inscription.head:
No. 116.
(A.R. No. 41 of 1912.)
ON A PILLAR LYING NEAR THE GODOWN IN THE MUNICIPAL MUSEUM, RAJAHMUNDRY,
EAST GODAVARI DISTRICT.
Ś. 1067 (21st (?) year of Vishṇuvardhana.)
inscription.p:
States that Sūkkala Prōli-seṭṭi of Penugoṇḍa made a gift of 25 cows for the
upkeep of a perpetual lamp in the temple of Vīrabhadrēśvara of Paṭṭisam.

inscription.head:
No. 117.
(A.R. No. 580 of 1925.)
ON THE RIGHT SIDE OF THE SMALL WHITE MARBLE PILLAR SET UP NEAR THE
DHVAJASTAMBHA IN THE TEMPLE OF RĀMALINGASVĀMI, VĒLPŪRU,
SATTENEPALLI TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1068.
inscription.p:
Damaged. Refers to a gift of birudu-māḍas for the upkeep of a perpetual
lamp in the temple of Rāmīśvara of Vēlupūru.

inscription.head:
No. 118.
(A.R. No. 269 of 1924.)
ON A PILLAR SET UP IN THE TEMPLE OF ĀNJANĒYASVĀMI, KONAKANCHI,
NANDIGĀMA TALUK, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Ś. 1068.
inscription.p: Records a gift of land by Rājēndra-Chōḍa to the Śiva temple at Koṇḍakañchi.

inscription.head:
No. 119.
(A.R. No. 144 of 1913.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE COURTYARD OF THE TEMPLE OF UTTARĒŚVARA,
PEDA KODAMAGUṆḌLA, PALNAD TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1069. (Prabhava.)
inscription.p:
States that the taxes pērusuṅkam, vaḍḍarāvaḷamu, kolehuvīsālu etc., due from the
village of Kodamagūḍa to Isāna-Peggaḍa, the mudiseli of Kulōttuṅga-Gōmkarāja,
were assigned for the maintenance of worship in the temples and for the support of
sanyāsins and students.

inscription.head:
No. 120.
(A.R. No. 709 of 1926.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE ROOM IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF SĪTĀRĀMASVĀMĪ,
PINNALI, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Ś. 1069. (Prabhava.)
inscription.p:
Very much damaged. Refers to one Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Kāmarāju as ruling
the land.

inscription.head:
No. 121.
(A.R. No. 327 of 1919.)
ON THE EAST FACE OF THE NANDI PILLAR SET UP IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF
MŪLASTHĀNĒŚVARA AT NĀDEṆḌLA, NARASARAOPETA TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1069.
inscription.p:
Records that king Malla of the Koṇḍapaḍumaṭi-seventy-three villages, made
a gift of a perpetual lamp to the temple of Mūlasthānēśvara of Nādiṇḍla. Malla's
father was Maṇḍa, grandfather Gaṇḍa and great grandfather Maṇḍa.

inscription.head:
No. 122.
(A.R. No. 458 of 1915.)
ON A MUTILATED STONE LYING NEAR THE TEMPLE OF SŌMĒŚVARA,
GAṆAPAVARAM, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Year missing.
inscription.p:
Damaged and not quite intelligible. Seems to state that the villagers granted
a piece of land to the temple builder Kāmōju for the merit of the king Kulōttuṅga
Rājēndra-[Chōḍa].

inscription.head:
No. 123.
(A.R. No. 40 of 1912.)
ON A PILLAR LYING NEAR THE GODOWN IN THE MUNICIPAL MUSEUM AT
RAJAHMUNDRY, EAST GODAVARI DISTRICT.
Ś. 1071.
inscription.p:
Mentions that Sōmana, (son of) Datyana-Peggaḍa lord of Oragāṇḍi presented
a perpetual lamp to god Vīrabhadrēśvara of Paṭṭisam. Incidentally, it mentions
other charities given by the same person in some other sacred places.

inscription.head:
No. 124.
(A.R. No. 328 of 1919.)
ON THE EAST FACE OF THE NANDI PILLAR SET UP IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF
MŪLASTHĀNĒŚVARA AT NĀDEṆḌLA, NARASARAOPETA TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1072.
inscription.p:
Refers to a gift of sheep by the wife of the Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Tammu
Maṇḍerāju for a perpetual lamp in the temple of Mūlasthāna-Mahādēva of Nādeṇḍla.

inscription.head:
No. 125.
(A.R. Nos. 568 to 571 of 1925.)
ON THE FOUR FACES OF A BIG WHITE MARBLE PILLAR SET UP NEAR THE
DHVAJASTAMBHA IN THE TEMPLE OF RĀMALINGĒŚVARA, VĒLPŪRU,
SATTENEPALLI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
(I have treated the whole as constituting a single inscription
but it may be otherwise.)
Ś. 1073.

Damaged and unintelligible. Refers to a gift of 55 (sheep) to the temple of
Rāmēśvara at Vēlpūru and mentions Rājēndra-Chōḍa.

inscription.head:
No. 126.
(A.R. No. 310 of 1920.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE TEMPLE OF SVARṆĒŚVARA AT GAṆAPAVARAM,
BHIMAVARAM TALUK, WEST GODAVARI DISTRICT.
Ś. 107[3].
inscription.p:
States that the Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Kolani Kāṭama-Nāyaka made a gift
of 50 inupayeḍlu for a perpetual lamp in the temple of Svarṇēśvara in
Padminīpuram.

inscription.head:
No. 127.
(A.R. No. 332 of 1919.)
ON THE NORTH-WEST PILLAR IN THE KALYĀṆA-MAṆḌAPA IN THE
TEMPLE OF TRIKŌṬĪŚVARA, KOTAPPA KOṆḌA,
NARASARAOPETA TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1[0]75.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. Refers to the gift of inupayeḍlu by Datyana-Peggaḍa-Sōmana
the Mudiseli of Boddana Nāraya, to the temple of Trikōṭīśvara of Kāvūru.

inscription.head:
No. 128.
(A.R. No. 330 of 1919.)
ON THE RIGHT DOOR JAMB OF THE MUKHA-MAṆḌAPA IN THE TEMPLE OF MŪLASTHĀNĒŚVARA,
NĀDEṆḌLA, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Ś. 1075.
inscription.p:
Refers to the gift of twelve birudu-gadyas by Sūrama, daughter of Dāmama,
for a perpetual lamp in the temple of Mūlasthāna-Mahādēva of Nādiṇḍla.

inscription.head:
No. 129.
(A.R. No. 390 of 1915.)
ON A PILLAR OF THE KALYĀṆA-MAṆḌAPA IN THE SAME TEMPLE.
Ś. 107[5].
inscription.p:
Damaged. Refers to a gift by a certain Kāme-[seṭṭi] for a perpetual lamp in
the temple of Mūlasthāna-Mahādēva of Nādiṇḍla.

inscription.head:
No. 130.
(A.R. No. 639 of 1920.)
ON A NANDI STONE SET UP IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF RĀMALINGASVĀMI,
CHILUMŪRU, TENALI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. [1]075.
inscription.p:
States that Muppa Nāganāyaka made a gift of land as emoluments to the
Sānis and the Mānis as also for a perpetual lamp in the temple of Rāmīśvara of
Chunumūru and for feeding ten Brahmins in a choultry in the said village. Mentions
Goṅkarāja of Velanāṇḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 131.
(A.R. No. 455 of 1915.)
ON A STONE LYING IN A FIELD IN GURAVAYAPALEM, HAMLET OF ELLAMANDA,
NARASARAOPETA TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1075 (Śrīmukha).
inscription.p:
States that a certain Buddana made a gift of the tank he had constructed at
Peṭlūru to the temple of Trikōṭīśvara of Kāvūru.

l.te: na svadattāṁ paradattāṁ vā yō ha-

inscription.head:
No. 132.
(A.R. No. 640 of 1920.)
ON THE NANDI STONE SET UP BEFORE THE TEMPLE OF RĀMALINGĒŚVARA,
CHILUMŪRU, TENALI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1075.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. Refers to grant of land by Muppa-Nāyaka the Raḍḍi of Chunu-
mūru for feeding five Brahmins daily in a choultry.

edition.l:
beṭṭina bīga 50
(after this some lines ·are lost.)

inscription.head:
No. 133.
(A.R. No. 323 of 1919.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE MAṆḌAPA AT THE NORTHERN ENTRANCE INTO THE TEMPLE OF
MALLĪŚVARA, BEZWADA, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Ś. 1075.
inscription.p:
States that Kaṇṭama, mother-in-law of Nāgārjuna, the treasurer of Boddana
Nārāyaṇa the chief of Bejavāḍa made a gift of 55 inupayeḍlu for a perpetual lamp
in the temple of Mallēśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 134.
(A.R. No. 552 of 1909.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE COURTYARD OF THE TEMPLE OF ANKĀLAMMA,
KAREMPŪDI, PALNAD TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1076. (Bhāva)
inscription.p:
Refers to the gift of 50 goats by Īśāna-Peggaḍa the minister of Kulōttuṅga-
Chōḍa Goṅkarāja, for a perpetual lamp in the temple of Sūrēśvara of Kāremapūṇḍi
built by him.

inscription.head:
No. 135.
(A.R. No. 347 of 1915.)
ON THE GARUḌA PILLAR IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF VALLABHARĀYA,
PAMIDIPĀDU-AGRAHARAM, NARASARAOPETA TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1077.
inscription.p:
Refers to a gift of land by Gaṇḍirāju, son of Appana, the astrologer, to the
temple of Chennakēśava of Pahiṁḍipāḍu for the merit of Rājēndra-Chōḍa and states
that the suṅka and the marriage tax in Pahiṁḍipāḍu were also assigned to the
said temple.

inscription.head:
No. 136.
(A.R. No. 566 of 1925.)
ON A STONE BUILT INTO THE PARAPET WALL OF THE TEMPLE OF RĀMALINGĒŚVARA,
VĒLPŪRU, SATTENEPALLI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1077.
inscription.p:
Refers to the gift of 55 sheep by Kasaviya Veduraya, dependant of Velanāṭi
Kulōttuṅga-Chōḍa Gōṅka for a perpetual lamp in the temple of Rāmīśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 137.
(A.R. No. 384 of 1915.)
ON THE RIGHT DOOR POST OF THE ENTRANCE TO THE TEMPLE OF SŌMĒŚVARA,
DRŌṆADŪLA, NARASARAOPETA TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1077.
inscription.p:
States that a certain Peggaḍa (name lost) who was the mudiseli of Kulōttuṅga-
Chōḍa Gōkarāja made a gift of five puṭṭis of land to the temple of Sōmēśvara
of Drōṇādi.

inscription.head:
No. 138.
(A.R. No. 664 of 1920.)
ON A PILLAR LYING IN THE COMPOUND OF THE TEMPLE OF MALLĒŚVARA, PERAVALI,
TENALI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1078.
inscription.p:
States that one Kommama built the temple of Mādhavadēva in the village of
Peruvali and that her son made a gift of land for naivēdyam to the said deity.

inscription.head:
No. 139.
(A.R. No. 329 of 1919.)
ON THE LEFT DOOR JAMB OF THE NORTHERN ENTRANCE INTO THE MUKHA-MAṆḌAPA
OF THE TEMPLE OF MŪLASTHĀNĒŚVARA AT NĀDEṆḌLA, NARASARAOPETA TALUK,
SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1078.
inscription.p:
States that a certain Nārāyaṇa, son of Daṇḍanāyaka Vēma-Nāyaka gave
55 sheep for a perpetual lamp in the temple of Mūlasthāna-Mahādēva of Nādiṇḍla. The
first 18 lines are in Telugu verse but the versification is faulty.

l.te: ni manumaṁ ḍamātya vēmani kumāra vā-

inscription.head:
No. 140.
(A.R. No. 854 of 1917.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE TEMPLE OF RĀMALINGĒŚVARA AT MASULIPATAM, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Ś. 1079.
inscription.p:
Illegible. Refers to (the temple of) Viśvēśvara in Ghaṇṭaśāla alias Chōḍa-
pāṇḍyapuram.

inscription.head:
No. 141.
(A.R. No. 318 of 1915.)
ON THE TOP TIER OF THE BASEMENT OF THE SOUTH WALL OF THE TEMPLE OF ŚAMBHUSVĀMI,
EḌAVALLI, NARASARAOPETA TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 107[9].
inscription.p:
States that Chaṇḍabhīma Pōtiseṭṭi, the merchant of Kulōttuṅga-Chōḍa
Gōṅkarāju built the temple of Svayambhū-Brahmēśvara of Eḍavalli and gave 55 sheep
for a perpetual lamp in the said temple.

l.te:
śrīmanmahāmaṁḍalēśvara kulōttuṁggacōḍagoṁkkarājula vyavahāri caṁḍabhīma pōtiseṭṭi śakava¨rṣaṁbulu

inscription.head:
No. 142.
(A.R. No. 115 of 1917.)
ON A PILLAR OF THE MADHYARANGAM IN THE TEMPLE OF ŚAKTĪŚVARA, NŪTAKKI,
GUNTUR TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1080.
inscription.p:
Damaged. Refers to the gift of inupayeḍlu for a perpetual lamp in the temple
of Śaktīśvara in Nūṭṭeṅki.

inscription.head:
No. 143.
(A.R. No. 114 of 1917.)
ON A PILLAR OF THE MADHYARANGAM IN THE TEMPLE OF ŚAKTĪŚVARASVĀMI,
NŪTAKKI, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Ś. 108[0].
inscription.p:
Incomplete. Refers to a perpetual lamp established by a certain Aṅgi Raṭṭaḍi
in the temple of Śaktīśvara of Nūdeṅki during the reign of Rājarāja.

inscription.head:
No. 144.
(A.R. No. 83 of 1917.)
ON A WHITE MARBLE NĀGA STONE IN THE TEMPLE OF AGASTYĒŚVARA,
GUNTUR, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1080.
inscription.p:
Damaged and incomplete. States that a certain Paṇḍyarāju built the temple
and maṇḍapa for god Agastyēśvara of Guṇṭūru in Oṁgēru-mārga and that the said
Paṇḍyarāju and some others endowed the temple with lands.

inscription.head:
No. 145.
(A.R. No. 847 of 1917.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE TEMPLE OF JALADHĪŚVARASVĀMI, GHAṆṬAŚĀLA, DIVI TALUK,
KRISHNA DISTRICT (JUST BELOW No. 846.)
Ś. 1081 (1[4]th year of Rājarāja)
inscription.p:
States that a certain Sōmarasa gave 5 Rājarāja-māḍas for a perpetual lamp
in the temple of Jaladhīśvara in Ghaṇṭaśāla.

inscription.head:
No. 146.
(A.R. No. 736 of 1920.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE MAṆḌAPA OF THE TEMPLE OF SŌMĒŚVARA AT JUTTIGA,
TANUKU TALUK, WEST GODAVARI DISTRICT.
Ś. 1082.
inscription.p:
States that a Brahmin named Pōta presented a perpetual lamp to the temple
of Sōmēśvara at Ḍuttika on the bank of the river Gūḍhastanī in the Pāvanavāra-
dēśa.

inscription.head:
No. 147.
(A.R. No. 734 of 1920.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE MAṆḌAPA IN FRONT OF THE SHRINE OF SŌMĒŚVARA IN
JUTTIGA, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Ś. 1082.
inscription.p: Same as above. The river is called here Gōdhastānī.

inscription.head:
No. 148.
(A.R. No. 439 of 1915.)
ON A NĀGA STONE SET UP IN FRONT OF A KOMATI'S HOUSE AT CHIRUMAMIḶḶA,
NARASARAOPETA TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1083.
inscription.p:
Partly illegible. Refers to a gift of lands by Kulōttuṅga-Chōḍa Gōkarāju
who worshipped the feet of Rājēndra-Chōḍa, to a certain temple and temple servants.

inscription.head:
No. 149.
(A.R. No. 440 of 1915.)
ON A NĀGA STONE SET UP IN FRONT OF A KOMATI'S HOUSE AT CHIRUMAMIḶḶA,
SAME TALUK, AND DISTRICT.
Ś. 1[083].
inscription.p:
Damaged. Refers to gift made by one Prōli[bōyi] to God Anantīśvara of
Chirumāmiḍi.

inscription.head:
No. 150.
(A.R. No. 795 of 1922.)
ON A STONE PLANTED IN A FIELD NEAR THE VILLAGE OF GARNĒPĀḌU,
BAPATLA TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1083.
inscription.p:
States that Paḍālu Gaṇḍaya, servant of the Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Parichchēdi
Tammu Bhīmarāju made a gift of land to the temple of Koppīśvara established by
him on the bund of a tank to the east of Vēṟūru.

inscription.head:
No. 151.
(A.R. No. 443 of 1915.)
ON A NĀGA PILLAR SET UP IN A STREET IN TIMMAPURAM,
NARASARAOPETA TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1083.
inscription.p:
States that Saraṇaya, brother of Kāmidēvi, a wife (?) of Kulōttuṅga-Chōḍa
Gōṅkarāju established the image of Gōkēśvara in Jēvaramu and made a gift of land
and an oil-mill to that temple and also of land to Brahmins.

inscription.head:
No. 152.
(A.R. No. 316 of 1924.)
ON A PILLAR LYING NEAR A DILAPIDATED MOSQUE AMONG THE RUINS OF THE
FORT AT GUḌIMEṬṬA, NANDIGAMA TALUK, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Ś. 1083.
inscription.p:
States that Tyāgi (Chāgi) Dōra made gifts of land and a tank to the temple of
Viśvēśvara of Guḍimeṭṭa in Nāthavāṭī.

inscription.head:
No. 153.
(A.R. No. 713 of 1920.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE TEMPLE OF ANANTABHŌGĒŚVARA, KOLLŪRU,
TENALI TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1 • • .
inscription.p:
Incomplete. Refers to the gift of 5 Tyāgi-gadyāṇas by a certain Kēte to a
certain temple.

inscription.head:
No. 154.
(A.R. No. 689 of 1920.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE TEMPLE OF ĀNJANĒYA AT ZAMPANI,
SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p:
States that Kulōttuṅga-Chōḍa Gōṅka's general Eṟapōta-Nāyaka made a gift
of 3 marutus of land to the north of the tank, to God Rāmēśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 155.
(A.R. No. 707 of 1926.)
ON A SLAB SET UP NEAR THE DHVAJASTAMBHA OF THE TEMPLE OF RĀMALINGASVĀMI,
PINNALI, PALNAD TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1085 (Svabhānu).
inscription.p:
States that, during the reign of the Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Anuṅgum Kāmarāju,
50 goats were entrusted to the custody of the four sons of Amarēśvara-Paṇḍita for a
perpetual lamp in the temple of Rāmēśvara at Piṅgali. The chief is described as
belonging to the Haihaya family.

l.te: vura vasamuna niccina mēṁka 50 lekkana mēṁ-
kālē kālē pālaniyyō bhavadbhi ssa¨rvvā nē-

inscription.head:
No. 156.
(A.R. No. 553 of 1909.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE TEMPLE OF ANKĀLAMMA, KAREMPŪDI, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Ś. [1]086 (Tāraṇa.)
inscription.p: Damaged and unintelligible. Refers to some Haihaya chief.

l.te: • • • hārājulu svasti śrī śakava¨rṣaṁbulu [1]086 nēṁṭṭi tāra
bū • lu naḍapaṁgalavāṁḍu || svadattaṁ paradatta[ṁ vā yō] harēti

inscription.head:
No. 157.
(A.R. No. 316 of 1920.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE TEMPLE OF SVARṆĒŚVARA, GANAPAVARAM, BHIMAVARAM TALUK,
WEST GODAVARI DISTRICT.
Ś. 1087.
inscription.p:
Damaged. Refers to the grant of land to the temple of Svarṇēśvara of Padminī-
pura, during the reign of Rājarāja.

l.te: ṣibhiḥ | śatru rēva hi śatru syāddha-

inscription.head:
No. 158.
(A.R. No. 132 of 1917.)
ON A STONE LYING BEHIND THE TEMPLE OF GŌKARṆĒŚVARA, PENUMULI, GUNTUR TALUK,
GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1087.
inscription.p:
States that Rājēndra-Chōḍarāja made a gift of land to the temple of goddess
Chāmuṇḍī of Kolaṅkalūru during the reign of Rājarāja, “emperor of the three
worlds.”

inscription.head:
No. 159.
(A.R. No. 293 of 1915.)
ON A NĀGA PILLAR SET UP IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF RĀMASVĀMI ON THE HILL AT
IKKURRU, NARASARAOPETA TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 10[8]8.
inscription.p:
States that Mallināyaka of Śiripura made a gift of land and cows to the temple
of Mallikārjuna established by him on the top of a hill at Ikkūru for the merit of the
Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Kulōttuṅga-Rājēndra-Chōḍarāja and also refers to other gifts
made by others to the same temple.

inscription.head:
No. 160.
(A.R. No. 708 of 1920.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE TEMPLE OF ANANTABHŌGĒŚVARA, KOLLŪRU,
TENALI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1088. (21st year of Rājarāja.)
inscription.p:
Refers to the grant of money (?) made by one Bhīmi-Nā[yaka] for a perpetual
lamp in the temple of Narēndrīśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 161.
(A.R. No. 147 of 1917.)
ON A STONE SET UP IN THE TEMPLE OF DANTĒŚVARA AT PRATTIPĀDU,
GUNTUR TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1089.
inscription.p:
States that a Vaiśya named Nārāyaṇa gave a perpetual lamp and 55 sheep for
its upkeep to the temple of Gaṇḍēśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 162.
(A.R. No. 78 of 1917.)
ON THE PROPER RIGHT COLUMN OF THE ENTRANCE INTO THE TEMPLE OF
SĪTĀRĀMASVĀMI, ERRABALEM, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Ś. 1089.
inscription.p:
States that a certain Jillya-Bōya made a gift of the village of Krapagaṟāpuri
free of all taxes to the Mahājanas of Uṇḍavelli for the merit of Rājēndra Kulōttuṅga-
Chōḍarāja.

inscription.head:
No. 163.
(A.R. No. 349 of 1915.)
ON A STONE FORMING THE THRESHOLD OF THE INNER ENTRANCE INTO THE TEMPLE OF
RĀMALINGASVĀMI, SĀTULŪRU, NARASARAOPETA TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 108-.
inscription.p:
States that Prōlama (wife of ?) Pritvina-Bōya gave 55 sheep to the temple of
Rāmēśvaradēva of Chāṁchalūru.

inscription.head:
No. 164.
(A.R. No. 108 of 1917.)
ON A NĀGA SLAB SET UP NEAR THE VILLAGE OF MAṆḌAPĀḌU,
GUNTUR TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
About Ś. 1090.
inscription.p:
States that one Mandaḍi Nāmi-Nāyaka, his brothers and other relations made
gifts of land to the temple of Mallikārjuna at Mandāvuram in the Koṇḍapaḍumaṭi
country and also to the pūjāris for the merit of Rājēndra-Chōḍa-Mahārāja, son of
Kulōttuṅga-Chōḍa Goṅka.

inscription.head:
No. 165.
(A.R. No. 105 of 1917.)
ON A NĀGA SLAB SET UP NEAR THE SAME VILLAGE.
Ś. 10 • .
inscription.p:
States that Prōli-Nāyaka and Malli-Nāyaka sons of Nāmi-Nāyaka made
gifts of land to god Mallikārjuna at Mandāvuramu for the merit of the Mahā-
maṇḍalēśvara Kulōttuṅga Rājēndra-Chōḍa.

inscription.head:
No. 166.
(A.R. No. 106 of 1917.)
ON A NĀGA SLAB SET UP NEAR THE SAME VILLAGE.
Ś. 10[90].
inscription.p:
States that Pedda Prōli-Nāyaka's son Nārāyaṇa gave 55 sheep for a
perpetual lamp in the temple of Mallikārjuna at Mandāvuramu.

inscription.head:
No. 167.
(A.R. No. 107 of 1917.)
ON A NĀGA SLAB SET UP NEAR THE SAME VILLAGE.
Ś. 1090.
inscription.p:
States that Māranāmātya chief of Origāṇḍipura gave 55 sheep for a perpetual
lamp in the temple of Mallikārjuna at Mandāvuramu for the merit of the
Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Kulōttuṅga Rājēndra-Chōḍa.

l.te: kuṁ beṭṭina goṟiyalu 55 vīniṁ jēkoni kāmanabōyuni koḍku kaṁpi-

inscription.head:
No. 168.
(A.R. No. 678 of 1920.)
ON A BROKEN STONE LYING IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF RĀMALINGĒŚVARA,
VĒMŪRU, TENALI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 10[9]0.
inscription.p: Incomplete and unintelligible.

inscription.head:
No. 169.
(A.R. No. 12 of 1908.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAHEBU BAZAAR, SATTENEPALLI, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1091.
inscription.p:
States that a certain Sāni Prōlima gave 55 sheep for a perpetual lamp in the
temple of Trikōṭīśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 170.
(A.R. No. 856 of 1917.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE TEMPLE OF RĀMALINGĒŚVARA AT
MASULIPATAM, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Ś. 10[9]1.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. Mentions God Viśvēśvara-Mahādēva of Ghaṇṭaśāla alias Chḍōa-
Pāṇḍyapuramu.

inscription.head:
No. 171.
(A.R. No. 658 of 1920.)
ON A PILLAR LYING IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF CHŌḌĒŚVARA AT
MOPAṞṞU, TENALI TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1092.
inscription.p:
States that Paḍavālu Gōṅka and his wife Gōkāmbikā made a gift of perpetual
lamps to the temple of Chōḍēśvara-Mahādēva of Mrōmpaṟṟu. Gives a list of the
dancing girls attached to the temple and the extent of land granted to each by way of
emoluments. Gōṅka who is described as the general of the ruler of the Velanāṇḍu
six-thousand is said to have vanquished thirty thousand Karnāṭa troops (horse).

inscription.head:
No. 172.
(A.R. No. 657 of 1920.)
ON A PILLAR LYING IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF CHŌḌĒŚVARA, MOPAṞṞU,
SAME TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 109[2].
inscription.p:
States that Gōka the general of the Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Kulōttuṅga Rājēndra-
Chōḍa granted 110 sheep for two perpetual lamps and his wife Gōkasāni granted
55 sheep for one perpetual lamp in the temple of Chōḍēśvara of Mrōmpaṟṟu. States
also that land was given for naivēdyam to the deity.

inscription.head:
No. 173.
(A.R. No. 545 of 1925.)
ON A STONE SET UP NEAR THE TEMPLE OF RĀMASVĀMI, ŚRĪRĀMAPURAM,
SARVASIDDHI TALUK, VIZAGAPATAM DISTRICT.
Ś. 1092.
inscription.p:
States that Kulōttuṅga Rājēndra-Chōḍa's minister and Commander-in-chief of
forces, Prōliya-Peggaḍa granted land and a garden in Chaṭṭipuramu to God
Chōḍīśvaramahādēva.

l.te: vāsana peggaḍaku dha¨rmmuvugāṁ jōḍīśvaramahādēvaraku nitya ni-
ṇamunaku śrāvaṇaṁ gaṭṭaṁ beṭṭi[dili]peṭṭina bhūmi saṭṭivuramuna rāje-
ḍi dakṣiṇabhāgamunaṁ buvvuṁdōṁṭṭakuṁ buṭṭeṁḍu nēlayuṁ gābera-
m beṭṭitimi ·īdharmmuvunaku nevvarēni vighnamu dalaṁccinavāru ga-

svadattaṁ paradattaṁ vā yō harēti vasuṁddharā ṣa-

inscription.head:
No. 174.
(A.R. No. 695 of 1920.)
ON A PILLAR BUILT INTO THE WALL OF THE TEMPLE OF RĀJĒŚVARA, AT
PEDA KOṆḌŪRU, TENALI TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1092.
inscription.p:
States that Mallidēva, son of Drōṇa, who was the son of Kanda who was lord of
Koṇḍrūru and the minister of Vikrama-Chōḍadēva gave land and cows for two
perpetual lamps in the temple of Rājarājēśvara. The donor is described as belonging to
the family of Śivadvijas of Koṇḍrūru.

inscription.head:
No. 175.
(A.R. No. 394 of 1915.)
ON A STONE LYING IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF NANDIKUNTA VINĀYAKA,
NĀDEṆḌLA, NARASARAOPETA TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1093.
inscription.p:
Damaged. Refers to a gift made by Koṇḍapuḍumaṭi Buddharāju and
Maṇḍērāju.

edition.l:
• • nāyakukoḍku
(one ·or two lines seem to be lost here.)

inscription.head:
No. 176.
(A.R. No. 655 of 1920.)
ON THE NANDI PILLAR LYING IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF RĀMACHANDRĒŚVARA
AT MŌDUKŪRU, TENALI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1093.
inscription.p:
States that Sūrapa-Nāyaka, a subordinate of Rājēndra-Chōḍadēva built the
temple of Chōḍīśvara at Mrōntukūru and granted lands to the temple and the pūjāri.

inscription.head:
No. 177.
(A.R. No. 49 of 1909.)
ON A PILLAR IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF RĀMALINGĒŚVARA, SIRIPURAM,
SATTENEPALLI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1093.
inscription.p:
The first part states that Rājēndra-Chōḍa, son of Velanāṭi Kulōttuṅga Chōḍa-
Gōṅka, who was lord of the sixteen thousand Āndhra country and who seized the
town Kolanu and killed its chief Bhīma, made gifts of lands to the temple of Rāmēśvara
of Siripuram one of the twelve pūṇḍis in Koṇḍapaḍumaṭi district and also to dancing
girls attached to it. The second part says that a certain Bolli-Nāyaka gave 55 sheep
for a perpetual lamp in the said temple. The third part refers to gifts made by other
persons to the same temple.

l.te: ṁḍai kālu prōlu soraṁ goṭṭina viḍmaṟa gā-
na kaṁdda tā nelakoni rauravāṁbudhi mu-
ṁtta vātakamu siddhamu dīniki hiṁsa sē-

yoppu siripuraṁ-

niki dha¨rmmā-

edition.p:
Note.—The rest of the inscription is greatly damaged. This inscription really consists of three
inscriptions—lines 1 to 86 constituting one inscription, lines 87 to 129, another inscription and the rest
yet another inscription. There is no information available as to whether all these lines are inscribed
on the same side or on different sides of the pillar.

inscription.head:
No. 178.
(A.R. No. 551 of 1925.)
ON THE FIRST PILLAR IN THE INNER MAṆḌAPA OF THE TEMPLE OF RĀMALINGASVĀMI,
VĒLPŪRU, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Ś. 1094.
inscription.p:
States that a certain Prōlama made a gift of 55 sheep for a perpetual lamp in the
temple of Rāmēśvara of Vēlpunūru.

inscription.head:
No. 179.
(A.R. No. 48 of 1909.)
ON A PILLAR IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF RĀMALINGĒŚVARA, SIRIPURAM,
SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Ś. 1094.
inscription.p:
States that three brothers Sūrapa, Nārāyaṇa and Nāgadēva, sons of Kaṭya-
Peggaḍa granted 55 sheep for a perpetual lamp in the temple of Rāmēśvara of
Siripuram. Sūrapa is said to have vanquished the enemies of his master Rājēndra-
Chōḍa.

inscription.head:
No. 180.
(A.R. No. 120 of 1917.)
ON THE NANDI-NĀGA PILLAR SET UP IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF MALLIKĀRJUNA
AT PĀMULAPĀḌU, GUNTUR TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1094.
inscription.p:
States that a certain Koṇḍana, grandson of Koṇḍaparaḍḍi of Pāmulapāḍu
gave 55 sheep for a perpetual lamp in the temple of Śiva.

l.te: gā¨rttikamu punnama nujvala guru dinamunam go-

edition.l: ṇḍaṁ ḍalaghuṁḍu dīpaṁbu nilpe harunaku vēḍkanu [||] [2*]

inscription.head:
No. 181.
(A.R. No. 121 of 1917.)
ON THE SAME PILLAR.
Ś. 1094.
inscription.p:
States that a certain Bolliraḍḍi gave 55 sheep for a perpetual lamp in the Śiva
temple at Pāmulapāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 182.
(A.R. No. 122 of 1917.)
ON THE SAME PILLAR.
Ś. 1094.
inscription.p:
States that a certain Kommaṇa gave 55 sheep for a perpetual lamp in the Śiva
temple at Pāmulapāḍu.

edition.l:
na yurutaradīpaṁbu śivuna koppaṁga nilpenu || [2*]
komma-

inscription.head:
No. 183.
(A.R. No. 123 of 1917.)
ON THE SAME PILLAR.
Ś. 1094.
inscription.p:
States that a certain Vēmi-Nāyaka gave 55 sheep for a perpetual lamp in the
temple of Mūlasthāna-Mallikārjuna at Pāmulapāḍu.

l.te: lapāṭaṁ beṭṭe satpūjita mai mahi nnegaḍa-
raka yā niśākaradivākara mai canaṁgā śakābda-

inscription.head:
No. 184.
(A.R. No. 704 of 1920.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE TEMPLE OF ANANTABHŌGĒŚVARA, KOLLŪRU, TENALI
TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. [1094] ([26th] year of Rājarāja.)
inscription.p:
Damaged. Refers to a grant of 55 sheep for a perpetual lamp in the temple of
Narēndrēśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 185.
(A.R. No. 124 of 1917.)
ON A NANDI PILLAR SET UP IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF MALLIKĀRJUNASVĀMI,
PĀMULAPĀḌU, GUNTUR TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1094.
inscription.p:
States that a certain Kommana gave 55 sheep for a perpetual lamp in the temple
of Śiva at Pāmulapāḍu.

l.te:
yuktuṁ ḍai pāmulapāṭa noppaṁgaṁ gapa¨rddiki
sajca-

ttaṁ pa-

inscription.head:
No. 186.
(A.R. No. 264 of 1905.)
ON A NANDI PILLAR PLANTED IN FRONT OF THE DARK ROOM IN THE TEMPLE OF
TRIPURĀNTAKĒŚVARA, TRIPURĀNTAKAM, MĀRKĀPURAM TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
Ś. 1095.
inscription.p:
Damaged. Refers to the grant of 25 cows for a perpetual lamp in the temple of
Tripurāntaka-Mahādēva by a friend of Velanāṭi Rājēndra-Chōḍa.

inscription.head:
No. 187.
(A.R. No. 703 of 1920.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE TEMPLE OF ANANTABHŌGĒŚVARA, KOLLŪRU, TENALI
TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 10[95] (2[6th] year of Rājarāja.)
inscription.p:
Damaged. Refers to the gift of goats made for a perpetual lamp in the temple of
Narēndrēśvara of Kollūru. The grantor's name is lost.

inscription.head:
No. 188.
(A.R. No. 183 of 1917.)
ON A SLAB SET UP NEAR THE TEMPLE OF AMARĒŚVARA AT JONNALAGEḌḌA,
GUNTUR TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 10[95].
inscription.p:
Somewhat unintelligible. Refers to the grant of land to the temple of
Vallabhēśvara of Jonnalageḍḍa in the reign of the Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Kulōttuṅga-
Rājēndra-Chōḍa.

inscription.head:
No. 189.
(A.R. No. 696 of 1920.)
ON A PILLAR BUILT INTO THE WALL OF THE TEMPLE OF RĀJĒŚVARASVĀMI AT
PEDA KOṆḌŪRU, TENALI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1095.
inscription.p:
States that Kandabhūpa's son Rāma, who was lord of Koṇḍrūru and was deeply
devoted to Śaiva Dharma made a gift of land to the temple of Rājarājēśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 190.
(A.R. No. 282 of 1924.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE TEMPLE OF KĀSI VIŚVĒŚVARA AT ANIGAṆḌLAPĀḌU,
NANDIGĀMA TALUK, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Ś. 10[9]6. (Jaya.)
inscription.p:
States that a certain Kēti-Nāyaka made a gift of land to the temple of
Paṇḍīśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 191.
(A.R. No. 308 of 1920.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE TEMPLE OF SVARṆĒŚVARA, GANAPAVARAM, BHIMAVARAM
TALUK, WEST GODAVARI DISTRICT.
Ś. [10]96.
inscription.p:
States that Vēṅgi-Mallidēvarāja's son, Gōkarāja, who belonged to the family of
Yuddhamalla and was lord of the six-thousand country made a gift of 30 cows to the
temple of Svarṇēśvara of Padminīpura.

inscription.head:
No. 192.
(A.R. No. 158 of 1913.)
ON A PILLAR SET UP IN THE VILLAGE OF YENIKEPĀḌU, MASULIPATAM
TALUK, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Ś. 109[6].
inscription.p:
States that the Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Kulōttuṅga Rājēndra-Chōḍayarāja granted
land for a perpetual lamp and also to the Sānis and Mānis in the temple of Kēśavadēva
of Bejavāḍa.

inscription.head:
No. 193.
(A.R. No. 852 of 1917.)
ON THE BROKEN WHITE MARBLE PILLAR IN THE TEMPLE OF VIŚVĒŚVARA, GHAṆṬAŚĀLA,
DIVI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1098.
inscription.p:
Damaged. States that a certain Nūṅkanārya made a gift of a perpetual lamp
to the temple of Bhōgēśvara during the reign of Rājēndra-Chōḍa, the ruler of the
Vēṅgi country.

inscription.head:
No. 194.
(A.R. No. 42 of 1912.)
ON A GRANITE STONE IN THE HOUSE OF THE PUJARI OF THE TEMPLE OF MĀRKAṆḌĒYA-
SVĀMI, RAJAHMUNDRY, EAST GODAVARI DISTRICT.
Ś. 1098.
inscription.p: Damaged. Refers to a gift of land to the temple of Vīrabhadrēśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 195.
(A.R. No. 574 of 1925.)
ON A NANDI PILLAR SET UP NEAR THE DHVAJASTAMBHA IN THE TEMPLE OF
RĀMALINGĒŚVARA AT VĒLPŪRU, SATTENEPALLI TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 109[8].
inscription.p:
States that a certain Prōlā-Sāni made a gift of 55 sheep for a perpetual lamp in
the temple of Rāmīśvara of Vēlpūru.

inscription.head:
No. 196.
(A.R. No. 274 of 1905.)
ON A PILLAR IN FRONT OF A ŚIVA SHRINE NORTH OF THE TEMPLE OF TRIPURĀNTAKA,
TRIPURĀNTAKAM, MĀRKĀPURAM TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
Ś. 1099.
inscription.p:
States that Vyāsarāsi-Paṇḍita the sthānapati of Tripurāntakam made a
gift of land in Miriyamupalli. It is not clear for what purpose the gift was made.

inscription.head:
No. 197.
(A.R. No. 36 of 1912.)
ON A PILLAR LYING NEAR THE GODOWN IN THE MUNICIPAL MUSEUM, AT
RAJAHMUNDRY, EAST GODAVARI DISTRICT.
Ś. 1102.
inscription.p:
States that Vēṅgi Gōka, lord of the six-thousand country, made a gift of
30 inupa-yeḍlu for a perpetual lamp in the temple of Vīrabha[drēśvara].

inscription.head:
No. 198.
(A.R. No. 553 of 1925.)
ON THE FOUR FACES OF A PILLAR IN THE OUTER MAṆḌAPA OF THE TEMPLE OF
RĀMALINGĒŚVARA AT VĒLPŪRU, SATTENEPALLI TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1104.
inscription.p:
States that Kōṭa Kētarāja made a gift of the villages of Bhīmarupāḍu and
Chōmanūru to God Rāmēśvara of Vēlpunūru. Gives a complete genealogy of the
Kōṭa chiefs.

inscription.head:
No. 199.
(A.R. No. 562 of 1925.)
ON THE THIRD PILLAR IN THE OUTER MAṆḌAPA OF THE TEMPLE OF RĀMALINGĒŚVARA
AT THE SAME VILLAGE.
Ś. 1104.
inscription.p:
States that Kōṭa Kētarāju granted several villages in Koṇḍa-Nāthavāḍi
Koṇḍapaḍumaṭi and Doḍḍi-Kaṇḍravāḍi countries to Brahmins for the merit of his
mother Sabbāmbikā, his father Bhīmarāju, his elder brother Chōḍarāju and of
himself. Gives a complete genealogy of the Kōṭa chiefs.

inscription.head:
No. 200.
(A.R. No. 563 of 1925.)
ON THE FOURTH PILLAR IN THE OUTER MAṆḌAPA OF THE TEMPLE OF RĀMALINGĒŚVARASVĀMI
AT THE SAME VILLAGE.
Ś. 1104.
inscription.p:
States that Kōṭa Kētarāja made a gift of perpetual lamps to the temple of
Rāmēśvara of Vēlpunūru and gave for their upkeep 55 sheep each, for his own merit
and for the merit of his father, mother and elder brother. Contains the donor's
genealogy.

l.te: nōti nētu miva sinthupatiṁ sthalatvaṁ dvīpāṁtta-
na sāmya nnirastō nakharāyudhō sau || ni-
ḍu mārevasamunaṁ beṭṭina goṟiyalu 55 vīniṁ jē-

inscription.head:
No. 201.
(A.R. No. 588 of 1925.)
ON A MUTILATED STONE LYING NEAR THE SAME TEMPLE.
Undated.
inscription.p: Contains a praśasti of Kōṭa Kētarāja's minister Prōla.

inscription.head:
No. 202.
(A.R. No. 322 of 1919.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE MAṆḌAPA AT THE NORTHERN ENTRANCE INTO THE TEMPLE OF
MALLĒŚVARA AT BEZWADA, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Ś. 110[8]
inscription.p:
States that Malli-Dēsaṭi son of Prōli-Dēsaṭi who is described as lord of
Kallaṟti-vishaya and the Tumbūru-nāṇḍu and as a worshipper of the feet of
Jōti-Siddhavaṭṭēsvara, gave 25 cows for a perpetual lamp in the temple of
Mallīśvara of Bejavāḍa.

inscription.head:
No. 203.
(A.R. No. 739 of 1920.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE TEMPLE OF SŌMĒŚVARA, JUTTIGA, TANUKU TALUK, WEST
GODAVARY DISTRICT.
Ś. 1109.
inscription.p: Illegible and incomplete. Refers to Vishṇuvardhana-Mahārāja.

inscription.head:
No. 204.
(A.R. No. 838 of 1917.)
ON A PILLAR LYING IN A STREET NEAR THE OFFICE OF THE PUBLIC WORKS
DEPARTMENT IN BEZWADA, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Ś. 1115.
inscription.p:
Damaged. Refers to a gift of cattle by a certain Raḍḍi for a perpetual lamp in
the temple of Mallēśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 205.
(A.R. No. 37 of 1912.)
ON A PILLAR LYING NEAR THE GODOWN IN THE MUNICIPAL MUSEUM AT RAJAHMUNDRY,
EAST GODAVARY DISTRICT.
Ś. 1117.
inscription.p:
States that a certain Kelli-Seṭṭi granted 25 cows for a perpetual lamp in the
temple of Vīrabhadrēśvara-Mahādēva.

inscription.head:
No. 206.
(A.R. No. 309 of 1920.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE TEMPLE OF SVARṆĒŚVARA AT GAṆAPAVARAM, BHIMAVARAM
TALUK, WEST GODAVARY DISTRICT.
Ś. 1117.
inscription.p:
States that Sōmayarāja, the chief of Kolani-maṇḍala gave 50 inupa-yeḍlu for a
perpetual lamp in the temple of Svarṇēśvara in Padminīpuram.

l.te: yatō dha¨rmma[ḥ] tatō jayaḥ ye[cca] • mācā¨ryyaliki •

inscription.head:
No. 207.
(A.R. No. 845 of 1917.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE MUKHA-MAṆḌAPA OF THE TEMPLE OF RĀJĒŚVARA, WEST VIPPAṞṞU
TANUKU TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1120.
inscription.p:
States that a certain Kāpa-Sāni got the maṇḍapa of the temple of Narēndrēśvara
of Vippaṟṟu, plastered.

l.te:
ṭi jēṣṭa • śu [1] va | ma • ttapa[kā
prasā]-

inscription.head:
No. 208.
(A.R. No. 561 of 1925.)
ON THE FOURTH FACE OF THE SECOND PILLAR IN THE OUTER MAṆḌAPA OF THE
TEMPLE OF RĀMALINGĒŚVARA, VĒLPŪRU, SATTENEPALLI TALUK,
GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1122.
inscription.p:
Refers to three perpetual lamps presented to the temple of Rāmēśvara of
Vēlpūru by Vendula Māche, Īdakoṇḍe and Mēḍebōyinī Bramme for the merit of
the Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Kōṭa Kētarāja.

inscription.head:
No. 209.
(A.R. No. 327 of 1924.)
ON A BROKEN NĀGA PILLAR LYING IN THE ŚIVA TEMPLE AT ZUZZŪRU, NANDIGĀMA
TALUK, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
(The beginning and the concluding portions are lost.)
Ś. 1124.
Incomplete. Refers to a gift made by the Teliki one-thousand.

inscription.head:
No. 210.
(A.R. No. 302 of 1924.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE KALYĀṆA-MAṆḌAPA OF THE TEMPLE OF MUKTĪŚVARA,
MUKTYĀLA, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Ś. 1129.
inscription.p:
States that Kēśava, chief of Īvani-Kaṇḍravāṭi gave 25 cows for a perpetual lamp
in the temple of Muktīśa.

inscription.head:
No. 211.
(A.R. No. 97 of 1909.)
ON A SLAB FIXED INTO THE WALL OF THE ENTRANCE TO THE JAGGA RAO OBSERVATORY
AT VIZAGAPATAM, VIZAGAPATAM DISTRICT.
Ś. 112-.
inscription.p:
Rather unintelligible. Refers to the grant of land to a merchant in Viśākha-
paṭṭaṇa alias Kulōṭṭuṅga-Chōḍapaṭṭaṇa. Mentions the Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Kulōṭṭuṅga
Prithiviśvara Mahārāja.

l.te: nāru • svadattāṁ paradattāṁ vā yō ha-

inscription.head:
No. 212.
(A.R. No. 141 of 1913.)
ON A SLAB PRESERVED IN THE COLLECTOR'S OFFICE, GUNTUR,
GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p:
States that a certain Seṭṭi who was a dependent of the Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara
Kulōṭṭuṅga-Chōḍa Gōṅka made a gift of 55 sheep for a perpetual lamp in the temple
of Buddhadēva.

inscription.head:
No. 213.
(A.R. No. 849 of 1917.)
ON A WHITE MARBLE PILLAR IN THE TEMPLE OF JALADHĪŚVARASVĀMI AT
GHAṆṬASĀLA, DIVI TALUK, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p:
Damaged and incomplete. Refers to a gift made by some one during the reign
of Kullōttuṅga-Chōḍa [Gōṅka].

inscription.head:
No. 214.
(A.R. No. 675 of 1923.)
ON A STONE LYING BEFORE THE TEMPLE OF SVAYAMBHUVĒŚVARA, VALIVĒRU,
TENALI TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p:
States that Gaṅga Sōmaya, son of Valivēri Guṇḍadēvayya made a gift of
50 sheep for a perpetual lamp in the temple of Vishṇu at (Valivēru.)

inscription.head:
No. 215.
(A.R. No. 184 of 1917.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE TEMPLE OF AMARĒŚVARA, ZONNALAGEDDA,
GUNTUR TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p:
States that Ammana, son of Tikkana and grandson of Bhīma, lord of
Amṛitapura in the Velanāṇḍu country, and a soldier serving under the Chōḍa
King presented jayaghaṇṭa, dhūpārati and dīpārati to the temple of Vallabhēśvara.

l.te:
svadattaṁ paradattaṁ vō yō hārēti vasuṁ
dharā ṣaṣṭi ¨rvva-

inscription.head:
No. 216.
(A.R. No. 709 of 1920.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE TEMPLE OF ANANTABHŌGĒŚVARA AT KOLLŪRU,
TENALI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Date illegible.
inscription.p:
States that Bōyi Jilvaya, a servant of Kāpa-Nāyaka gave 5 Rājanārāyaṇagadyas
to Narēndrēśvara temple at Kollūru.

inscription.head:
No. 217.
(A.R. No. 709 of 1917.)
ON A STONE LYING IN A FIELD IN SELAPĀDU, GUNTUR TALUK,
SAME DISTRICT.
Date missing.
inscription.p: Damaged and unintelligible. Mentions Vishṇuvardhana.

l.te:
paranāsi vēvuru • po • • nukaviḷa
lanucaṁpina • • nu

inscription.head:
No. 218.
(A.R. No. 264 of 1924.)
ON A BROKEN PILLAR LYING NEAR THE IMAGE OF ĀNJANĒYA AT KANCHALA,
NANDIGĀMA TALUK, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Year not given.
inscription.p:
States that Malli-Nāyaka of Vippaṟla-kula made a gift to the temple of
Rāmēśvara of Kaṁchcherla.

inscription.head:
No. 219.
(A.R. No. 39 of 1912.)
ON A PILLAR LYING NEAR THE GODOWN IN THE MUNICIPAL MUSEUM AT
RAJAHMUNDRY, EAST GODAVARI DISTRICT.
The Year is lost.
inscription.p: Damaged and unintelligible. Mentions a certain Rāyaparāja.

l.te: • • • yadā bhūmi stasya tasya tathā phalaṁ

inscription.head:
No. 220.
(A.R. No. 358 of 1915.)
ON A STONE BUILT INTO THE SOUTH WALL OF THE TEMPLE OF RĀMALINGĒŚVARA,
VAIDANA, NARASARAOPETA TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Year not given.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. Refers to a ruler (name lost) of the Six-thousand country, south
of the river Krishṇavēṇṇā.

edition.l:
birudu
(The rest of the stone is built in.)

inscription.head:
No. 221.
(A.R. No. 392 of 1915.)
ON A STONE BUILT INTO THE PLATFORM OF THE TAMBRALA MAṆḌAPA IN THE
TEMPLE OF MŪLASTHĀNĒŚVARA, NĀDEṆḌLA, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Date missing.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. Refers to the grant (of land) made by one Kommiseṭṭi to the
temple of Sōmēśvara and of 55 sheep by a certain Koṇḍiseṭṭi for a perpetual lamp in
the temple of Chōḍēśvara of Nādiṇḍla. Also refers to a convention entered into by
the Teliki one thousand of Bejavāḍa to the effect that the jewels of a deceased wife go to
the husband and not to the other relations of the deceased woman.
Beginning Lost.

inscription.head:
No. 222.
(A.R. No. 133 of 1917.)
ON A STONE BUILT INTO THE WEST WALL OF THE TEMPLE OF VĒṆUGŌPĀLASVĀMI,
POTTŪRU, GUNTUR TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Date missing.
inscription.p:
Damaged and incomplete. Refers to a grant made by a person of the
Parichchhēda family.

inscription.head:
No. 223.
(A.R. No. 382 of 1915.)
ON A STONE SET UP OUTSIDE THE COMPOUND OF THE TEMPLE OF VĪRABHADRASVĀMI,
DRŌṆADULA, NARASARAOPETA TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Śaka Year not given.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. Refers to the grant of land made by Śrīnātharāja Veṅkaṭādri-
dēva-Mahārāja to the temple of the goddess Akkaladēvi of Drōṇādi.

inscription.head:
No. 224.
(A.R. No. 239 of 1905.)
ON THE EAST WALL OF THE DARK ROOM (CHĪKAṬI MIDDI) IN THE TEMPLE OF
TRIPURĀNTAKĒŚVARA, TRIPURĀNTAKAM, MĀRKĀPURAM TALUK,
KURNOOL DISTRICT.
Śaka Year not given.
inscription.p:
States that a certain Manuma Gannaya made a gift of cows, calves and
bullocks for perpetual lamps in the temple of Tripurāntakadēva.

inscription.head:
No. 225.
(A.R. No. 583 of 1925.)
ON A STONE LYING NEAR THE GĀLIGŌPURA-MAṆḌAPA OF THE TEMPLE OF
RĀMALINGĒŚVARA, VĒLPŪRU, SATTENAPALLI TALUK,
GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Year missing.
inscription.p:
States that Māṟeḍḍi, son of Guṇḍapa-Raḍḍi made a gift of 28 inupa-yeḍlu for a
perpetual lamp in the temple of Rāmēśvara of Vēlpunūru.

inscription.head:
No. 226.
(A.R. No. 447 of 1905.)
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE IN THE TEMPLE OF
VIJAYARĀGHAVA PERUMĀḶ, TIRUTTANI,
CHITTORE DISTRICT.
Year not given
inscription.p:
States that one Nārāyaṇarāja of the Karikāla-Chōḍa family built the temple
of god Vīrarāghava.

inscription.head:
No. 227.
(A.R. No. 397 of 1915.)
ON A MUTILATED SLAB (NOW USED AS A SURVEY STONE) IN A FIELD AT NĀDEṆḌLA,
NARASARAOPETA TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Date missing.
inscription.p:
Damaged. Refers to the grant of land made by some one for feeding 15 Brahmins
daily. Mentions Buddharāja of the Koṇḍapaḍumaṭi family.
