inscription.head:
No. 228.
(A.R. No. 398 of 1915.)
ON A MUTILATED STONE LYING IN THE SAME FIELD.
Date missing.
inscription.p:
States that Manma Maṇḍayarāja of the Koṇḍapaḍumaṭi family made a grant
of land at Nādeṇḍla to the temple of Sakalēśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 229.
(A.R. No. 855 of 1917.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE TEMPLE OF RĀMALINGĒŚVARA AT MASULIPATAM, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Year missing.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. Refers to the gift of 5 Kulōttuṅga-māḍas by a certain Pōliseṭṭi to
God Viśvēśvara of Ghaṇṭaśāla alias Chōḍa-Pāṇḍyapuram.
The beginning is lost.

inscription.head:
No. 230.
(A.R. No. 311 of 1915.)
ON A SLAB LYING IN A FIELD IN KĀMEPALLI, NARASARAOPETA TALUK,
GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p:
States that Kāma-Chō[ḍa-Mahārāja] made a gift of three puṭṭis of land to
Kāmēpalli Kētajiyya.

inscription.head:
No. 231.
(A.R. No. 407 of 1915.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF MALLĒŚVARA, BABBEPALLI,
SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p:
States the Tiru[kali]dēva-Chōḍa-Mahārāja established the temple of Tirukalīśa in
the village of Pabbapalli and made a gift of land to the said temple.

l.te: muṁkuṁṭa dakṣiṇāna kha 1 | yūritū¨rppuna kha 1 | paḍu-

inscription.head:
No. 232.
(A.R. No. 412 of 1915.)
ON A STONE BUILT INTO A WELL, PUNŪRU, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Year is not given.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. A portion of the usual praśasti of a Telugu-Chōḍa chief is
preserved.

inscription.head:
No. 233.
(A.R. No. 381 of 1915.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF VĪRABHADRASVĀMI, DRŌṆADULA,
SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p:
Illegible. Mentions the Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Anuṅgudēva Mahārāja of Pallināṇḍu
and the village Drōṇadi in Kammanāṇḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 234.
(A.R. No. 370 of 1915.)
AT THE BOTTOM OF THE RIGHT HAND COLUMN OF THE ENTRANCE INTO THE TEMPLE OF
ŚAṄKARĒŚVARA, KOṆIDENA, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Year not given.
inscription.p:
States that a certain Bollana gave 55 cows (?) for a perpetual lamp in the temple
of Śaṅkara-Mahādēva. Mentions a Chōḍa-Mahārāja.

inscription.head:
No. 235.
(A.R. No. 267 of 1905.)
ON A NANDI PILLAR IN FRONT OF THE DARK ROOM IN THE TEMPLE OF TRIPURĀNTAKĒŚVARA,
TRIPURĀNTAKAM, MARKAPURAM TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
Year doubtful.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. Refers to the Nāga pillar put up by Śrīdhara-Bhaṭṭa, the priest of
Pottapi Kāmadēva-Chōḍa-Mahārāja.

inscription.head:
No. 236.
(A.R. No. 377 of 1915.)
ON A STONE LYING NEAR THE TEMPLE OF ĀNJANĒYASVĀMI, JONNATALI, NARASARAOPETA
TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Year missing.
inscription.p:
Damaged and incomplete. Refers to a grant of land and perpetual lamps to
Tripurāntakadēva. Mentions Kulōttuṅga-Chōḍa Gōṅkarāja.

inscription.head:
No. 237.
(A.R. No. 348 of 1915.)
ON A STONE BUILT INTO THE NORTH WALL OF THE TEMPLE OF MALLĒŚVARA,
MULAKALŪRU, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p: Incomplete and damaged. Mentions [Ku]lōttuṅga-Chōḍa-Gō[ṅka].

inscription.head:
No. 238.
(A.R. No. 127 of 1917.)
ON A MUTILATED STONE BUILT INTO THE EAST WALL OF THE TEMPLE OF VĒṆUGŌPĀLA-
SVĀMI, PEDAPALAKALŪRU, GUNTUR TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p: Damaged and incomplete. Refers to a gift of land.

inscription.head:
No. 239.
(A.R. No. 699 of 1926.)
ON A STONE BUILT INTO THE SOUTH WALL OF THE TEMPLE OF RĀMĒŚVARA AT ĀCHANTA,
NARASAPURAM TALUK, WEST GODAVERY DISTRICT.
Year missing.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. Refers to certain gifts made presumably to the temple of
Rāmēśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 240.
(A.R. No. 342 of 1915.)
ON A SLAB SET UP NEAR THE IMAGE OF PŌLĒRAMMA, NEKARIKALLU, NARASARAOPETA
TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Year doubtful.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. Refers to the gift of land made by Chōḍapaseṭṭi, the minister of
Kalikāla-Chōḍa Gaṇḍa to the temple of goddess Nūṅkanamma. Gaṇḍa is stated to
have belonged to Rāṭṭa-vaṁśa.

l.note:
The figure in line 16 looks like 1117 but that does not tally with the year Dhātṛi. The
words Dhātṛi are however indistinct.

inscription.head:
No. 241.
(A.R. No. 273 of 1905.)
ON A SLAB LYING ON THE ROOF OF A SMALL MAṆḌAPA IN FRONT OF THE RĀMADĒVA
SHRINE IN THE TEMPLE OF TRIPURĀNTAKĒŚVARA, TRIPURANTAKAM,
MARKAPUR TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
Ś. 1107. (Viśvāvasu)
inscription.p:
States that Kākatīya Rudradēva I made a gift of the village of Rēvūru on the
banks of the Kṛishṇavēṇī river, in the Koṇḍapalli-nāḍu to god Tripurāntakēśvara of
Śrīparvata.

inscription.head:
No. 242.
(A.R. No. 300 of 1924.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN FRONT OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE IN THE TEMPLE OF MUKTĪŚVARA,
MUKTIYĀLA, NANDIGAMA TALUK, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Ś. 1121.
inscription.p:
States that the chief Tyāgi-Pōta made a gift of the village of Mukti-grāma,
thirty cows and a crown set with precious stones to god Mukti-Mahēśvara. Gives the
geneology of Pōta.

inscription.head:
No. 243.
(A.R. No. 575 of 1925.)
ON A NANDI PILLAR SET UP NEAR THE DHVAJASTAMBHA IN THE TEMPLE OF
RĀMALINGĒŚVARASVĀMI AT VĒLPŪRU, SATTENEPALLI TALUK,
GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1121.
inscription.p:
States that a certain Malli-Reḍḍi gave a perpetual lamp to god Rāmēśvara-
Mahādēva of Vē[lpunūru].

edition.l:
ṭṭeṁ gaḍu bhakti • • • || [3*] svasti śaka
varṣaṁbulu 1121

inscription.head:
No. 244.
(A.R. No. 576 of 1925.)
ON A NANDI PILLAR SET UP NEAR THE DHVAJASTAMBHA IN THE TEMPLE OF
RĀMALINGĒŚVARA AT THE SAME VILLAGE.
Ś. 1127.
inscription.p:
States that a certain Kēti Seṭṭi made a gift of 12 Gōkana-gadyānas for a
perpetual lamp in the temple of Rāmēśvara-Mahādēva of Vēlpunūru.

inscription.head:
No. 245.
(A.R. No. 262 of 1924.)
ON A BROKEN PILLAR LYING NEAR THE IMAGE OF ĀNJANĒYASVĀMI, KANCHALA, NANDIGAMA
TALUK, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Ś. 1127.
inscription.p: Damaged and incomplete. Mentions the Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Chāgirāja.

inscription.head:
No. 246.
(A.R. No. 177 of 1905.)
ON THE WEST WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE IN THE TEMPLE OF TRIPURĀNTAKA,
TRIPURĀNTAKAM, MARKAPUR TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
Ś. 11[3]0. (Vibhava)
inscription.p:
States that Mādhavadēva who was the chief of five Karaṇas (pañchakaraṇādhi-
pati) made a gift of 50 cows for a perpetual lamp in the temple of Tripurāntaka-
Mahādēva for the merit of his son Kanāradēva.

inscription.head:
No. 247.
(A.R. No. 109 of 1917.)
ON A STONE LYING IN A FIELD AT MUKKAMALA, GUNTUR TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1130.
inscription.p:
States that Vippaṟula Koṇḍapa-Nāyaka and Guṇḍapa-Nāyaka of Durjay-
ānvavāya made gifts of land to certain temples and also to certain persons, presumably
servants of the temples, for the merit of Kētarāju.

inscription.head:
No. 248.
(A.R. No. 803 of 1922.)
ON A PILLAR PLANTED BEFORE THE TEMPLE OF CHENNAKĒŚAVASVĀMI, IḌUPULAPĀḌU,
BAPATLA TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1131.
inscription.p:
States that the village of Iḍupulapāḍu in the Karmarāshṭra was originally
granted by Mukkaṇṭi Pallava to certain Brahmins of Kāṇva-Śākhā and that king
Gaṇapatidēva regranted it to the Brahmins. Contains a list of the donees and
their shares.

inscription.head:
No. 249.
(A.R. No. 554 of 1925.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE OUTER MAṆḌAPA OF THE TEMPLE OF RĀMALINGĒŚVARA VĒLPŪRU,
SATTENEPALLI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1131.
inscription.p:
States that Kasadi Sūramadēvi concubine of Kōṭa Kētarāja, gave 55 inupayeḍlu
for a perpetual lamp in the temple of Rāmēśvara of Vēlpunūru.

inscription.head:
No. 250.
(A.R. No. 281 of 1924.)
ON A PILLAR SET UP IN THE TEMPLE OF KĀŚI-VIŚVANĀTHA AT ANIGAṆḌLAPĀḌU,
NANDIGAMA TALUK, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Ś. 113[1].
inscription.p:
States that Īvani-Kaṇḍravāṭi Kēśavarāja made a gift of land (?) for the upkeep
of the worship of Mallikārjunadēva of Mūlasthāna. Refers to Paṇḍēśvaradēva and
certain priests.

inscription.head:
No. 251.
(A.R. No. 280 of 1924.)
ON A PILLAR SET UP IN THE SAME TEMPLE.
Ś. 11[3]1. (Śukla)
inscription.p:
States that Ivani-Kandrāḍi Kēśavarāja gave a maṟtu of land in Vēmutippa
for the worship of god Gōkīśvara-Mahādēva.

inscription.head:
No. 252.
(A.R. No. 560 of 1925.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE OUTER MAṆḌAPA OF THE TEMPLE OF RĀMALINGĒŚVARA, VĒLPŪRU,
SATTENAPALLI TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1131.
inscription.p:
States that Kōṭa Kētarāja's concubine Guṇḍadēvi gave 55 inupayeḍlu for a
perpetual lamp in the temple of Rāmēśvara of Vēlpunūru.

inscription.head:
No. 253.
(A.R. No. 304 of 1924.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE KALYĀṆA-MAṆḌAPA IN THE TEMPLE OF MUKTĪŚVARA, MUKTYĀLA,
NANDIGAMA TALUK, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Undated. (vide No. 234)
inscription.p:
States that Īvani-Kaṇḍravāṭi Kēśavadēva gave 25 cows for a perpetual lamp
in the temple of Muktīśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 254.
(A.R. No. 204 of 1905.)
ON FOUR FACES OF A PILLAR SET UP IN FRONT OF THE LINGA ON THE NORTH SIDE OF THE
KITCHEN IN THE TEMPLE OF TRIPURĀNTAKĒŚVARA, TRIPURANTAKAM, MARKAPUR TALUK,
KURNOOL DISTRICT.
Ś. 1131.
inscription.p:
States that Mēlāmbikā, sister of Kākatīya Gaṇapatidēva and wife of Ekkaḍi
Malla-Rudra, son of Buddarāja, chief of Nātavāḍi consecrated the image of
Mailāmbikēśvara in the temple of Tripurāntaka and endowed it with land in the village
of Dittālu. Gives the Kākatīya geneology.

inscription.head:
No. 255.
(A.R. No. 303 of 1924.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE KALYĀṆA-MAṆḌAPA OF THE TEMPLE OF MUKTĪŚVARA AT
MUKTYĀLA, NANDIGAMA TALUK, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Date lost.
inscription.p: Incomplete. Gives the geneology of Īvani-Kaṇḍravāṭi Kēśavarāja.

inscription.head:
No. 256.
(A.R. No. 88 of 1917.)
ON A STONE SET UP IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF MALLĒŚVARA, IPPATAM,
GUNTUR TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1133.
inscription.p:
Gives a list of temples and Brahmins to whom Kōṭa Mummaḍidēvarāja made a
gift of land at Ipaṭa during the reign of Kākatīya Gaṇapatidēva.

inscription.head:
No. 257.
(A.R. No. 324 of 1915.)
ON THE GARUDASTAMBHA IN THE TEMPLE OF VĒNUGŌPĀLASVĀMI, UPPUMAGULŪRU,
NARASARAOPETA TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1133.
inscription.p:
Damaged and partly illegible. Refers to the gift of an oil-mill and land made by
Ballichōḍarāja presumably to some temple.

inscription.head:
No. 258.
(A.R. No. 271 of 1905.)
ON THE FOUR FACES OF A PILLAR IN THE DARK ROOM OF THE TEMPLE OF
TRIPURĀNTAKĒŚVARA, TRIPURANTAKAM, MARKAPUR TALUK,
KURNOOL DISTRICT.
Ś. 1134. (Āngīrasa)
inscription.p:
States that a certain Rāmachandra Narēndra, grandson of Paṭṭapu Rāma-
dēvarāja made a gift of several villages to god Tripurāntaka of Tripurāntakam.

inscription.head:
No. 259.
(A.R. No. 284 of 1924.)
ON A PILLAR LYING IN THE TEMPLE OF CHANDRAMAULĪŚVARA, ANUMANCHIPALLI,
NANDIGAMA TALUK, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Ś. 1134. (Śrīmukha)
inscription.p:
States that a Brahmin Annimanchi, minister of Tyāgi Pōta, chief of Nātavaṭī
built a Śiva temple at Animanchipaḷḷi and endowed it with lands.

edition.p: Note:—The year 1134 should be taken as an expired year.

inscription.head:
No. 260.
(A.R. No. 79 of 1917.)
ON THE PROPER RIGHT COLUMN OF THE ENTRANCE INTO THE TEMPLE OF
SĪTĀRĀMASVĀMI, ERRABĀLEM, GUNTUR TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1135.
inscription.p:
States that Kōta Kētarāju's concubines Arama and Kētama and their father
Eṟṟamanāyaka presented an utsavadēva to the temple at Uṇḍavelli and gave a puṭṭi
of land for the worship of that utsavadēva.

inscription.head:
No. 261.
(A.R. No. 717 of 1920.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE TEMPLE OF KĒŚAVASVĀMI, DUGGIRALA, TENALI TALUK,
SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 113[6.] (Bhāva ?)
inscription.p:
Refers to the delimitation of the boundaries between Duggaṟēlapūṇḍi on the one
hand and Īvani, Duggamapūṇḍi and Moṟamapūṇḍi on the other hand, made by
Mallaparāja and Rudrarāja under the orders of king Gaṇapatidēva, given after he
occupied the Velanāṇḍu country.

l.te: nu peggaḍlaṁ gānaṁ bōyina vāru ma-

inscription.head:
No. 262.
(A.R. No. 723 of 1930.)
ON A STONE BUILT INTO THE TEMPLE OF RĀMĒŚVARA, NATTA-RĀMĒŚVARAM,
TANUKU TALUK, WEST GODAVARI DISTRICT.
Ś. 1140.
inscription.p:
States that Kolani Sōmaya, son of Kēśavadēva, lord of Kamalākarapura,
built the temple of Rāmēśvara at Duttika for the merit of his wife Anyamadēvi. Also
refers to gifts of land made by some other persons to the same temple.

inscription.head:
No. 263.
(A.R. No. 331 of 1915.)
ON A SLAB IN THE COMPOUND OF THE TEMPLE OF VĪRABHADRASVĀMI,
KOPPARAM, NARASARAOPETA TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1140.
inscription.p:
States that Ekki-Nāyaka, Rudri-Nāyaka, Pīna Rudri-Nāyaka, Rāji-Nāyaka
and Prōli-Nāyaka, sons of Prōla Rauta who was the tantrapāla of the Kākatīya king
Gaṇapatidēva gave to Prēkēṭi Mādirāju one martu of wet land and four puṭṭis of dry
land in Kopparam.

inscription.head:
No. 264.
(A.R. No. 851 of 1917.)
ON THE WHITE MARBLE PILLAR IF THE TEMPLE OF JALADHĪŚVARA,
GHAṆṬAŚĀLA, DIVI TALUK, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Ś. 1142 (Vikrama)
inscription.p:
States that Māriseṭṭi of Miḍḍikula-gōtra gave 25 cows and one breeding bull for
a perpetual lamp in the temple of Jaladhīśvara of Ghaṇṭaśāla.

inscription.head:
No. 265.
(A.R. No. 188 of 1917.)
ON A SLAB SET UP AT DURGIDĒVIPĀḌU IN THE VILLAGE OF MADIPĀḌU
SATTENEPALLI TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 114[2].
inscription.p:
States that one Muche-Nāyaka of Musuṇḍiri-anvaya, Penuṅgula-vaṁśa and
Manuma-kula, gave one puṭṭi of dry land to god Mallikārjuna of Jammipalli.

inscription.head:
No. 266.
(A.R. No. 237 of 1905.)
ON THE EAST WALL OF THE DARK ROOM (CHĪKATI-MIDDI) IN THE TEMPLE OF
TRIPURĀNTAKĒŚVARA, TRIPURANTAKAM, MARKAPUR TALUK,
KURNOOL DISTRICT.
Ś. uncertain (Vyaya).
inscription.p:
States that Eṟaganna-Nāyaka, the general (paḍālu) of the Mahāpradhāni
Podukamūri Annaladēva gave 25 cows for a perpetual lamp in the temple of
Tripurāntakadēva.

inscription.head:
No. 267.
(A.R. No. 174 of 1917.)
ON A STONE SET UP NEAR A POTTER'S HOUSE IN TĀḌIKOṆḌA,
GUNTUR TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1143.
inscription.p: Refers to the grant of ten tūmus of land to god Mailārudēva of Tāḍikoṇḍa.

inscription.head:
No. 268.
(A.R. No. 735 of 1920.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE TEMPLE OF SŌMĒŚVARA AT JUTTIGA, TANUKU TALUK,
WEST GODAVARI DISTRICT.
Ś. 1143.
inscription.p:
Refers to the gift of 50 inupa-yeḍlu by Eṟadēvaya, son of Daṇḍavalakala
Jommaya, servant of Kēśava-Maṇḍalika to the temple of Vāsukiravi-Sōmēśvara of
Ḍuttika.

inscription.head:
No. 269.
(A.R. No. 140 of 1917.)
ON A PILLAR OF THE MADHYARANGA, IN THE TEMPLE OF DANTĒŚVARA, PRATTIPĀḌU,
GUNTUR TALUK GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1144.
inscription.p:
States that Parichchhēda Kusmarāju established the image of Gaṇḍēśvara and
endowed it with two martus of land for the merit of his wife Sabbamadēvi.

l.te: ra pratiṣṭa sēsi tama kulapatni yaina sa-
paradattaṁ vā yō harēti vasuṁddharā śaṣṭi

inscription.head:
No. 270.
(A.R. No. 456 of 1915.)
ON A SLAB LYING IN A FIELD IN MUNUMAKA, NARASARAOPETA TALUK,
SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1145.
inscription.p:
Damaged and partly illegible. Refers to a gift of land to the temple of Amarēśvara
for the merit of Gaṇapatidēva-Mahārāja.

inscription.head:
No. 271.
(A.R. No. 330 of 1915.)
ON A PILLAR SET UP IN THE COURTYARD OF THE TEMPLE OF VĪRABHADRASVĀMI,
KOPPARAM, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Ś. 1145.
inscription.p:
States that the sons of Prōlrautu, the tantrapāla of Kākati Gaṇapatidēva made
gifts of land to Maṟuvāḍa Brammarāja, Kākāṇḍi Śrīrāmapeddi, Prōlepeddi
Lakkanappa and Nāraṇappa.

inscription.head:
No. 272.
(A.R. No. 597 of 1925.)
ON A MUTILATED STONE LYING NEAR A WELL AT VĒLPŪRU,
SATTENEPALLI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 114-.
inscription.p:
States that Guṇḍebōya's son (name lost) gave 55 sheep for a perpetual lamp in
the temple of Rāmēśvara at Vēlpunūru.

l.te:
nakuṁ beṭṭina goṟiyalu 55 vīniṁ jēkoni tama
putrā-

inscription.head:
No. 273.
(A.R. No. 325 of 1924.)
ON A BROKEN NĀGA PILLAR IN THE ŚIVA TEMPLE AT ZUZZŪRU, NANDIGAMA
TALUK, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Ś. 1150. (Sarvadhāri)
inscription.p: Incomplete. Mentions god Paramēśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 274.
(A.R. No. 275 of 1924.)
ON A PILLAR SET UP TO THE SOUTH OF THE ŚIVA TEMPLE, NAVĀBUPĒṬA,
SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Ś. 115[2]. (Tāraṇa)
inscription.p:
States that Chāgi Pōtarāja made a gift of wet land to god Svayambhu-
nātha-Mahādēva of Kuṟukkūru. Also gives a long list of similar gifts of land made to
the same temple by several other persons from time to time.

l.te: nadi na 4 || reddāla venaka na 3 ·ā paḍu[ma]-

inscription.head:
No. 275.
(A.R. No. 274 of 1924.)
ON A BROKEN PILLAR LYING IN FRONT OF THE SAME TEMPLE.
Ś. 1152. (This year tallies neither with Tāraṇa nor with Sādhāraṇa.
inscription.p:
Gives a list of grants of land made by Chāgi Pōtarāja and Chāgi Gaṇapatidēva
to god Sōmanāthadēva of Kuṟukūru and to Vīraṇa (Vīrēśvara) of Kosavīḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 276.
(A.R. No. 273 of 1924.)
ON THREE PIECES OF A BROKEN SLAB LYING IN FRONT OF THE SAME TEMPLE.
Ś. 1152. (This year does not tally with Tāraṇa.)
inscription.p:
Damaged and incomplete. Gives a list of land made by Chāgi Pōtarāja to god
Svayambhūdēva of Kuṟukūru.

inscription.head:
No. 277.
(A.R. No. 265 of 1924.)
ON A PILLAR IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF VĒṆUGŌPĀLASVĀMI, MĀGALLU,
NANDIGAMA TALUK, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Ś. 1152 (?).
inscription.p:
Damaged and incomplete. Refers to a gift of land made by the sons of Bachu
Sūrayya to god Gōpāladēva of Māgallu (?) for the benefit of the Kākatīya King
Ga[ṇapatidēva].

inscription.head:
No. 278.
(A.R. No. 606 of 1909.)
ON A PILLAR LYING IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF KŌDANḌARĀMASVĀMI,
MŌṬUPALLI, BAPATLA TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1153. (Khara.)
inscription.p:
States that Siddayadēva-Mahārāja of the family of Mukkaṇṭi Kāḍu[vaṭṭi*]
gave away the village of Ādūru to the temple of Gaṇapatiprasanna-Kēśavadēva at
Mōṭṭupalli for the merit of Kākatīya Gaṇapatidēva.

inscription.head:
No. 279.
(A.R. No. 60 of 1917.)
ON A STONE WITH THE IMAGE OF HUNUMĀN IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF
RĀMA, ANANTAVARAM, GUNTUR TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 115[3].
inscription.p:
States that one Ponungōṭi Rēvi Raḍḍi made a gift of land to the Mahājanas of
Vaḍḍamāni at the rate of one puṭṭi and ten tūms per share.

inscription.head:
No. 280.
(A.R. No. 305 of 1924.)
ON A SLAB SET UP TO THE SOUTH OF THE KALYĀṆAMAṆḌAPA IN THE TEMPLE OF
MUKTĪŚVARASVĀMI AT MUKTYĀLA, NANDIGAMA TALUK, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Ś. 1156.
inscription.p: Incomplete and damaged. Seems to refer to the Durjaya family.

inscription.head:
No. 281.
(A.R. No. 141 of 1917.)
ON A PILLAR OF THE MADHYARANGA OF THE TEMPLE OF DANDĒŚVARASVĀMI,
PRATTIPĀḌU, GUNTUR TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1156.
inscription.p: Gives a list of Brahmins to whom Kōṭa Gaṇapatidēva made gifts of land.

inscription.head:
No. 282.
(A.R. No. 134 of 1917.)
ON A GARUDA PILLAR IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF VĒṆUGŌPĀLASVĀMI, AT
POTTŪRU, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Ś. 115[7].
inscription.p:
States that Parichchhēdi Bhīmarāja. son of Kommarāja consecrated the image
of god Gōpāla and built a temple over it and also endowed it with lands.

inscription.head:
No. 283.
(A.R. No. 278 (a) of 1905.)
ON A SLAB SET UP CLOSE TO THE PRĀKĀRA ON THE RIGHT SIDE OF THE CLOSED UP
WESTERN ENTRANCE TO THE TEMPLE OF TRIPURĀNTAKĒŚVARA
TRIPURANTAKAM, MARKAPUR TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
Ś. 1157. (Manmatha)
inscription.p:
States that a certain Vishaveli Masake-Sāhiṇi gave 25 cows for a perpetual lamp
in the temple of Tripurāntakadēva, in commemoration of his having won the battle of
Chintalapūṇḍi which he fought riding (the horse) Puṇyamūrti.

inscription.head:
No. 284.
(A.R. No. 463 of 1915.)
ON A SLAB IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF BRAHMĒŚVARA, KOṆḌANĀYANAVARAM,
GANNAVARAM TALUK, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Ś. 1157.
inscription.p:
States that Nāgadēva of the family of Kāḍuveṭṭi made a gift of cows to the
temple of Brahmēśvara.

l.te: ya tēnāsi dattā jalamahi varā | savvrīhi

inscription.head:
No. 285.
(A.R. No. 484 of 1913.)
ON A SLAB PRESERVED IN AN UNDERGROUND ROOM OF THE
CENTRAL MUSEUM, MADRAS.
Ś. 1157.
inscription.p:
Damaged and incomplete. Seems to refer to a gift of cows made by the son of
the wet nurse (dādi) of Kōṭa Manma-Kētarāja to the temple of god Rudra. (?)

inscription.head:
No. 286.
(A.R. No. 720 of 1920.)
ON A BEAM IN THE ROOF OF THE TEMPLE OF MUKTĪŚVARA, KOLLIPARA,
TENALI TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 11[5]8.
inscription.p: Incomplete and unintelligible. Mentions god Muktīśvara-Mahādēva.

inscription.head:
No. 287.
(A.R. No. 278(b) of 1905.)
IN CONTINUATION OF A. R. 278 (A) OF 1905. ON A SLAB SET UP CLOSE TO THE PRĀKĀR
ON THE RIGHT SIDE OF THE CLOSED UP WESTERN ENTRANCE TO THE TEMPLE
OF TRIPURĀNTAKĒŚVARA, TRIPURANTAKAM, MARKAPUR TALUK,
KURNOOL DISTRICT.
Ś. 1159. (Hēviḷambi)
inscription.p:
States that a certain Masaṁge Ravuta gave 13 cows for a perpetual lamp in the
temple of Tripurāntaka-Mahādēva.

l.te:
vatsara kā¨rttika śū5 budhava | śrītripurāṁ

inscription.head:
No. 288.
(A.R. No. 63 of 1917.)
ON A STONE LYING NEAR A WELL IN ĀTMAKŪRU, GUNTUR TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1160.
inscription.p:
States that Pālakolani Kēti-Nāyaka built a temple for god Lakshmīśvara at
Ātukūru in Doḍḍakaṇḍravāḍi and gave 25 cows for a perpetual lamp in the said
temple. Also gives a list of gifts of land made by the same person to the said temple
and also to certain Brahmins.

inscription.head:
No. 289.
(A.R. No. 65 of 1917.)
ON A NANDI PILLAR SET UP NEAR A TANK, AT BĒJĀTPURAM, SAME TALUK
AND DISTRICT.
Ś. 11[6]0.
inscription.p:
States that Prōle-Nāyaka, lord of Poḍvupura and minister of Kōṭa Gaṇapati-
dēva consecrated the images of Rāmēśvara and Gaṇē[śvara] at Pulipāḍu and built
temples for the said deities and for goddess Brōsinamma and had four tanks dug, that
his younger brother consecrated the images of Kēśavadēva and Kuppavināyaka and built
temples for them and that both of them granted lands to temple priests.

inscription.head:
No. 290.
(A.R. No. 558 of 1925.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE OUTER MAṆḌAPA OF THE TEMPLE OF RĀMALINGĒŚVARA,
VĒLPŪRU, SATTENEPALLI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1161. (Vikāri)
inscription.p:
States that a certain Aita-Sāni a dependent of Kākati Gaṇapatidēva gave
25 cows for a perpetual lamp in the temple of Rāmēśvara of Vēlpunūru.

l.te: sāni koḍku gōkinā·iniki yaitasāniki suputru laina

inscription.head:
No. 291.
(A.R. No. 556 of 1925.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAME MAṆḌAPA.
Ś. 1162.
inscription.p:
States that Aḍapa Kētana-Bōya, a subordinate of Kōṭa Gaṇapatidēva gave
55 inupa-yeḍlu for a perpetual lamp in the temple of Rāmēśvara of Vēlpūru.

inscription.head:
No. 292.
(A.R. No. 722 of 1910.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE TEMPLE OF AGASTYĒŚVARA, KOLAKALŪRU
TENALI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 116[3].
inscription.p:
States that a certain Amari Nāyaka gave 2 puṭṭis of land as emoluments to the
dancing girls attached to the temple of Agastīśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 293.
(A.R. No. 324 of 1919.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE MAṆḌAPA AT THE NORTHERN ENTRANCE INTO THE TEMPLE OF
MALLĒŚVARA AT BEZWADA, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Ś. 1163.
inscription.p:
States that Paruvala Malliseṭṭi of Goṇṭūru and belonging to the Peṇṭikula-
gōtra gave 25 cows for a perpetual lamp in the temple of Mallīśvara.

l.te:
ṇammuduvanākhyaḥ sūnu rāsīttataḥ paruvā nnāh[va]ya satsutaḥ pṛthuyaśō būmāṁ

inscription.head:
No. 294.
(A.R. No. 562 of 1909.)
ON A SLAB FORMING THE WALL OF A SMALL SHRINE IN THE COURTYARD OF THE
TEMPLE OF DOḌḌA MALLĒŚVARA, CHINTAPALLI, SATTENEPALLI TALUK,
GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 11[6]3. (Plava)
inscription.p:
Incomplete. Refers to a gift of land to Mūlasthānam Mallināthadēva at Chinta-
palli.

inscription.head:
No. 295.
(A.R. No. 254 of 1924.)
ON A SLAB LYING CLOSE TO THE STREAM AT AḌAVIRĀVULAPĀḌU,
NANDIGĀMA TALUK, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Ś. 1164. (Śubhakṛit)
inscription.p:
Mentions grants of land made by Drōṇādula Pōti-Nāyuḍu and others to god
Rāmanātha-Mahādēva at Rāvulapāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 296.
(A.R. No. 236 of 1905.)
ON THE EAST WALL OF THE DARK ROOM IN THE TEMPLE OF TRIPURĀNTAKĒŚVARA,
TRIPURANTAKAM, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
Ś. 1164. (Śubhakṛit)
inscription.p:
States that Chāgi Muppaladēvi gave 25 cows for a peretual lamp in the temple
of Tripurāntaka-Mahādēva. Also mentions Nāthavāḍi Rudradēva-Mahārāja,
Kākatīya Maila-Mahādēvi and Muppaladēvi.

l.te:
[vīniṁ] jēkoni ·eḍa • pabōni • • • dāmanabōyiṁḍu nitya māneṁḍu neyi
neyi tana [biḍḍabiḍḍataramu]

inscription.head:
No. 297.
(A.R. No. 721 of 1920.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE TEMPLE OF AGASTYĒŚVARASVĀMI, KOLAKALŪR,
TENALI TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 11[64].
inscription.p:
States that Reṇṭūri Ekkiṭi, servant of Koṇḍapaḍumaṭi Bētarāja gave 50 cows
for two lamps in the temple of Agastīśvara of Kolaṅkulūru.

inscription.head:
No. 298.
(A.R. No. 607 of 1909.)
ON A PILLAR LYING IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF KŌDANḌARĀMASVĀMI, MŌṬUPALLI,
BAPATLA TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1164. (Śubhakṛit)
inscription.p:
States that Bayya Chōḍa-Mahārāja gave a puṭṭi of wet land to god Prasanna-
Kēśavadēva of Mōṭṭupaḷḷi.

inscription.head:
No. 299.
(A.R. No. 465 of 1915.)
ON THE TOP BEAM OF THE INNER ENTRANCE INTO THE TEMPLE OF BRAHMĒŚVARA,
KOṆḌANĀYANAVARAM, GANNAVARAM TALUK, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Ś. 11[65] (?).
inscription.p:
States that Paruva-Anni-śeṭṭi of Peṇḍikula-gōtra gave 25 cows for a perpetual
lamp in the temple of Prasanna Vallabha-Kēśavadēva of Goṇṭūru.

inscription.head:
No. 300.
(A.R. No. 598 of 1925.)
ON A MUTILATED STONE LYING NEAR A WELL AT VĒLPŪRU, SATTENEPALLI TALUK,
GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1165. (Śōbhakṛit)
inscription.p:
States that Aḍapa Kētana-Bōya gave 55 inupa-yeḍlu for a perpetual lamp in the
temple of Rāmēśvara-Mahādēva of Vēlpunūru.

inscription.head:
No. 301.
(A.R. No. 311 of 1920.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE TEMPLE OF SVARṆĒŚVARA AT GANAPAVARAM,
BHĪMAVARAM TALUK, WEST GODAVARI DISTRICT.
Ś. 1165.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. Refers to a gift made by Enamadala Anuma-Nāyaka, servant of
Kolani Maṅgayadēvarāja to the temple of Svarṇēśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 302.
(A.R. No. 826 of 1922.)
ON A STONE BUILT INTO THE PLATFORM OF THE TEMPLE OF KĀŚĪNĀTHA AT
KOMMŪRU, BAPATLA TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1166.
inscription.p:
States that a certain Prōli-Nāyaka gave seven Kēsari-gadyas for a perpetual lamp
in the temple of Agastīśvara of Kommūru.

inscription.head:
No. 303.
(A.R. No. 466 of 1915.)
ON A STONE SET UP IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF RĀMALINGĒŚVARA,
SANTARĀVŪRU, BAPATLA TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1167. (Viśvāvasu)
inscription.p:
Records the grant of land by Karaṇam Māraya to god Rāmanātha for the
benefit of Gaṇapatidēva-Mahārāja.

inscription.head:
No. 304.
(A.R. No. 245 of 1905.)
ON THE EAST WALL OF THE DARK ROOM (CHĪKAṬI-MIDDI) IN THE TEMPLE
OF TRIPURĀNTAKĒŚVARA, TRIPURĀNTAKAM, MARKAPUR TALUK,
KURNOOL DISTRICT.
Ś. 1167. (Viśvāvasu)
inscription.p:
States that Kākatīya Gaṇapatidēva's agent Pōchana-Peggaḍa-Gaṇapaya
remitted customs duties payable on three hundred pack bullocks used in importing goods
into the town of Tripurāntakam which belongs to god Tripurāntaka.

edition.p: Note:—The figure 1177 ·in the first line is evidently a mistake for 1163•

inscription.head:
No. 305.
(A.R. No. 235 of 1905.)
ON THE EAST WALL OF THE DARK ROOM IN THE SAME TEMPLE.
Ś. 1168. (Parābhava)
inscription.p:
States that Chāgi Muppaladēvi gave 25 cows for a perpetual lamp in the temple
of Tripurāntaka-Mahādēva.

edition.l:
na kiccina modavulu 25 vīniṁ jēkoni cennapaseṭṭi penniseṭṭi [nitya]māneṁḍu neyi tana
(The ·inscription stops here ·abruptly)•

inscription.head:
No. 306.
(A.R. No. 234 of 1905.)
ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE DARK ROOM IN THE SAME TEMPLE.
Ś. 1168. (Parābhava)
inscription.p:
States that Chāgi Gaṇapayarāja gave 25 cows for a perpetual lamp in the temple
of Tripurāntaka-Mahādēva.

l.te: • • • ccina modavulu 25 vīniṁ jēkoni tipirebōnikoḍuku mallebōyi

inscription.head:
No. 307.
(A.R. No. 244 of 1905.)
ON THE BASE OF THE NORTH WALL OF THE SAME DARK ROOM.
Ś. 1168. (Parābhava)
inscription.p:
States that Amarēśvara, son of Karaṇam Mallaparāja, minister of Chāgi
Dōrayarāja gave 25 cows for a perpetual lamp in the temple of Tripurāntaka-Mahādēva.

inscription.head:
No. 308.
(A.R. No. 243 of 1905.)
ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE SAME DARK ROOM.
Ś. 1168. (Parābhava)
inscription.p:
States that Kōṭa Mummaḍi Pōtarāja gave 15 cows for a perpetual lamp in the
temple of Tripurāntaka-Mahādēva.

l.te: śastisahitaṁ śrīmanmahāmaṁḍalēśvara kōṭa mummaḍipōtarājulu svasti śrīśu-
varaku damataṁḍri cōḍrājunakuṁ damatalliṁ viṁjamadēvulakuṁ danatammuṁ-

inscription.head:
No. 309.
(A.R. No. 312 of 1915.)
ON A CARVED STAMBHA IN THE TEMPLE OF HANUMĀN, KĀKĀNI,
NARASARAOPETA TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1168.
inscription.p:
States that a Brahmin named Śrī Rāma Peddi consecrated the image of Kēśava
in the agrahāra of Kākāṇḍa in the kingdom of Kākatīya Gaṇapati and that he and some
others endowed the temple with lands.

inscription.head:
No. 310.
(A.R. No. 138 of 1917.)
ON A SLAB LYING IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF VĒNUGŌPĀLASVĀMI, POTTŪRU,
GUNTUR TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1168.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. The portion which describes the actual grant is missing. The portion
available refers to what was probably a gift made to a Śiva temple by Parichchēda
Bhīmarāja, Tammu Bhīmarāja, Dēvarāja and Gaṇapadēvarāja for the merit of their
father Kōmmarāja and mother Sūraladēvi. Contains the usual Parichchēdi titles.

edition.p: Note:—The impression of the inscription on the second face is missing.

inscription.head:
No. 311.
(A.R. No. 241 of 1905.)
ON THE EAST WALL OF THE DARK ROOM (CHĪKAṬI-MIDDI) IN THE TEMPLE OF
TRIPURĀNTAKĒŚVARA, TRIPURANTAKAM, MARKAPUR TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
Ś. 1168. (Parābhava)
inscription.p:
States that a certain Prōlāsāni gave 50 cows for a perpetual lamp in the temple of
Tripurāntaka-Mahādēva, for the merit of her husband Gadya Sūrapa-Nāyaka.

inscription.head:
No. 312.
(A.R. No. 563 of 1915.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE COURTYARD OF THE TEMPLE OF JŌTINĀTHA, AT JŌTI,
SIDHOUT TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
Ś. 1169. (Plavaṅga)
inscription.p:
States that, during the reign of Rakkasa Gaṅgarasadēva, Chente Rāmi-Nāyaka
built the compound wall and gōpura of the temple of Jōtinātha, the temple of Īrugala-
sāni at Takaprōlu and had two tanks dug and a temple built at Chēṭūru.

inscription.head:
No. 313.
(A.R. No. 257 of 1924.)
ON A SLAB LYING UNDER A TREE NORTH OF THE VILLAGE OF MUPPALLA,
NANDIGAMA TALUK, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Ś. 11[6]9.
inscription.p:
States that general Māka, son of Kommana, servant of Tyāgi Manma-Pōta,
lord of Bṛihatkāñchī (Pedda Kancherla ?) gave 25 cows for a perpetual lamp in the
temple of Rāmēśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 314.
(A.R. No. 586 of 1925.)
ON A STONE LYING NEAR THE GĀLIGŌPURA MAṆṬAPAM IN THE TEMPLE OF
RĀMALINGĒŚVARA, VĒLPŪRU, SATTENEPALLI TALUK,
GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1169. (Plavaṅga)
inscription.p:
States that Dōchena Peggaḍa assigned (?) to the temple of Rāmēśvara at Vēlpu-
nūru the duty known as aḍḍavaṭṭa and duties leviable on sales and purchases of
various articles, on oil-mills and on marriages, for the merit of Kākatīya Gaṇapatidēva
Mahārāja.

lcina bhaṁḍi vilcina varu vilcina yē

inscription.head:
No. 315.
(A.R. No. 253 of 1905.)
ON A PILLAR AT THE ENTRANCE TO THE DARK ROOM (CHĪKAṬI-MIDDI)-
RIGHT SIDE-IN THE TEMPLE OF TRIPURĀNTAKĒŚVARA, TRIPURĀNTAKAM,
MARKAPUR TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
Ś. 1169. (Plavaṅga)
inscription.p:
States that Dharma Śivāchārya, disciple of Bimala Śivāchārya of the Gōḷagiri-
maṭha built the Bhaṇḍāruvu-maṭham (temple treasury) in the temple of Tripurāntaka-
Mahādēva.

inscription.head:
No. 316.
(A.R. No. 175 of 1917.)
ON A STONE LYING NEAR A HILLOCK TO THE WEST OF TĀḌIKOṆḌA, GUNTUR TALUK,
GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1169. (?) (Kīlaka)
inscription.p:
States that Kōṭa Immaḍi Gaṇapatirāja gave a puṭṭi of land to the temple of
Chenna-Mallēśvara.

edition.p:
Note:—This inscription is largely illegible. This reading is based on the transcript prepared
in the Epigraphical Office.

inscription.head:
No. 317.
(A.R. No. 537 of 1925.)
ON A PILLAR LYING NEAR THE TEMPLE OF CHENNAKĒŚVASVĀMI, KŌLAVENNU,
BEZWADA TALUK, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Ś. 1170.
inscription.p:
States that Agent (Sarvādhikāri) Siviri Annaya assigned, to the temple of
Prasanna-Kēśavadēva of Kōlavennu, the customs duties (suṅkam) for the merit of
Gaṇapatidēva-Mahārāja.

inscription.head:
No. 318.
(A.R. No. 240 of 1905.)
ON THE EAST WALL OF THE DARK ROOM (CHĪKAṬI-MIDDI) IN THE TEMPLE OF
TRIPURĀNTAKA, TRIPURĀNTAKAM, MARKAPUR TALUK,
KURNOOL DISTRICT.
Ś. 1170. (Kīlaka)
inscription.p:
States that a certain Daṇḍena-Peggaḍa gave 25 cows for a perpetual lamp in the
temple of Tripurāntakēśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 319.
(A.R. No. 232 of 1905.)
ON THE SAME WALL.
Ś. 1170. (Kīlaka).
inscription.p:
States that Kālari Pinnamāri-Nāyaka gave 25 cows for a perpetual lamp in the
temple of Tripurāntakadēva.

inscription.head:
No. 320.
(A.R. No. 405 of 1915.)
ON A NANDI PILLAR STANDING IN A STREET IN PURUSHŌTTMAPAṬṬAṆAM,
NARASARAOPETA TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 11[70]. (Kīlaka)
inscription.p:
States that Ekkaya, younger brother of Bhāskaradēva, minister of Gaṇapati-
dēva gave away the village of Oḍḍapūṇḍi to god Śaṅkarēśvara of Koṭyadona.

inscription.head:
No. 321.
(A.R. No. 559 of 1925.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE OUTER MAṆḌAPA OF THE TEMPLE OF RĀMALINGĒŚVARA,
VĒLPŪRU, SATTENEPALLI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1170. (Kīlaka)
inscription.p:
States that a certain Sūrapa-Nāyaka gave 25 cows for a perpetual lamp in the
temple of Rāmēśvara at Vēlpunūru.

inscription.head:
No. 322.
(A.R. No. 227 of 1905.)
ON THE BASE OF THE NORTH WALL, SECOND TIER OF THE DARK ROOM IN THE
TEMPLE OF TRIPURĀNTAKĒŚVARA, TRIPURĀNTAKAM, MARKAPUR
TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
Ś. 1170. (Kīlaka)
inscription.p:
States that Mahādēvarāja, son of Nātavāṭi Rudradēva-Mahārāja and Kāketa
Mailala-Mahādēvi, gave 25 cows for a perpetual lamp in the temple of
Tripurāntakēśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 323.
(A.R. No. 228 of 1905.)
ON THE BASE OF THE NORTH WALL, SECOND TIER OF THE
DARK ROOM IN THE SAME TEMPLE.
Ś. 1170. (Kīlaka)
inscription.p:
States that Mummaḍi Gaṇampaṭṭavaṇḍu (?) son of Natāvāṭi Rudradēva and
Kāketa Mailala-Mahādēvi gave 25 cows for a perpetual lamp in the temple of
Tripurāntakadēva.

inscription.head:
No. 324.
(A.R. No. 225 of 1905.)
ON THE BASE OF THE SAME WALL.
Ś. 1170. (Kīlaka)
inscription.p:
States that Rudradēva, son of Nātavāṭi Rudradēva-Mahārāja and Kāketa
Mailala-Mahādēvi gave 25 cows for a perpetual lamp in the temple of Tripurāntakadēva.

inscription.head:
No. 325.
(A.R. No. 555 of 1925.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE INNER MAṆḌAPA OF THE TEMPLE OF RĀMALINGĒŚVARA,
VĒLPŪRU, SATTENEPALLI TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1171.
inscription.p:
States that a certain Rāyidēva gave 55 sheep for a perpetual lamp in the temple
of Rāmēśvara of Vēlpūru.

inscription.head:
No. 326.
(A.R. No. 557 of 1925.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE OUTER MAṆḌAPA OF THE SAME TEMPLE.
Ś. 1171.
inscription.p: Incomplete. Mentions Rāyanāmātya, son of Kāmanāmātya and Kāmāmbā.

edition.p:
[Note:—The date Śaka 1171 Āshāḍha Śu. 14 Sunday as given in this and in the precedinginscrip-
tion corresponds regularly to Sunday the 5th July 1248 A.D. thereby indicating that the year was a
current and not an expired one, as is usually used.]

inscription.head:
No. 327.
(A.R. No. 249 of 1905.)
ON A PILLAR AT THE ENTRANCE INTO THE DARK ROOM IN THE TEMPLE OF
TRIPURĀNTAKĒŚVARA, TRIPURĀNTAKAM, MARKAPUR TALUK,
KURNOOL DISTRICT.
Ś. 1171. (Saumya)
inscription.p:
States that Jāyasāni, wife of Chōḍebōya, son of Eñjali Chōḍebōya, gave
50 goats for a perpetual lamp in the temple of Tripurāntaka-Mahādēva.

l.te: svajanatā bhuvi tasya patnī | śaṁbhō ru maiva
divijādhipatē śśa cīva viṣṇōra maiva hi-

inscription.head:
No. 328.
(A.R. No. 221 of 1905.)
ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE DARK ROOM IN THE SAME TEMPLE.
Ś. 1171. (Saumya)
inscription.p:
States that Chikarāja assigned, to the temple of Tripurāntakēśvara (?), the duties
payable on salt imported into and sold in Tripurāntakam, the town of Tripurāntakēśvara.
The grant is said to have been made for the merit of Nūvula Rāmadēva Peggaḍa during
the reign of Kākatīya Gaṇapatidēva-Mahārāja.

edition.p: Note:—The year Saumya corresponds to Ś. 1171 (expired) or Ś. 1172 (current).

inscription.head:
No. 329.
(A.R. No. 226 of 1905.)
ON THE BASE OF THE NORTH WALL, SECOND TIER OF THE DARK ROOM
(CHĪKATI-MIDDI), IN THE SAME TEMPLE.
Ś. 1172. (Sādhāraṇa)
inscription.p:
States that Koṇḍapaḍumaṭi Murāri Gaṇapaṁdēva-Mahārāja gave 25 cows for
a perpetual lamp in the temple of Tripurāntakadēva.

l.te:
• • • na mōdālu 25 vīni cēkoni silavareḍḍi nityamāneḍu nēyi na[ḍa]paṁgala
vāḍu ||

inscription.head:
No. 330.
(A.R. No. 82 of 1917.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE TEMPLE OF ĀNJANĒYASVĀMI, GUNTUR, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1172.
inscription.p:
States that Bōḍuga Prōle-Bōya is to supply a sōla of ghee daily for a perpetual
lamp in the temple of Chōḍīśvara of Chēmbrōlu.

inscription.head:
No. 331.
(A.R. No. 209 of 1905.)
ON THE BASE OF THE WEST WALL OF THE DARK ROOM IN THE TEMPLE OF TRIPURĀNTAKĒŚVARA,
TRIPURĀNTAKAM, MARKAPUR TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
Ś. 1172. (Sādhāraṇa)
inscription.p:
States that the son of a certain Śrīpati-Nāyaka and a certain Lakumā-Bāyi
gave 10 cows each for a lamp in the temple of Tripurāntakadēva.

l.te:
svasti śrīśakavaruṣaṁbulu 1172 ·agu nēṭi sādhāraṇasaṁvatsara kā¨rttikapa¨rvva nimitya
muna

inscription.head:
No. 332.
(A.R. No. 283 of 1905.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE COURTYARD OF THE MŪLABRAHMĒŚVARA TEMPLE
AT THE FOOT OF THE TRIPURĀNTAKAM HILL, TRIPURĀNTAKAM,
SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1172 (Sādhāraṇa)
inscription.p:
States that Gaṇḍapeṇḍāru Gaṅgaya-Sāhiṇi gave away all the lands in the
villages of Bōyalapalli and Reḍlapalli with all sources of income to god Mūlasthāna-
dēva of Tripurāntakamu, for the merit of Gaṇapatīdēva-Mahārāja.

l.te: • ḍiya • ya diśāpaṭṭa mūrurāya jagadaḷa barabala • • •

edition.p: Note:—The year Sādhāraṇa corresponds to the Śaka year 1172 and not to 1175.

inscription.head:
No. 333.
(A.R. No. 462 of 1915.)
ON A PILLAR IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF BRAHMĒŚVARA, KOṆḌANĀYANIVARAM,
GANNAVARAM TALUK, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Ś. 1173.
inscription.p:
States that the chief, Nārāyaṇa, younger brother of Nāga of Bhāradvāja-gōtra
gave 25 cows for a lamp and one and half marutus of land for naivēdyam in the temple
of Brahmēśvara.

l.te:
dēvā yāsmai nivēdyā¨rddhaṁ dattā bhū śśā
limā-

inscription.head:
No. 334.
(A.R. No. 571 of 1909.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE TEMPLE OF VAṄKĒŚVARASVĀMI, DURGI,
PALNAD TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1173 (Virōdhikṛit)
inscription.p:
States that Nāmadēva-Paṇḍita, the agent of Gaṇḍapeṇḍāru Gaṅgayya-Sāhiṇī,
who was governing the country from Pānuṅgallu to Mārjavāḍi during the reign of
Kākatīya Gaṇapatidēva, consecrated the image of Vaṅkēśvara at Dugyapaṭṭaṇa in
Pallināṇḍu in the name of his father Vāyi-Paṇḍita and that Gaṅgayya-Sāhiṇi endowed
the temple with the village of Neypūru east of Nāgārjuna Kōṭa. Also mentions other
gifts to the same temple. Gives a full account of the Kākatīya family.

inscription.head:
No. 335.
(A.R. No. 251 of 1905.)
ON A PILLAR ON THE RIGHT SIDE OF THE ENTRANCE INTO THE DARK ROOM
(CHĪKAṬI MIDDI) IN THE TEMPLE OF TRIPURĀNTAKA, TRIPURĀNTAKAM,
MARKAPUR TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
Ś. 1173 (Virōdhikṛit)
inscription.p:
States that Meḍapīkilla Eṟṟanūkiseṭṭi gave 25 cows for a perpetual lamp in the
temple of Tripurāntakadēva.

l.te:
seṭi tama ta[ṁ]ḍri jāmiseṭikiṁ tama talli
māra-

inscription.head:
No. 336.
(A.R. No. 252 of 1905.)
ON A PILLAR AT THE ENTRANCE TO THE SAME ROOM.
Ś. 117[3] (Virōdhikṛit)
inscription.p:
States that Mēḍapikilla Nūkiseṭṭi gave 25 cows for a perpetual lamp in the
temple of Tripurāntakadēva.

l.te: ṭṭi tama taṁḍri būmi seṭṭikiṁ dama talli du-

inscription.head:
No. 337.
(A.R. No. 333 of 1924.)
ON A PILLAR LYING IN THE ĀNJANĒYA TEMPLE AT KONATAMATKŪRU,
NANDIGAMA TALUK, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Ś. 1173.
inscription.p:
States that a certain Dāmanāmātya of Lōhita-gōtra consecrated the image of god
Sūrēśvara, built a temple and maṇḍapa for the same and also endowed it with land for the
merit of Leṅkavaṇṭi Kōka, the minister of Kākatīya Gaṇapatidēva and of a certain
Annaya.

inscription.head:
No. 338.
(A.R. No. 195 of 1905.)
ON THE BASE OF THE SOUTH WALL OF THE KITCHEN-VANṬAMIDDI-IN FRONT OF THE
MAṆḌAPA IN THE TEMPLE OF TRIPURĀNTAKĒŚVARA, TRIPURANTAKAM,
KURNOOL DISTRICT.
Ś. 1174 (54th year of Gaṇapatidēva) (Parīdhāvi)
inscription.p:
Incomplete. Mentions Kākati Gaṇapatidēva with his titles and also refers to an
unnamed pontiff of the Gōḷagī-maṭha.

inscription.head:
No. 339.
(A.R. No. 229 of 1905.)
ON THE BASE OF THE NORTH WALL, SECOND TIER OF THE DARK ROOM IN THE
SAME TEMPLE.
Ś. 1174. (Parīdhāvi)
inscription.p:
States that Pedalūri Sōmarāja, son of Jākarāja gave 25 cows for a perpetual
lamp in the temple of Tripurāntaka-Mahādēva for the merit of his wife Vidda Sāni.

l.te: munaku jākarāju koḍuku śrīmallikā¨rjunadēvaru divyaśrīpādapadmārādhaku laina

inscription.head:
No. 340.
(A.R. No. 223 of 1905.)
ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE SAME ROOM.
Ś. 1174 and 1175 (Parīdhāvi and Pramādi)
inscription.p:
States that Viśvēśvara-Śivāchārya of the Gōḷagī-maṭha, who was the guru of
Kākatīya Gaṇapatidēva, made grants of land to god Tripurāntaka-Mahādēva.

inscription.head:
No. 341.
(A.R. No. 219 of 1905.)
ON THE SAME WALL.
Ś. 1175. (Pramādi)
inscription.p:
States that a certain Dāmōdarappayya gave 50 cows for a perpetual lamp in the
temple of Tripurāntaka-Mahādēva.

inscription.head:
No. 342.
(A.R. No. 216 of 1905.)
ON THE BASE OF THE WEST WALL OF THE DARK ROOM IN THE SAME TEMPLE.
Ś. 1175. (Pramādi)
inscription.p:
States that a certain Nūṅkana Bōya gave 13 cows for a perpetual lamp in the
temple of Tripurāntaka-Mahādēva.

l.te: pauṣyaśu 5 śu nāṁṭi makarasaṁkrāṁtinimittamuna śrītripurāṁtaka mahādēva-

inscription.head:
No. 343.
(A.R. No. 231 of 1905.)
ON THE EAST WALL OF THE SAME ROOM.
Ś. 1176. (Ānanda)
inscription.p:
States that Nāmadēva-Paṇḍita, the agent of Gaṇḍapeṇḍāru Gaṅgayya-Sāhiṇi,
a dependent of Kākatīya Gaṇapatidēva, made a gift of the village of [K]eṅkulakuṇṭa
and 40 cows to the temple of Tripurāntaka-Mahādēva.

inscription.head:
No. 344.
(A.R. No. 564 of 1925.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE OUTER MAṆḌAPA OF THE TEMPLE OF RĀMALINGĒŚVARA, VĒLPŪRU,
SATTENEPALLI TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1176.
inscription.p:
States that Gaṇapatidēvarāja, son of Kōṭa Bayyaladēvi, gave away Bhaṇḍāra-
mu Akkama as a sāni (dancing girl) to the temple of Rāmēśvara at Vēlpunūru and
also land, house-site and a garden.

inscription.head:
No. 345.
(A.R. No. 169 of 1905.)
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE IN THE TEMPLE OF TRIPURĀNTAKĒŚVARA,
TRIPURĀNTAKAM, MARKAPUR TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
Ś. 1176. (Ānanda)
inscription.p:
States that Śānta-Śambhu, son of Viśvēśāchārya built a vimāna with white
marbles (in the temple of Tripurāntakēśvara).

inscription.head:
No. 346.
(A.R. No. 176 of 1905.)
ON THE SAME WALL.
Ś. 1177. (Rākshasa)
inscription.p:
States that Gaṇḍapeṇḍāru Gaṅgayya-Sāhiṇi made a gift of the village of
Pūlacheṟuvu in Moṭṭavāḍi to god Tripurāntaka-Mahādēva for the merit of Gaṇapati-
dēva-Mahārāja. States further that Gaṅgayya-Sāhiṇi was a Kāyastha by caste.

l.te:
svasti śrīmanmahāmaṁḍalēśvara bāhattariniyōgadhipati ·anēkadēśādhipa • tu ssamaya
samuddhara[ṇa paścima]rāyadāmōdarasainyadhiśāpaṭṭa ·ēkāṁga[vi]-

samārādhanatatpara maṁḍalikabrahmarākṣasa gaṁḍape-

ṁḍāru gaṁgayyasāhiṇi svasti śrīśubhaśakavaruṣaṁbulu 1177 ·agu nēṁṭi rākṣasasaṁva
tsara śrāvaṇaśuddha 15 maṁgaḷavāramunāṁḍu sōma-

grahaṇanimittamuna gaṇapatidēvamahārājunakuṁ tamaku nabhyudayaparaṁpara gāvalayu
nani śrītripurāṁtaka śrīmahādēvarakuṁ dama

saṁdhya ·aṁgaraṁgabhōgālaku moṭṭavāḍilōni pulaceṟvu ·aneḍi·ūru catussīmamuṁ saka

mahāpātakānaṁ baḍuvāru || svadattāṁ para-

inscription.head:
No. 347.
(A.R. No. 210 of 1905.)
ON THE BASE OF THE WEST WALL OF THE DARK ROOM IN THE SAME TEMPLE.
Ś. 1177. (Rākshasa)
inscription.p:
States that Āruvaṅka Komara and Nāgu gave 30 cows for a perpetual lamp in
the temple of Tripurāntakēśvara.

l.te: ṁvatsara śrāvaṇa ba 14[sō] nāṇḍu śrī śrī tripurāṁtaka śrīmahādēvaraku ·āruvaṁka komara
·āruvaṁka peṁḍārpu cāṁgadēvanāyinikūṁtuṟu nāgu • vi tamataṇḍri cāngadē-
beṭṭina mōdālu 30 vīniṁ jēkoni nityamāneṁḍu neyi ·ācaṁdrā¨rkkamu naḍapaṁgalavāru ||

inscription.head:
No. 348.
(A.R. No. 745 of 1920.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE MAṆḌAPA OF THE TEMPLE OF SŌMĒŚVARA, JUTTIGA,
TANUKU TALUK, WEST GODAVARI DISTRICT.
Ś. 1177.
inscription.p:
States that Bhaṇḍāru Koṇḍa-Nāyaka and his son Vīrabhadra-Nāyaka built a
karavālabhairava-maṇḍapa in the temple of Vāsuki Ravi Sōmēśvara of Duttika and gave
some money also. The statement about payment of money is not clear. The recital
that the year was 1177th year of Vishṇuvardhana-Mahārāja is meaningless.

inscription.head:
No. 349.
(A.R. No. 700 of 1926.)
ON A STONE BUILT INTO THE SOUTH WALL OF THE TEMPLE OF RĀMĒŚVARA, ACHANTA,
NARSAPURAM TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1177.
inscription.p:
States that Rājemarāja Eṟṟa Lakshmīrāju, lord of Vēṅgi, and Pina Lakshmī-
rāju gave 25 inupa-yeḍlu for a perpetual lamp in the temple of Āsaṇṭa Rāmēśvara-Mahā-
dēva, that Vishṇuvardhana Vijayāditya gave his daughter Mailāradēvi in marriage
to Āsaṇṭa Sūraparāja and gave her, as dowry, a garden containing 500 arecanut trees
and that their son Pina Lakshmīrāju presented a garden containing 1,000 arecanut
trees to Āsaṇṭa Rāmēśvara-Mahādēva.

inscription.head:
No. 350.
(A.R. No. 211 of 1905.)
ON THE BASE OF THE WEST WALL OF THE DARK ROOM IN THE TEMPLE OF TRIPURĀNTAKĒŚVARA,
TRIPURANTAKAM, MARKAPUR TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
Ś. 1177. (Rākshasa)
inscription.p:
States that a certain Mahāpradhāni Ananta gave 100 cows for two perpetual lamps
in the temple of Tripurāntaka-Mahādēva.

l.te: svasti śrīśakavaruṣaṁbulu 1171 ·agu nēṁṭi rākṣasasaṁvatsara māgha ba 14 gu nāṁ-
ṭi śivarātripa¨rvanimityamuna śrītripurāṁta śrīmanmahādēvaraku svasti samastapra-
• • ḍu ·akhaṁḍadīpamulu reṁṭikiṁ beṭṭina mōdālu 100 vīniṁ jēkoni [meṟaka]

edition.p: Note:—The figure in line 1 should be 1177.

inscription.head:
No. 351.
(A.R. No. 535 of 1925.)
ON A PILLAR NEAR THE TEMPLE OF CHENNAKĒŚAVA AT KŌLAVENNU, BEZWADA TALUK,
KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Ś. 1177.
inscription.p:
States that general Pōtayaṁgāru and Āravelli Mārapōta Mañchiṁgāru
presented a perpetual lamp each to the temple of Prasanna Kēśava of Kōlavennu and
gave lands to certain persons on condition of their paying a certain number of
Gōkana-rūkalu for the maintenance of the lamps.

inscription.head:
No. 352.
(A.R. No. 538 of 1925.)
ON A PILLAR LYING NEAR THE SAME TEMPLE.
Ś. 1177.
inscription.p:
States that general Pedaya and a certain Maṁchi gave gifts of perpetual lamps to
god Prasanna Kēśava of Kōlavennu and that the former gave a piece of land to Kāṭe
Bōyuṇḍu on condition of his paying 24 Gōkana-rūkas (coins) annually for the upkeep
of his lamp.

inscription.head:
No. 353.
(A.R. No. 534 of 1925.)
ON A PILLAR NEAR THE SAME TEMPLE.
Ś. 1178.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. Seems to state that one Mārāma Brēka gave twelve cows for half a
lamp in the temple of Kēśavadēva of Kōlavennu.

inscription.head:
No. 354.
(A.R. No. 461 of 1915.)
ON A SLAB IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF BRAHMĒŚVARA, KOṆḌANĀYINIVARAM,
GANNAVARAM TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1179.
inscription.p:
States that king Udaya Manma gave 25 cows and a khaṇḍu of land for a perpetual
lamp in the temple of Brahmēśvara and also two martus of land for naivēdyam.

inscription.head:
No. 355.
(A.R. No. 203 of 1905.)
ON THE TOP OF THE DOORWAY OF THE WEST WALL OF THE KITCHEN IN THE TEMPLE
OF TRIPURĀNTAKĒŚVARA, TRIPURANTAKAM, MARKAPUR TALUK,
KURNOOL DISTRICT.
Ś. 1179. (Piṅgaḷa)
inscription.p:
States that Chāḷukya Nārāyaṇa Bhīmarāja Siddayadēva gave the village of
Keduṟēpalli on the Musi in Pūṅgināṇḍu to god Tripurāntaka-Mahādēva. Gives a
string of the donor's birudas. The donor seems to be a small local chief.

l.te: ttāṁ vā yō harēta vasuṁdharāṁ ṣaṣṭi va-

inscription.head:
No. 356.
(A.R. No. 320 of 1915.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE KALYĀṆA-MAṆḌAPA OF THE TEMPLE OF ŚAMBHUSVĀMI AT EḌAVALLI,
NARASARAOPET TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1179 (?). The cyclic year (Durmati) and the Śaka year do not tally.
inscription.p:
Partly illegible. States that Vēdagirirāja Tirumaladēvarāja gave a piece of
land to three Kāsis (masons) who built the maṇḍapa of the temple of Svayambhūdēva.

edition.l:
gaṁgōju gōpōju ·anamōjukunu vrittigānu peṭina cēnu • • •
(The rest is illegible.)

inscription.head:
No. 357.
(A.R. No. 464 of 1915.)
ON A PILLAR IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF BRAHMĒŚVARA, KOṆḌANĀYANIVARAM, GANNAVARAM
TALUK, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Ś. 1180.
inscription.p:
States that one Muppi Seṭṭi gave 25 cows and 7 tūms of land for a perpetual
lamp in the temple of Brahmēśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 358.
(A.R. No. 391 of 1915.)
ON A PILLAR OF THE KALYĀṆA-MAṆḌAPA IN THE TEMPLE OF MŪLASTHĀNĒŚVARA,
NĀDIṆḌLA, NARASARAOPET TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1180.
inscription.p:
States that Kōṭa Gaṇapatidēva, son of Bayyaladēvi assigned to the temple of
Mūlasthāna-Mahādēva two kēsar ivīsas (coins) on each māḍa realized by the sale in
Nādiṇḍla of Baṇḍibānisa (?) horses and other livestock.

inscription.head:
No. 359.
(A.R. No. 215 of 1905.)
ON THE BASE OF THE WEST WALL OF THE DARK ROOM IN THE TEMPLE OF TRIPURĀNTAKĒŚVARA,
TRIPURANTAKAM, MARKAPUR TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
Ś. 1180. (Kālayukti)
inscription.p:
States that a certain Mollala Annaparaḍḍi gave 25 cows for a perpetual lamp
in the temple of Tripurāntaka-Mahādēva.

l.te:
svasti śrīśubhaśakavaruṣaṁbulu 1180 ·agu nēṁṭi kālayuktisaṁvatsara nijakā¨rttika śu 15
maṁ nāṁṭṭi sōmagrahaṇanimittamuna śrītripurāṁttaka śrīmanmahā-

dēvara ·akhaṁḍadīpānaku mollala ·annaparaḍḍi tamatalli[ke] • sānikiṁ damataṁḍri prōliraḍḍikiṁ
buṇyamugā niccina mōdālu 25 gu[ṟṟa?]mu 1•

inscription.head:
No. 360.
(A.R. No. 740 of 1920.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE TEMPLE OF SŌMĒŚVARA, JUTTIGA, TANUKU TALUK,
WEST GODAVARI DISTRICT.
Ś. 1181.
inscription.p:
States that Vishṇu (?) the minister of Vīrabhadrēśvara of the Chālukya-vaṁśa
who married Kākatīya Gaṇapatidēva's daughter Rudrama-Mahādēvi presented a
perpetual lamp to the temple of Sōmēśvara of Ḍuttika. The inscription is incomplete.

inscription.head:
No. 361.
(A.R. No. 102 of 1917.)
ON A NANDI STONE SET UP NEAR THE TEMPLE OF RĀMALINGĒŚVARA, MANDAḌAM,
GUNTUR TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1181. (Siddhārthi)
inscription.p:
States that a certain Sūrapareḍḍi gave 25 cows for a perpetual lamp in the
temple of Rāmēśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 362.
(A.R. No. 217 of 1905.)
ON THE BASE OF THE WEST WALL OF THE DARK ROOM IN THE TEMPLE OF TRIPURĀNTAKĒŚVARA
TRIPURANTAKAM, MARKAPUR TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
Ś. 1181. (Siddhārthi)
inscription.p:
States that Allāḍa Pemmaya-Mahārāja who claims to be of the Pallava family
gave 25 cows for a perpetual lamp in the temple of Tripurāntaka-Mahādēva.

inscription.head:
No. 363.
(A.R. No. 309 of 1924.)
ON A SLAB SET UP TO THE RIGHT OF THE WESTERN ENTRANCE TO THE CENTRAL SHRINE OF THE
TEMPLE OF LAKSHMĪNARASIMHASVĀMI, VĒDĀDRI, NANDIGAMA TALUK,
KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Ś. 1181.
inscription.p:
States that Tyāgi Manma Gaṇapatidēvarāja gave the village of Vēmupalli
to god Pratāpanarasiṁha.

inscription.head:
No. 364.
(A.R. No. 733 of 1920.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE TEMPLE OF SŌMĒŚVARA, JUTTIGA, TANUKU TALUK,
WEST GODAVARI DISTRICT.
Ś. 1181.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. States that a certain Kommā-sāni and her sons Vīrapa-Nāyaka
and Induśēkhara-Nāyaka presented a perpetual lamp to the temple of Vāsuki Ravi
Sōmēśvara of Duttika and also gave some land for its upkeep.

inscription.head:
No. 365.
(A.R. No. 691 of 1920.)
ON A STONE USED AS A STEP IN THE TEMPLE OF RĀMALINGĒŚVARA, ZAMPANI,
TENALI TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1181.
inscription.p:
Damaged. States that a certain Nāgiseṭṭi gave a number of cows for a lamp in
the temple of Rāmīśvara of Sampani.

edition.l:
• • dālu
(The rest is damaged and illegible.)

inscription.head:
No. 366.
(A.R. No. 139 of 1913.)
ON A SLAB FROM DHARAṆIKŌṬA PRESERVED IN THE COLLECTOR'S OFFICE, GUNTUR.
Ś. 1 • • .
inscription.p: Incomplete and damaged. Mentions Gaṇapatidēva, the Koṭa chief.

inscription.head:
No. 367.
(A.R. No. 181 of 1905.)
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE MAṆḌAPA IN FRONT OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE IN THE
TEMPLE OF TRIPURĀNTAKĒŚVARA, TRIPURANTAKAM,
KURNOOL DISTRICT.
Ś. 1181. (61st year of Gaṇapatidēva) (Siddhārthi)
inscription.p:
States that Nātavāḍi Kumāra Gaṇapatidēva-Mahārāja gave 50 cows for a
perpetual lamp in the temple of Tripurāntaka-Mahādēva.

inscription.head:
No. 368.
(A.R. No. 220 of 1905.)
ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE DARK ROOM IN THE SAME TEMPLE.
Ś. 1181. (61st year of Gaṇapatidēva) (Siddhārthi)
inscription.p:
States that Kuṟapāṭi Sūra-Reḍḍi and his wife Eṟika-Sāni gave 25 cows for a
perpetual lamp in the temple of Tripurāntaka-Mahādēva.

No. 369.
(A.R. No. 213 of 1905.)
ON THE BASE OF THE WEST WALL OF THE SAME ROOM.
Ś. 1181. (61st year of Gaṇapatidēva) (Siddhārthi)
inscription.p:
States that Īndalūri Gannaya gave 25 cows for a perpetual lamp in the temple
of Tripurāntaka-Mahādēva.

inscription.head:
No. 370.
(A.R. No. 208 of 1905.)
ON THE WEST WALL OF THE SAME ROOM.
Ś. 1181.
inscription.p:
States that Gaṇḍapeṇḍāru Jannigadēva-Mahārāja, feudatory of Gaṇapati,
the Kākatīya king, gave the village of Gaṇḍrakōṭa-Anuṅgurājupalli in the Palināṇḍu
to god Tripurāntakadēva.

inscription.head:
No. 371.
(A.R. No. 196 of 1905.)
ON THE EAST WALL OF THE KITCHEN (VAṆṬA MIDDI) IN FRONT OF THE MAṆḌAPA
IN THE SAME TEMPLE.
Ś. 1182. (62nd year of Gaṇapatidēva) (Raudri)
inscription.p:
States that king Gaṇapatidēva gave the villages of • • • pāḍu on the north bank
of Guṇḍēṟu (Guṇḍlakamma) in Kammanāṇḍu and Rēḍumaḍapalli on the Musi in
Pūṅgi-nāṇḍu to god Tripurāntakadēva. States that Gaṇapatidēva belonged to the
Solar race.

inscription.head:
No. 372.
(A.R. No. 584 of 1925.)
ON A STONE LYING NEAR THE GĀLIGŌPURAM MAṆḌAPA OF THE TEMPLE OF
RĀMALINGĒŚVARA, VĒLPŪRU, SATTENEPALLI TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 118[2].
inscription.p: States that Agginēni Mārinēṇḍu sold his garden to Guṇḍaparaḍḍi Māṟeḍḍi.

inscription.head:
No. 373.
(A.R. No. 283 of 1924.)
ON A PILLAR LYING IN THE TEMPLE OF CHANDRAMAULĪŚVARA, ANUMANCHIPALLI,
NANDIGAMA TALUK, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Ś. 1182. (Raudri)
inscription.p:
States that a certain Brahmin Chāvali Bhāskara consecrated the image of
Chāgi-Gaṇapēśvara and that king Chāgi Manma endowed the temple with land. Des-
cribes the Chāgi family as of Kshatriya caste (bāhujakula) and gives the donor's
genealogy.

inscription.head:
No. 374.
(A.R. No. 530 of 1925.)
ON A PILLAR LYING NEAR THE TEMPLE OF CHENNAKĒŚAVASVĀMI, KŌLAVENNU,
BEZWADA TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Year unknown.
inscription.p: Damaged. Refers to a minister of king Gaṇapati.

inscription.head:
No. 375.
(A.R. No. 292 of 1924.)
ON A PILLAR LYING NEAR A WELL IN A FIELD TO THE WEST OF TĀḌUVĀYI, NANDIGAMA
TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
The year not given.
inscription.p:
Refers to gifts of land made by Kāṭreḍḍi for the merit of Gaṇapatidēva-Mahā-
rāja and by Kāṇyāyuṇḍu for the merit of Rudradēva-Mahārāja to the temple of
Mallikārjunadēva of Tāḍuvāya. Rudradēva is evidently Rudrama. Mention of both
sovereigns in the same inscription is significant.

inscription.head:
No. 376.
(A.R. No. 769 of 1922.)
ON A STONE BUILT INTO THE BACK WALL OF THE TEMPLE OF CHENNAKĒŚAVASVĀMI,
NĀYANIPALLI, BAPATLA TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Year unknown.
inscription.p:
States that, in the course of his conquest of the South, king Gaṇapatidēva pro-
tected the king of Nellūru having killed his enemies Paḍihāri Bayyana, Tikkana and
others, that he vanquished (?) Kulōttuṅga Rājēndra Chōḍa of Draviḷa maṇḍala, that
he received presents (?) of elephants from the king of Nellūru, that he saved the Bhṛiṅgi
maṭha on the Śrīparvata and that he consecrated the image of Kumāra Gaṇapēśvara-
mahādēva after his own name in • • palli. The concluding portion is missing.

edition.p: Note:—Some lines at the top and one at the bottom are lost.

inscription.head:
No. 377.
(A.R. No. 641 of 1920.)
ON A BLACK STONE LYING BEFORE THE TEMPLE OF VĒNUGŌPĀLASVĀMI, CHILUMŪRU,
TENALI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Year unknown.
inscription.p:
States that Viṭṭala-Nāyaka and Gōki-Nāyaka gave land to the temple of
Gōpālasvāmi and that the Ekkaṭi of Āthumūḍi also gave land to the same temple.
Mentions Kākatīya Gaṇapatidēva.

inscription.head:
No. 378.
(A.R. No. 321 of 1915.)
ON A STONE LYING NEAR THE TEMPLE OF KŌDAṆḌARĀMASVĀMI IN EḌAVALLI, NARASARAOPĒṬA
TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Year not given.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. Refers to a charitable grant made for the merit of Gaṇapatidēva-
Mahārāja.

inscription.head:
No. 379.
(A.R. No. 303 of 1915.)
ON A STONE NEAR THE CULVERT TO THE EAST OF THE VILLAGE, ROMPICHERLA, SAME
TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Year not given.
inscription.p:
Incomplete and damaged. Refers to grant of land to god Kēśavadēva of Koṇḍa-
muṭru for the merit of Gaṇapatidēva-Mahārāja. The donor appears to have been a
Kōṭa chief.

inscription.head:
No. 380.
(A.R. No. 313 of 1915.)
ON THE MIDDLE TIER OF THE SOUTH WALL OF THE BASEMENT OF THE TEMPLE OF
ŚAMBHUSVĀMI, EḌAVALLI, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Year not given.
inscription.p:
States that Vaṅkyarāja Kāmarāja gave land to the temple of Svayambhūdēva
for the merit of Kōṭa Kētarāja.

inscription.head:
No. 381.
(A.R. No. 142 of 1913.)
ON A SLAB BROUGHT FROM YANAMADALA AND PRESERVED IN THE COLLECTOR'S OFFICE,
GUNTUR, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 11 • 3.
inscription.p:
Incomplete and somewhat damaged. States that a certain Bēta(?)rāja founded
the temple of Gōpālasvāmi and endowed it with land, that Queen Gaṇapama gave it an
oil-mill and a garden and that certain merchants assigned to it certain customs duties
and taxes. Gaṇapama was probably the wife of the Kōṭa chief.

inscription.head:
No. 382.
(A.R. No. 767 of 1922.)
ON A STONE BUILT INTO THE EASTERN WALL OF THE TEMPLE OF VALLABHARĀYA, AT
SVARṆA, BAPATLA TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. Refers to a grant of land to a Śiva temple for the merit of Gaṇapati
dēva.

inscription.head:
No. 383.
(A.R. No. 263 of 1924.)
ON A BROKEN PILLAR NEAR THE IMAGE OF ĀNJANĒYA AT KANCHALA, NANDIGAMA
TALUK, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Year not given.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. Refers to certain gifts of land made to the temple of Indranātha-
Mahādēva by a subordinate of king Gaṇapati.

inscription.head:
No. 384.
(A.R. No. 326 of 1924.)
ON A BROKEN NĀGA PILLAR IN THE ŚIVA TEMPLE AT ZUZZŪRU, SAME TALUK
AND DISTRICT.
Date missing.
inscription.p: Incomplete. Refers to the sons-in-law of Chāgi Pōtarāja.

inscription.head:
No. 385.
(A.R. No. 258 of 1924.)
ON A BROKEN SLAB LYING IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF MALLIKĀRJUNA AT MUPPĀḶLA,
SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Year missing.
inscription.p:
Incomplete and damaged. Records the gift of 55 velleḍḷu (sheep ?) to god Bhīmeś-
vara by a servant of the Chāgi family.

inscription.head:
No. 386.
(A.R. No. 533 of 1925.)
ON A PILLAR NEAR THE TEMPLE OF CHENNAKĒŚAVASVĀMI AT KŌLAVENNU, BEZWADA
TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Date missing.
inscription.p:
Refers to perpetual lamps presented to the temple of Prasanna Kēśavadēva of
Kōlavennu by Meṇṭu-Maṁchi, Dontalapāṭi Pōtepeddi and general Annaya.

inscription.head:
No. 387.
(A.R. No. 643 of 1920.)
ON A STONE LYING IN FRONT OF A PRIVATE HOUSE AT CHUṆḌŪRU,
TENALI TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Date missing.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. Gives a list of land gifts made to certain persons presumably con-
nected with a temple.
(The beginning is lost.)

inscription.head:
No. 388.
(A.R. No. 282 of 1905.)
ON A NANDI PILLAR LYING BY THE SIDE OF THE STEPS ON THE HILL LEADING
TO THE TEMPLE OF TRIPURĀNTAKA, TRIPURANTAKAM, MARKAPUR TALUK,
KURNOOL DISTRICT.
Date missing.
inscription.p:
Incomplete and damaged. Refers to endowments of lands made to a maṭha and
a satra (choultry) established for the use of Śuddha Śaivas and to gifts of land made to
certain Brahmins.

inscription.head:
No. 389.
(A.R. No. 104 of 1917.)
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE SHRINE OF THE GODDESS IN THE TEMPLE OF
MALLIKĀRJUNA, MANDAPĀḌU, GUNTUR TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Date missing.
inscription.p:
The beginning is lost. The first nine lines of the remaining portion refer to a
certain Prōla, son of Vidda-Nāyaka and Vennama. The remaining lines contain
details of lands granted to certain temples and persons.
(The beginning is lost.)

inscription.head:
No. 390.
(A.R. No. 171 of 1917.)
ON A MUTILATED NANDI PILLAR LYING NEAR THE TEMPLE OF MŪLASTHĀNĒŚVARA,
TĀḌIKOṆḌA, SAME DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. Refers to certain gifts of land made by the grandson of Kōṭa
Vēnammadēvi.

edition.p: Note:—Between lines 8 and 9 some matter seems to have been lost.

inscription.head:
No. 391.
(A.R. No. 181 of 1917.)
ON A HERO-STONE LYING TO THE WEST OF VUNGUTŪRU, GUNTUR TALUK,
SAME DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p:
States that Kūna-Bōya, son of Baṇḍāruvu Chōḍa-Bōya, a soldier of Kōṭa
Bētarāja fought with Parvati Rēvaḍu at Gāralapāḍu and killed Bhīmarāja and
another and went to heaven.

inscription.head:
No. 392.
(A.R. No. 424 of 1926.)
ON A FRAGMENT OF A SLAB LYING BEHIND THE TEMPLE OF KṚISHṆA,
MUSALIMAḌUGU, NANDIKOTKURU TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
Year missing.
inscription.p: Incomplete, damaged and unintelligible.

inscription.head:
No. 393.
(A.R. No. 212 of 1905.)
ON THE SAME STONE AS No. 211 OF 1905.
Date missing.
inscription.p:
States that Brammadēva and Rāmadēva gave 11 cows and a heifer to the
temple of Tripurāntakadēva.

inscription.head:
No. 394.
(A.R. No. 214 of 1905.)
ON THE BASE OF THE WEST WALL OF THE DARK ROOM IN THE TEMPLE OF
TRIPURĀNTAKĒŚVARA, TRIPURANTAKAM, MARKAPUR TALUK,
SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1183 (Durmukhi.)
inscription.p:
States that Indulūri Gannaya, son of Gaṇapaya, gave 25 cows for a perpetual
lamp in the temple of Tripurāntakadēva.

edition.p: Note.— du¨rmukha seems to be ·an ·error for du¨rmati•

inscription.head:
No. 395.
(A.R. No. 94 of 1917.)
ON THE HUGE NANDI PILLAR LYING NEAR THE RUINED TEMPLE IN MALKĀPURAM,
GUNTUR TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
(Published in the Journal of the Andhra Historical Research Society,
Vol. IV, pp. 147-64.)
Ś. 1183. (Durmati)

Gives a detailed account of the Kākatīya family and of the foundation and
pontifical succession of the Gōlaki-maṭha of the Śaivas and states that king Gaṇapatidēva
promised the village of Mandara in the Velanāḍu-Kaṇḍravāṭi country to his guru
Viśvēśvara Śivāchārya and that Gaṇapati's daughter Rudramadēvi made a formal gift
of that village along with the village of Velaṅgapūṇḍi, that Viśvēśvara Śiva established
a new village with the name of Viśvēśvara-Gōlaki and peopled it with persons of different
castes brought from various parts of the country, that he also established the temple of
Viśvēśvara, a Sanskrit college, a maṭha for Śaivas, a choultry for feeding people without
distinction of caste and creed, a general and a maternity hospital, besides some other
things and that he made grants of land for the maintenance of all these institutions.
Gives a detailed description of the administration of the trust and of the village affairs.
Incidentally, it mentions a large number of other religious and charitable institutions
established by Viśvēśvara Śiva in several other places. Kākatīyas are described as
belonging to the Solar race of Kshatriyas.

inscription.head:
No. 396.
(A.R. No. 95 of 1917.)
AT THE FOOT OF THE FIRST SIDE OF THE SAME PILLAR.
Ś. Year not given.
inscription.p:
This inscription is in the nature of an appendix to the last one and contains a
list of land grants made to several persons connected with the temple of Viśvēśvara.

edition.p:
Note.—This inscription is in the nature of an appendix to the big Sanskrit inscription A.R. No. 94
of 1917 inscribed on the same pillar and must be read along with that inscription.

inscription.head:
No. 397.
(A.R. No. 460 of 1915.)
ON A PILLAR SET UP IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF BRAHMĒŚVARA, KOṆḌĀNĀYANIVARAM.
NUZVID TALUK, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Ś. 1183.
inscription.p:
States that Vīranārāyaṇa Chōḍa Baddigadēvarāja presented a perpetual lamp
to the temple of Brahmēśvara and also land for its maintenance.

inscription.head:
No. 398.
(A.R. No. 194 of 1905.)
ON THE EAST WALL OF THE MAṆḌAPA RIGHT OF THE ENTRANCE IN THE TEMPLE OF
TRIPURĀNTAKĒŚVARA, TRIPURĀNTAKAM, MĀRKAPUR TALUK,
KURNOOL DISTRICT.
Ś. 11[8]3. (Durmati)
(2nd year of Rudradēva-Mahārāja)
inscription.p:
Damaged. States that a general of Rudradēva-Mahārāja (the name of the
general is missing) gave 50 cows for a perpetual lamp in the temple of Tripurāntakadēva.

l.te: kavaṁdatāṁḍava gōdāvarītīra samaragāṁḍiva bhaṁḍanabhīma bhuvanābhirāma maṁḍalikabhaya-

inscription.head:
No. 399.
(A.R. No. 103 of 1917.)
ON A NANDI STONE SET UP BEHIND THE TEMPLE OF RĀMALINGASVĀMI,
MANDAḌAM, GUNTUR TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1184. (Dundubhi)
inscription.p: Incomplete. Refers to gift of land by Kōṭa Gaṇapatidēvarāja to Rājagurudēva.

inscription.head:
No. 400.
(A.R. No. 272 of 1905.)
ON A SLAB BUILT INTO THE SOUTH WALL OF THE SHRINE OF RĀMADĒVA
IN THE PRĀKĀRA OF THE TEMPLE OF TRIPURĀNTAKĒŚVARA,
TRIPURĀNTAKAM, MĀRKAPUR TALUK,
KURNOOL DISTRICT.
Ś. 118[5]. (Rudhirōdgāri)
inscription.p:
States that Karumāṇikka Perumāḍi-Nāyaka gave lands in the village of
Abhinava-Gaṇapāvuram to god Tripurāntakadēva.

inscription.head:
No. 401.
(A.R. No. 565 of 1925.)
ON A STONE BUILT INTO THE PARAPET WALL OF THE TEMPLE OF RĀMALINGĒŚVARA,
VĒLPŪRU, SATTENAPALLI TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1186. (Raktākshi)
inscription.p:
Damaged and incomplete. Refers to some gifts of land made during the reign of
Kākatīya Rudradēva-Mahārāja.

inscription.head:
No. 402.
(A.R. No. 550 of 1909.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE MAṆḌAPA IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF SŪRĒŚVARA, AT
KĀREMPŪḌI, PALNAD TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1186. (Raktākshi)
inscription.p:
States that Gaṇḍapeṇḍāru Meyidēvarāja, agent of Jannigadēva-Mahārāja
who ruled the country from Pānuṅgallu to Kaivāramu-kōṭa, made a gift of land to
god Sūrēśvaradēva. The concluding portion says that Karaṇam Annipeddi built the
compound wall and gōpura of the temple.

l.te: svadattaṁ paradattaṁ vā yō harēti vasuṁddhara |

yesya yesya yadā bhūmi stasya tasya tadā
phalaṁ ||

inscription.head:
No. 403.
(A.R. No. 537 of 1913.)
ON A PILLAR LYING IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF GŌPĀLASVĀMI, ĪPŪRU
VINUKOṆḌA TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1187. (Raktākshi)
inscription.p:
States that Goṅkaya, son of Sūrepeggaḍa gave a number of goats for a perpetual
lamp in the temple of Allāḍanātha of Īpūru, for the merit of Kākatīya Rudradēva-
Mahārāja.

edition.p: Note.—Raktākshi corresponds to Ś. 1186.

inscription.head:
No. 404.
(A.R. No. 309 of 1915.)
ON A STONE LYING IN A FIELD TO THE EAST OF SANTAMĀGULŪRU,
NARASARAOPETA TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1187. (Krōdhana)
inscription.p:
Damaged. Refers to a gift of land to the Arusa-maṭha of the temple of
Mallināthadēva on the Śrīparvata hill.

inscription.head:
No. 405.
(A.R. No. 164 of 1917.)
ON A SLAB IN THE TEMPLE OF ŚIVA AT SĒKŪRU, GUNTUR TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1187. (Krōdhana)
inscription.p:
Refers to grant of land made to the temple of Sōmēśvara-Mahādēva of Chēkūru
for the merit of Rudrayya.

inscription.head:
No. 406.
(A.R. No. 299 of 1915.)
ON A BROKEN GARUDA-STAMBHA IN THE TEMPLE OF VĒṆUGŌPĀLASVĀMI, ROMPICHERLA
NARASARAOPETA TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1187.
inscription.p:
Refers to the establishment of the temple of Gōpinātha in Ṟompicheṟla by
Vallabha-mantri, minister of Kōṭa Bhīmarāja, and gifts of land made to it.

edition.p:
Note.—The first sheet of the impression of the inscription is not found. The reading of the original
transcript is adopted.

inscription.head:
No. 407.
(A.R. No. 207 of 1905.)
ON THE WEST WALL OF THE DARK ROOM (CHĪKAṬIMIDDI) IN THE TEMPLE OF
TRIPURĀNTAKĒŚVARA, TRIPURĀNTAKAM, MARKAPUR TALUK,
KURNOOL DISTRICT.
Ś. 1188. (Kshaya)
inscription.p:
States that Tammirāju, the minister of Pedda Mallayya Preggaḍa, who was
himself the minister of Kākatīya Gaṇapatidēva gave 25 cows for a perpetual lamp in
the temple of Tripurāntakadēva for the merit of his son Tripurāri.

tu ta[ṁ]mirāju tamapriyaputruṁḍu śrītripurāriki sa¨rvā¨rtthasādhaka mai-

inscription.head:
No. 408.
(A.R. No. 172 of 1917.)
ON A NANDI PILLAR IN THE TEMPLE OF MŪLASTHĀNĒŚVARA, TĀḌIKOṆḌA,
GUNTUR TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 118[8.]
inscription.p:
States that the chief Manma Pōta consecrated the image of Kāmēśvara and that
his queen Paṇḍāmbikā made gift of land to Kāmēśvara and Manma-Pōtīśvara and also
endowed perpetual lamps in these temples.

inscription.head:
No. 409.
(A.R. No. 642 of 1920.)
ON A STONE LYING IN FRONT OF A PRIVATE HOUSE AT CHUṆḌŪRU,
TENALI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 11[8]8.
inscription.p:
States that Pottepi Kā[ma]-Chōḍarāja consecrated the image of Chōḍa-Ballīś-
vara in the name of his younger brother and built a temple over it, and that Manma
Gaṇapaya-Nāyaka made a gift of land to that temple.

edition.p:
Note:—Lines 19 to 24 seem to constitute a separate inscription. The first incription stops sudden-
ly in line 18.

inscription.head:
No. 410.
(A.R. No. 607 (b) of 1909.)
ON A PILLAR LYING IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF KŌDAṆḌARĀMASVĀMI, MŌṬṬUPALLI,
BAPATLA TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
[Below A.R. No. 607 (a)]
Ś. 1188. (Kshaya)
inscription.p:
States that Mōṭṭupalli Golla-Manda gave 25 cows for a perpetual lamp in the
temple of Prasanna Chennakēśava of Mōṭṭupalli and that a piece of land was given to
the cowherd who was to feed those cows and supply ghee for the lamp.

inscription.head:
No. 411.
(A.R. No. 582 of 1925.)
ON A STONE LYING NEAR THE GĀLIGŌPURA-MAṆḌAPA OF RĀMALINGĒŚVARA,
VĒLPŪRU, SATTENEPALLI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. [11]88.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. Refers to a gift made for the merit of Kōṭa Mummaḍi-Nandarāja,
[Oda]rāja and Gaṇḍaparāja.

inscription.head:
No. 412.
(A.R. No. 378 of 1915.)
ON A BROKEN PILLAR IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF CHENNAKĒŚVASVĀMI,
KŌLALAPŪḌI, NARASARAOPETA TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. [11]88. (Expired Prabhava)
inscription.p:
States that a certain Vallabharāja consecrated the image of Komāra-Gōpinātha
in Kōnalapūṇḍi in Kammanāḍu for the merit of Peda Kommarāja and endowed the
image with lands.

inscription.head:
No. 413.
(A.R. No. 401 of 1915.)
ON TWO STONES BUILT INTO THE PŌTURĀZU SHRINE, KANUPARRU, NARASARAOPETA TALUK, SAME
DISTRICT.
Ś. 1189.
inscription.p:
States that Māramarāja and some others assigned the duties payable on sales, to
the temple of Gōpinātha for the merit of Pratāpa-Rudradēva.

edition.p: Note.—Lines 10 to 14 are in smaller letters than the rest of the inscription.

inscription.head:
No. 414.
(A.R. No. 247 of 1905.)
ON THE NORTH WALL LEFT OF THE ENTRANCE OF THE DARK ROOM IN THE TEMPLE OF
TRIPURĀNTAKĒŚVARA, TRIPURĀNTAKAM, MARKAPUR TALUK,
KURNOOL DISTRICT.
Ś. 1189. (Prabhava)
inscription.p:
States that a certain Nārepa gave 13 cows and a bullock for a perpetual lamp in
the temple of Tripurāntakadēva.

inscription.head:
No. 415.
(A.R. No. 175 of 1905.)
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE IN THE SAME TEMPLE.
Ś. 1189. (Prabhava)
inscription.p:
States that Siddayadēva-Chōḍa-Mahārāja gave 50 cows for a perpetual lamp in
the temple of Tripurāntaka-Mahādēva for the merit of his father Perumāḍidēva Chōḍa-
Mahārāja and mother Dāmaladēvi.

l.te: māḍida(dē)vacōḍa mahārājukuṁ tama talli dāmaladēvikiṁ buṇyamugānu

inscription.head:
No. 416.
(A.R. No. 289 of 1922.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE MAṆḌAPA OF THE TEMPLE OF MUKTĪŚVARA, SŪRAVARAM,
GANNAVARAM TALUK, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Ś. 1189.
inscription.p:
States that a certain Mallebōya gave 25 cows for a perpetual lamp in the temple
of Brahmēśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 417.
(A.R. No. 190 of 1905.)
ON THE WEST WALL OF THE MAṆḌAPA LEFT OF THE ENTRANCE TO THE TEMPLE OF
TRIPURĀNTAKĒŚVARA, TRIPURĀNTAKAM, MARKAPUR TALUK,
KURNOOL DISTRICT.
Ś. 1[1]89. (Prabhava)
inscription.p:
States that Ēṟuva Manumilidēvarāja gave 25 cows for a perpetual lamp in the
temple of Tripurāntaka-Mahādēva.

inscription.head:
No. 418.
(A.R. No. 189 of 1905.)
ON THE WEST WALL OF THE SAME MAṆḌAPA.
inscription.p: Śaka year is lost but may be 1189 (See A.R. No. 190 of 1905, above.)

Incomplete and damaged. Refers to an endowment for a perpetual lamp in the
temple of Tripurāntaka-Mahādēva. The name of the donor is lost.

inscription.head:
No. 419.
(A.R. No. 828 of 1922.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE GARBHAGRIHA OF THE TEMPLE OF MALLĒŚVARA AT VARAGĀNI,
BAPATLA TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1189. (Prabhava)
inscription.p:
States that duties payable on sales and purchases made in Origāṇḍi are assigned
to the temple of Prasanna-Chenna-Mallēśvara of the same village.

inscription.head:
No. 420.
(A.R. No. 259 of 1924.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE RUINED TEMPLE OF ĀNJANĒYASVĀMI, MUNAGĀLAPALLI,
NANDIGAMA TALUK, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Ś. 1190 (Vibhava).
inscription.p:
States that Manuma Chāgirāja, son of Bhīmarāja, made gifts of land to god
Bhīmēśvara of Munabalapalli.

inscription.head:
No. 421.
(A.R. No. 314 of 1924.)
ON A NĀGA PILLAR LYING NEAR A DILAPIDATED MOSQUE AMONG THE RUINS OF THE FORT
AT GUḌIMEṬṬA, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Ś. 1190. (Vibhava.)
inscription.p:
States that Dāḍi Ganna-Nāyaka made a gift of land to god Viśvanātha of
Guḍimeṭṭa during the reign of Kākatīya Rudra-Mahārāja.

inscription.head:
No. 422.
(A.R. No. 573 of 1909.)
ON A SLAB IN THE TEMPLE OF GŌPĀLASVĀMI, DURGI, PALNAD TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1191. (Śukla).
inscription.p:
States that, while Rudrama-Mahādēvi, the paṭṭōddhati (?) of Gaṇapatidēva, was
ruling at Ōruṅgallu and her servant Gaṇḍapeṇḍāra Jannigadēvarāja was governing
the country from Pānuṅgallu to Mārjavāḍa, karaṇam Nāmaya consecrated the image
of Gōpinātha at Dugya in Pallināḍu and made grants of land and assigned certain
taxes for its worship.

inscription.head:
No. 423.
(A.R. No. 155 of 1917.)
ON A MUTILATED STONE LYING IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF VĪRABHADRASVĀMI AT
RĀYAPŪḌI, GUNTUR TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1191.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. Seems to refer to a grant of land to a temple at Rāvipūṇ[ḍi] by
Parvata-Nāyaka, the bodyguard (aṅgaraksha) of Kākatīya Rudradēva-Mahārāja.

inscription.head:
No. 424.
(A.R. No. 156 of 1917.)
ON A MUTILATED NANDI-PILLAR IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF SŌMĒŚVARA
IN THE SAME VILLAGE.
Ś. 1191.
inscription.p:
Damaged. Refers to a gift of land to god Sōmēśvara of Rāvipūṇ[ḍi] made by
Parvata-Nāyaka, the bodyguard of Kākatīya Rudradēva-Mahārāja.

inscription.head:
No. 425.
(A.R. No. 660 of 1920.)
ON A STONE BUILT INTO THE NORTH WALL OF THE TEMPLE OF CHENNAKĒŚAVA,
AT MŪLPŪRU, TENALI TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1192.
inscription.p:
Incomplete and damaged. Refers to the grant of land made to the temple of
Nīla-Kēśa[va] by Vallaya-Nāyaka, the guardian of the palace of Kākatīya Rudradēva.

l.te: maṭa ma • • • • ku peṭṭinadi nā0 || ka •

inscription.head:
No. 426.
(A.R. No. 222 of 1905.)
ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE DARK ROOM IN THE TEMPLE OF TRIPURĀNTAKĒŚVARA,
TRIPURANTAKAM, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
Ś. 1192. (Pramōdūta).
inscription.p:
States that Parichchhēda Vaḍamāni Kōṭadēvarāja gave 17 cows for a lamp in
the temple of Tripurāntakadēva.

inscription.head:
No. 427.
(A.R. No. 761 of 1922.)
ON THE NANDI-PILLAR PLANTED BEFORE THE TEMPLE OF VIŚVĒŚVARA, PEDDA GANJĀM,
BAPATLA TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1192. (Pramōdūta)
inscription.p:
States that a son of a certain seṭṭi, who was the son of Pinna-Seṭṭi, consecrated
the image of Pinnēśvaradēva at Pedda-Gaṁjāmu and made grants of a village
and customs duties for the worship of the image. The grants include certain salt pans
and the duties include aḍḍavaṭṭa-suṅkamu, and duty on salt sold in seven villages. There
was also a gift of 100 sheep for the upkeep of two perpetual lamps. The lands were given
free of taxes ari, appanamu, and pullari (grazing tax). The King was Kākatīya
Rudradēva-Mahārāja.

l.te: ṭṭa kommu māṟeḍḍi sēsi sagaṁ bā-

inscription.head:
No. 428.
(A.R. No. 246 of 1905.)
ON THE BASE OF THE NORTH WALL OF THE DARK ROOM IN THE TEMPLE OF TRIPURĀNTAKĒŚVARA,
TRIPURĀNTAKAM, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
Ś. 1192. (Pramōdūta).
inscription.p:
Damaged. States that a certain servant (leṅka) of Kākatīya Rudrayyadēva-
Mahārāja gave money for the upkeep of a perpetual lamp in the temple of Tripurāntaka-
Mahādēva.

l.te: [śu 15] gu nāṁṭi mahākā ¨rttika pa¨rvanimityamuna svasti śrītripurāntaśrīma-

inscription.head:
No. 429.
(A.R. No. 192 of 1905.)
ON THE EAST WALL OF THE MAṆḌAPA CLOSE TO THE BLOCKED UP DOORWAY
IN THE SAME TEMPLE.
Ś. 1192. (Pramōdūta.)
inscription.p:
States that certain merchants assigned to god Tripurāntaka-Mahādēva magama at
the rate of two [kan]ari-vīsas per māḍa on all goods sold in Tripurāntakam.

inscription.head:
No. 430.
(A.R. No. 193 of 1905.)
ON THE SAME WALL.
Pramādi (Ś. 1192 ?)
inscription.p:
States that the Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Parichchhēda Allāḍanāthadēvarāja and his
younger brother Bhīmarāja gave 25 cows for a perpetual lamp in the temple of
Tripurāntaka-Mahādēva for the merit of their father Gōkarāja.

inscription.head:
No. 431.
(A.R. No. 248 of 1905.)
ON THE DOOR POST OF THE DARK ROOM IN THE SAME TEMPLE.
Ś. 1194. (Āṅgīrasa)
inscription.p:
States that Gaṇḍapeṇḍāra Tripurāridēva-Mahārāja presented certain jewels
to god Tripurāntakadēva.

inscription.head:
No. 432.
(A.R. No. 168 of 1905.)
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE IN THE SAME TEMPLE.
Ś. 1194 (Expired.) (Śrimukha)
inscription.p:
States that Gaṇḍapeṇḍāra Ambadēva-Mahārāja gave lands (?) to certain
priests of the temple of Tripurāntakadēva.
