inscription.head:
No. 433.
(A.R. No. 536 of 1925.)
ON A STONE LYING NEAR THE TEMPLE OF CHENNAKĒŚAVA AT KŌLAVENNU,
BEZWADA TALUK, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Ś. 119[5].
inscription.p: Incomplete and damaged. Refers to the gift of a lamp to a certain temple.

inscription.head:
No. 434.
(A.R. No. 179 of 1917.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF RUDRĒŚVARA,
VAḌḌHĒŚVARAM, GUNTUR TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1196.
inscription.p: Incomplete and damaged. Mentions Kākatīya Rudra and Malli-Nāyaka.

l.te:
gu nēṁṭi vaiśākha śu 1 sō | (·utta)·uttarāyaṇa
saṁkrā • • • kā-

inscription.head:
No. 435.
(A.R. No. 801 of 1922.)
ON THE FOUR FACES OF A GARUḌA-PILLAR IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF SĪTĀRĀMASVĀMI,
GANIKAPŪḌI, BAPATLA TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1197. (Yuva)
inscription.p:
States that Goḍla Nārāyaṇadāsa and Goḍla-Gōpāladāsa made a gift of the
village of Ganakepāḍu to the temple of Bhāvanārāyaṇadēva of Gōshṭīvana with the
consent of all the learned men of the village. Gives also a list of several gifts of land
made by other persons to the same temple.

inscription.head:
No. 436.
(A.R. No. 532 of 1925.)
ON A PILLAR LYING NEAR THE TEMPLE OF CHENNAKĒŚAVASVĀMI AT KŌLAVENNU.
SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Ś. 1197.
inscription.p: Damaged and incomplete.

inscription.head:
No. 437.
(A.R. No. 137 of 1917.)
ON THE SAME STONE AS A.R. No. 136 OF 1917, POTTŪRU, GUNTUR TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 119[8].
inscription.p:
Damaged. States that a certain Māra-Bōya gave a number of sheep for a lamp
in the temple of Gōpāladēva for the merit of his master Parichchhēda Bhīmarāja.

inscription.head:
No. 438.
(A.R. No. 718 of 1920.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE TEMPLE OF KĒŚAVASVĀMI AT DUGGIRĀLA,
TENALI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1198. (Dhātu)
inscription.p:
Damaged. Refers to the gift of a flower garden. The gift seems to have been
formally made by the sons of a certain Kodama Siṁhamu with the consent of all the
villagers. The name of the village is lost. The dimensions of the several lines of the
trees are given in terms of arm's length (chētulu). Refers to the Kākatīya king Rudradēva-
Mahārāja.

inscription.head:
No. 439.
(A.R. No. 135 of 1917.)
ON A GARUḌA-PILLAR SET UP IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF VĒṆUGŌPĀLASVĀMI,
POTTŪRU, GUNTUR TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1199.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. States that a certain Eṟapōta presented a perpetual lamp to the
temple of Gōpāladēva of Pāttūru.

inscription.head:
No. 440.
(A.R. No. 609 of 1909.)
ON A PILLAR LYING IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF KŌDAṆḌARĀMASVĀMI
AT MŌṬṬUPALLI, BAPATLA TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1199. (Īśvara)
inscription.p:
Incomplete. Refers to the gift of a perpetual lamp to the temple of Prasanna-
Kēśava of Mōṭṭupalli by a shepherd of that village for the merit of Rudradēva-Mahā-
rāja.

inscription.head:
No. 441.
(A.R. No. 136 of 1917.)
ON THE GARUḌA-PILLAR IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF VĒṆUGŌPĀLASVĀMI,
POTTŪRU, GUNTUR TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1199.
inscription.p:
Somewhat damaged. Refers to gifts of cattle for perpetual lamps in the temple
of Gōpāladēva for the merit of Parichchhēda Bhīmarāja and Tam[mu] Bhimarāja.

inscription.head:
No. 442.
(A.R. No. 333 of 1915.)
ON A BROKEN NANDI-PILLAR SET UP IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF RĀMALINGASVĀMI,
RĀVIPĀḌU, NARASARAOPETA TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1199. (Expired)
inscription.p:
States that Sāmanta Pōti-Nāyaka consecrated the image of Sūrēśvara in
Rāvūru in the name of his father and for the merit of Kākatīya Rudradēva-Mahārāja,
built a three-peaked temple and a maṇḍapa for it and endowed it with land and that he also
presented a flower and fruit garden and a perpetual lamp to the temple. The inscribed
stone is said to have been put up by Pōtinēḍu's sons Pōtaya and Māraya. Pōtinēḍu
bears a long list of birudas among which are (1) an ornament of Durjayakula (2) the
elevator (vardhana) of the Valaūṭla-vaṁśa, and (3) a lion to the mad elephant, that is
Gajapati.

inscription.head:
No. 443.
(A.R. No. 539 of 1913.)
ON A SLAB SET UP OUTSIDE THE PRĀKĀRA OF THE TEMPLE OF VĪRABHADRASVĀMI
AT ĪPŪRU, VINUKONDA TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1200. (Bahudhānya)
inscription.p:
States that Bachchu Nārepaseṭṭi gave to god Allāḍanātha of Īpūru a gadyāṇa
per month for the merit of Rudradēva-Mahārāja.

inscription.head:
No. 444.
(A.R. No. 532 of 1913.)
ON A SLAB IN THE TEMPLE OF GŌPĀLASVĀMI, IN THE SAME VILLAGE.
Ś. 1200. (Bahudhānya)
inscription.p:
States that the sons of Bolnāyuḍu, the body-guard of Kākatīya Rudradēva-
Mahārāja, made gifts of land. The name of the donee is not given.

inscription.head:
No. 445.
(A.R. No. 533 of 1913.)
ON A PILLAR LYING IN FRONT OF THE SAME TEMPLE.
Ś. 1200. (Bahudhānya)
inscription.p:
States that Bachchu Nārepaseṭṭi gave a gadyāṇa per month to god Allāḍanātha
of Īpūru for the merit of Kākatīya Rudradēva-Mahārāja.

inscription.head:
No. 446.
(A.R. No. 101 of 1917.)
ON A NANDI STONE SET UP NEAR THE TEMPLE OF RĀMALINGASVĀMI AT MANDAḌAM,
GUNTUR TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1201. (Pramādi)
inscription.p:
States that a certain Amariseṭṭi gave 25 cows for a perpetual lamp in the temple
of Rāmīśvara of Mandaram in Doḍḍi Kaṇḍravāḍa.

inscription.head:
No. 447.
(A.R. No. 712 of 1920.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE TEMPLE OF ANANTABHŌGĒŚVARASVĀMI, KOLLŪRU,
TENALI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1201.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. Seems to state that a certain portion of the aḍḍavaṭṭa-suṅkamu realis-
ed in Kollūru was assigned to the temple of Narēndrīśvara of the same village for the
merit of Rudradēva-Mahārāja.

inscription.head:
No. 448.
(A.R. No. 406 of 1911.)
ON A BROKEN SLAB LYING AT THE RUINED MAIN ENTRANCE INTO THE TEMPLE OF
PARAŚURĀMĒŚVARA, ATTIRĀLA, RAJAMPETA TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
Ś. 120[1]. (Pramādi)
inscription.p:
Much damaged. Seems to refer to a gift of land to the temple of Paraśurāmēś-
vara of Aṟatuṟēvula for the merit of Ambadēva-Mahārāja. Gives also a list of other
charities established by the donor whose name is lost.

inscription.head:
No. 449.
(A.R. No. 148 of 1917.)
ON THE LAMP COLUMN SET UP IN THE TEMPLE OF VĒṆUGŌPĀLASVĀMI, PRATTIPĀḌU,
GUNTUR TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1202.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. States that Padmanābha, the minister of the chief Kannāra, set up
a garuḍa-stambha in front of the temple of Gōpinātha of Prattipāḍu and also gave land
to the same temple.

inscription.head:
No. 450.
(A.R. No. 804 of 1922.)
ON A PILLAR PLANTED IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF SAKALĒŚVARA AT NAṆḌŪRU,
BAPATLA TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1202. (Pramādi)
inscription.p:
States that Mahāpradhāni Gaṇapaddēva-Mahārāja made a gift of land to the
temples of Kaṇṭharīśvara and Daśamīśvara of Naṇḍūru.

inscription.head:
No. 451.
(A.R. No. 834 of 1922.)
ON THE NANDI PILLAR NEAR THE DHVAJASTAMBHA IN THE TEMPLE OF CHINMAYĒŚVARA
AT MALLAVŌLU, BANDAR TALUK, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Ś. 1202.
inscription.p:
States that Chānaya-Nāyaka, the body-guard of Pratāpa Rudradēva, made a
gift of land to god Chimmīśvara of Mallavrōlu. States that certain utensils were also
given to the temple. Pratāpa Rudra is said to be ruling (Sāmrājyadīkshitē).

inscription.head:
No. 452.
(A.R. No. 97 of 1917.)
AT THE BOTTOM OF FACE B OF THE HUGE NANDI-PILLAR LYING NEAR A RUINED TEMPLE,
MALKĀPURAM, GUNTUR TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1204. (Chitrabhānu)
inscription.p:
States that Mahādēva-Bhaṭṭōpādhyāya, son of [Modali]piḷa-Paṇḍita, gave 50
sheep and Īśana-Bōya 50 sheep for a perpetual lamp each in the temple of Viśvēśvara.
The latter made the gift for the merit of Kāśīśvara Śivāchārya.

edition.p:
Note.—The chronogram yugapakṣēṁdau is unintelligible. The correct Śaka year meant is
evidently 1204 which corresponds to Chitrabhānu.

inscription.head:
No. 453.
(A.R. No. 98 of 1917.)
ON FACE C OF THE SAME NANDI-PILLAR.
Ś. 1204. (Chitrabhānu)
inscription.p:
States that a certain Mallayapreggaḍa who was the officer in charge of the grain
depot (dhānyādhipa) of Viśvēśvara-Dēśika gave 50 sheep for a perpetual lamp in the
temple of Viśvanātha.

edition.p:
Note.—The chronogram yugapakṣacaṁdra means 124. This is evidently a mistake for 1204 which
corresponds to Chitrabhānu.

inscription.head:
No. 454.
(A.R. No. 96 of 1917.)
ON FACE C OF THE SAME NANDI-PILLAR.
Ś. 1204.
inscription.p:
States that Kāśīśvara Śivāchārya, son of Vidyāśiva and Sōmāmbikā gave 25
cows for a perpetual lamp in the temple of Viśvanātha.

inscription.head:
No. 455.
(A.R. No. 829 of 1922.)
ON A SLAB LYING IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF MALLĒŚVARA AT NĀGAṆḌLA,
BAPATLA TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1204. (Chitrabhānu)
inscription.p:
Damaged. Seems to state that certain suṅkams were assigned to a certain temple
for the merit of a Kākatīya king (name lost).

inscription.head:
No. 456.
(A.R. No. 99 of 1917.)
AT THE BOTTOM OF FACE C OF THE HUGE NANDI PILLAR LYING NEAR THE RUINED TEMPLE,
MALKĀPURAM, GUNTUR TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1204. (Chitrabhānu)
inscription.p:
States that a certain Kaniseṭṭi gave 25 cows for a perpetual lamp in the temple
of Viśvanāthadēva.

l.te: mu benuseṭlagōtru laina nāmiseṭikoḍku kaniseṭi tama tallidaṁḍrula-
·ipaṭṭu kaḷāmu vaṁgatōṭi dīpānaku nitya māneṁḍu nēti lekka

inscription.head:
No. 457.
(A.R. No. 206 of 1905.)
ON THE WEST WALL OF THE DARK ROOM (CHĪKAṬI-MIDDI) IN THE TEMPLE OF
TRIPURĀNTAKA, TRIPURĀNTAKAM, MARKAPUR TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
Ś. 1205. (Chitrabhānu)
inscription.p:
States that a certain Mante Vallabhuni Rāmaya gave a piece of land and also
some cows to the temple of Tripurāntakadēva.

inscription.head:
No. 458.
(A.R. No. 152 of 1917.)
ON A MUTILATED STONE IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF VĪRABHADRA AT RĀYAPŪḌI,
GUNTUR TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 120. .
inscription.p: States that a certain Amari Nēṇḍu gave a piece of land to a temple in Rāvipūṇḍi.

inscription.head:
No. 459.
(A.R. No. 130 of 1917.)
ON A PILLAR SET UP IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF MALLĒŚVARA AT PENUMĀKA,
SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Ś. 1210. (Sarvadhāri)
inscription.p:
Damaged. Refers to gifts of land made by several persons of Penumbāka, pre-
sumably to a temple, during the time when Paruvata-Nāyaka was governing the country
under the orders of Rudramahādēvi.

inscription.head:
No. 460.
(A.R. No. 242 of 1905.)
ON THE EAST WALL OF THE DARK ROOM (CHĪKAṬI-MIDDI) IN THE TEMPLE OF
TRIPURĀNTAKA, TRIPURĀNTAKAM, MARKAPUR TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
Ś. 1210 (Sarvadhāri)
inscription.p:
States that a certain Savadara Ambayareḍḍi gave 50 cows, one salaga bullock
and one breeding bull for a perpetual lamp in the temple of Tripurāntakadēva.

inscription.head:
No. 461.
(A.R. No. 153 of 1917.)
ON A MUTILATED PILLAR IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF VĪRABHADRA, RĀYAPŪḌI,
GUNTUR TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1211.
inscription.p:
Damaged and incomplete. Refers to certain gifts of land made presumably to a
temple. Mentions Rāvipūṁ[ḍi].

inscription.head:
No. 462.
(A.R. No. 536 of 1913.)
ON A PILLAR LYING IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF GŌPĀLASVĀMI AT
ĪPŪRU, VINUKONDA TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1211. (Virōdhi)
inscription.p:
Partly illegible. Refers to a gift of land made by Boggarapu Eṟṟaṅgēti-Nāyaka
to god Allāḍanātha of Īpūru for the merit of Rudradēva-Mahārāja and Bolnāyaka, the
latter presumably a dependent of the former.

inscription.head:
No. 463.
(A.R. No. 410 of 1911.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE COURT-YARD OF THE TEMPLE OF VARADARĀJASVĀMI,
PŌLI, RAJAMPET TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
Ś. 1212.
inscription.p:
Damaged and incomplete. Refers to Ambadēva-Mahārāja who was ruling with
Vallūru as his capital.

(Some lines seem to have been lost in
the beginning.)

inscription.head:
No. 464.
(A.R. No. 174 of 1905.)
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE IN THE TEMPLE OF TRIPURĀNTAKĒŚVARA,
TRIPURĀNTAKAM, MARKAPUR TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
Ś. 1212. (Vikṛiti)
inscription.p:
States that Gaṇḍapeṇḍāra Ambadēva-Mahārāja instituted a worship of
Tripurāntaka-Mahādēva between the early morning worship and the midday worship.

l.te: ka parabalasādhaka maṁḍalika brahmārākṣasa nāmādi samastapraśastisahi-

inscription.head:
No. 465.
(A.R. No. 268 of 1905.)
ON A SLAB SET UP TO THE NORTH OF THE DARK ROOM (CHĪKAṬI-MIDDI)
IN THE SAME TEMPLE.
Ś. 1212. (Vikṛiti)
inscription.p:
States that Gaṇḍapeṇḍāra Ambadēva-Mahārāja confirmed the grant of all the
villages and kānukas previously granted to the temple of Tripurāntakēśvara and made the
villages, tax-free. The chief belonged to the Kāyastha caste and his capital was Khaṇḍi-
kōṭa (Gaṇḍikōṭa ?). The inscription gives his genealogy and a long list of his exploits and
birudas. He is said to have cut off the heads of no less than 75 kings in battle, to have
vanquished, among others, king Gaṇapatidēva, Ēṟuva Mallidēva, Kēśava and Mallik-
ārjuna and to have re-established Manumagaṇḍa Gōpāla on the throne of Vikrama-
siṁhapura (Nellore).

It is further stated that the temple authorities granted the village of Gaṅgāvuram
to Nanda Śivulu in view of the fact that he was chiefly instrumental in obtaining
the regrant of the endowment.

inscription.head:
No. 466.
(A.R. No. 173 of 1905.)
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE IN THE SAME TEMPLE.
Ś. 1213. (Khara)
inscription.p: Incomplete. Mentions Ambadēva-Mahārāja and his subordinate Khadēva (?)

l.te: ·agu nēṁṭi kharasaṁvatsara nijāṣāḍha śu 15 bu | nāṁṭi [pa]-

inscription.head:
No. 467.
(A.R. No. 238 of 1905.)
ON THE EAST WALL OF THE DARK ROOM (CHĪKAṬI-MIDDI) IN THE SAME TEMPLE.
Ś. 1213. (Khara)
inscription.p:
States that Kaṇṭulūri Annaladēva gave a number of cows for a perpetual lamp in
the temple of Tripurāntaka-Mahādēva.

inscription.head:
No. 468.
(A.R. No. 318 of 1924.)
ON A PILLAR LYING NEAR A DILAPIDATED MOSQUE AMONG THE RUINS OF THE FORT AT
GUḌIMEṬṬA, NANDIGAMA TALUK, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Ś. 1213.
inscription.p:
States that Dāḍi Sōmaya-Sāhiṇi and Peddaya-Sāhiṇi gave lands to the temple
of Viśvanātha-Mahādēva who were the officers of Rudrarāja.

l.te: śrāvaṇa śu 15 ma • nāṁṭi rātri sōma

inscription.head:
No. 469.
(A.R. No. 548 of 1909.)
ON A SLAB IN THE TEMPLE OF BHĪMALINGĒŚVARA, JŪLAKALLU,
PALNAD TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1213. (Khara)
inscription.p:
States that Sōmayādula Rudradēva, general of all the forces of the king, gave
2 puṭṭis of land to god Mūlasthāna-Bhīmanātha of Juvulakallu, for the merit of
Kumāra Rudradēva-Mahārāja.

inscription.head:
No. 470.
(A.R. No. 174-A of 1905.)
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE IN THE TEMPLE OF TRIPURĀNTAKĒŚVARA,
TRIPURĀNTAKAM, MARKAPUR TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
Ś. 1213.
inscription.p: Incomplete. Seems to refer to chief Ambadēva.

edition.l:
• • • • • • 1213 ·a • • • •
(The rest of the inscription is lost.)

inscription.head:
No. 471.
(A.R. No. 183 of 1905.)
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE MAṆḌAPA IN FRONT OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE
IN THE SAME TEMPLE.
Ś. 1213. (Khara)
inscription.p:
States that general Mummaḍi-Nāyaka, son of Nāgi-Nāyaka of Rēcheṟla-kula,
granted 50 cows, 3 bulls and one lamp-stand to the temple of Tripurāntaka-Mahādēva.

inscription.head:
No. 472.
(A.R. No. 711 of 1926.)
ON A SLAB PLANTED IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF GŌPĀLASVĀMI, PINNALI,
PALNAD TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1214. (Nandana)
inscription.p:
States that Bollaya-Nāyaka chief of Maḍapalli on the southern bank of the
Gōdāvarī rebuilt the temple of Bhīmanātha of Piṅgali and endowed it with land. This
was done during the reign of Kākatīya Rudradēva-Mahārāja.

inscription.head:
No. 473.
(A.R. No. 180 of 1905.)
ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE IN THE TEMPLE OF TRIPURĀNTAKĒŚVARA,
TRIPURĀNTAKAM, MARKAPUR TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
Ś. 1214. (Nandana)
inscription.p:
States that several merchants jointly made a grant to the temple of Tripurāntaka-
Mahādēva of the duty amounting to a māna (of grain) per māḍa worth of grain sold in
certain villages and also on sale of grains such as rice, green gram and gingely in the
market of Tripurāntakam.

inscription.head:
No. 474.
(A.R. No. 170 of 1905.)
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE IN THE SAME TEMPLE.
Ś. 1214. (Nandana)
inscription.p:
States that Vikramōttuṅga Rājēndra-Chakravarti presented a gold four-faced
kōhaḷi to the temple of Tripurāntaka-Mahādēva.

inscription.head:
No. 475.
(A.R. No. 172 of 1905.)
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE IN THE SAME TEMPLE.
Ś. 1215. (Vijaya)
inscription.p:
Rather unintelligible. Refers to the construction of two tanks in the village of
Peda-Pulacheṟu belonging to the temple of Tripurāntakadēva.

inscription.head:
No. 476.
(A.R. No. 610 of 1909.)
ON A PILLAR LYING IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF KŌDAṆḌARĀMASVĀMI
AT MŌṬUPALLI, BAPATLA TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1215. (Expired) (Jaya).
inscription.p:
States that a certain Brammi Reḍḍi contributed 30 māḍas for the construction of
a stone maṇḍapa in the temple of [Che]nnakēśava-Perumāḷḷu.

l.te: • caitra śuddha | pa guru svasti śrī • •

inscription.head:
No. 477.
(A.R. No. 276 of 1924.)
ON A PILLAR SET UP TO THE SOUTH OF THE ŚIVA TEMPLE AT NAVĀBUPĒṬA,
NANDIGAMA TALUK, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Ś. 1216. (Jaya)
inscription.p: Refers to the gift of land to a certain temple.

inscription.head:
No. 478.
(A.R. No. 139 of 1917.)
ON A STONE LYING IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF VĒṆUGŌPĀLASVĀMI AT POTTŪRU,
GUNTUR TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
(The first portion of the inscription is very much damaged and illegible.)
Ś. 1216. (Jaya)
States that Malnēni Kēti-Nēḍu gave land to the temple of Sōmanāthadēva.

inscription.head:
No. 479.
(A.R. No. 179 of 1905.)
ON THE WEST WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE IN THE TEMPLE OF TRIPURĀNTAKĒŚVARA,
TRIPURĀNTAKAM, MARKAPUR TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
Ś. 1216. (Jaya)
inscription.p:
States that general Aḍidam Malu (Mallu ?) who was the right-hand man of
Rudradēva and the chief minister of Pratāpa Rudradēva consecrated the image of
goddess Trailōkya-Sundarī and celebrated the marriage of that goddess and Tripurāntaka-
Mahādēva and that the temple authorities undertook to celebrate the marriage festival
annually.

l.te: dabhaktu laina śrīmatu ·aḍidaṁmaluṁgāru svasti śrī śakavaruṣaṁbulu
1216 ·agu nēṁṭi jayasaṁvatsara phālguṇa śu 15 gu | nāṁḍu trailōkya-
sundari yaniyeḍi pēranu suvarṇapratima gānu gaurīdēvi viccēyinci śrī-

inscription.head:
No. 480.
(A.R. No. 45 of 1909.)
ON A SLAB IN THE COURTYARD OF THE TEMPLE OF TRIPURĀMBĀ IN THE BED OF THE TANK
AT THE SAME VILLAGE.
Ś. 1218. (Durmukhi)
inscription.p:
States that those engaged in the sale of salt, assigned to the temple of Tripurādēvi,
certain duties on sales for the merit of Kākatīya Pratāpa-Rudradēva, his general,
Rudradēva, and his chief minister Pōchirāju Peddirāju. The portion of the inscription
fixing the scale of duties assigned is not quite intelligible.

inscription.head:
No. 481.
(A.R. No. 533 of 1929.)
ON A SLAB BUILT INTO THE MAṆḌAPA IN FRONT OF THE VISHṆU TEMPLE AT MALLIPŪḌI,
TANUKU TALUK, WEST GODAVARI DISTRICT.
Ś. 1219.
inscription.p:
States that Pṛithivī-Vallabha Dēvakumāra, son of king Mahādēva, gave
50 cows for a perpetual lamp in the temple of Agastyēśvara. The donor bears the title
of the Eastern Chāḷukyas.

inscription.head:
No. 482.
(A.R. No. 570 of 1909.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE COURTYARD OF THE TEMPLE OF VANKĒŚVARA,
DURGI, PALNAD TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1219. (Hēviḷambi)
inscription.p:
States that Goṅgula Pōchi-Nāyaka, a dependent of General Rudradēva, made a
gift of land to the temple Vaṅkēśvaradēva of Dugya (Durgi). The general is said to be
ruling the land.

l.te:
khā nīrnēla rēvināyini ceṟvu venaka bāvi
·uttarā-

inscription.head:
No. 483.
(A.R. No. 572 of 1909.)
ON A SLAB IN THE TEMPLE OF GŌPĀLASVĀMI AT THE SAME VILLAGE.
Ś. 1219. (Hēviḷambi)
inscription.p:
States that Goṅgula Pōchi-Nāyaka and Perumāreḍḍi Pōtinēnigāru made
gifts of land to god Gōpinātha of Dugya while Rudradēva, commander of all the forces
of the king was governing the country.

l.te: rādhaku ṁḍaiva svasti śrīmatu gōṁgula pōcinā-

dāravēmula polamuna nīrnēla mō • vā
tikā-

edition.h4: At the bottom of the stone.

inscription.head:
No. 484.
(A.R. No. 841 of 1917.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE MUKHA-MAṆḌAPA OF THE TEMPLE OF RĀJĒŚVARA IN WEST VIPPAṞṞU,
TANUKU TALUK, WEST GODAVARI DISTRICT.
Ś. 1221.
inscription.p:
States that some one whose name is lost supplied a stone pillar to be put up in the
north-west corner of the maṇḍapa in the temple of Narēndrēśvara in Vippaṟṟu.

inscription.head:
No. 485.
(A.R. No. 842 of 1917.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAME MUKHA-MAṆḌAPA.
Ś. 1221.
inscription.p:
States that Pedapalli Sūrapa-Nīṇḍu supplied some of the pillars of the maṇḍapa
in the temple of Narēndrēśvara of Vippaṟṟu.

inscription.head:
No. 486.
(A.R. No. 843 of 1917.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAME MUKHA-MAṆḌAPA.
Ś. 1221.
inscription.p:
States that Goḍamalāla Gaṅgana supplied a stone pillar to be put up in the
south-east corner of the maṇḍapa in the temple of Narēndrēśvara of Vippaṟṟu.

inscription.head:
No. 487.
(A.R. No. 844 of 1917.)
ON THE SAME PILLAR.
Ś. 1221.
inscription.p:
States that Kāpaya Bhaktuḍu put up śākhas up to the east gate, in the maṇḍapa
of the temple of Narēndrēśvara of Vippaṟṟu.

inscription.head:
No. 488.
(A.R. No. 565 of 1909.)
ON A SLAB SET UP NEAR THE WELL CALLED DĒVATULABHĀVI, VOPPICHEṞLA,
PALNAD TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1221. (Vikāri)
inscription.p:
States that, while Pratāpa-Rudradēva was king and Gajasāhiṇi Guṇḍaya-
Nāyaka was governing the Gurindāla, Piṅgali and other provinces, the residents of
Vrappicheṟla instituted a charity for supplying free water to men and cattle in the
village and made a grant of land as endowment to the men employed in supplying the
water.

kaṟta vē·i gōvula dānamu sēsina phalamu vaḍayuduru

inscription.head:
No. 489.
(A.R. No. 296 of 1924.)
ON A SLAB LYING IN THE ZAMINDAR'S RESIDENCE, NAḌIGŪḌEM, NANDIGAMA TALUK,
KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Ś. 1222. (Śārvari)
inscription.p:
States that the residents of Tāḍuvāya assigned to the temple of Chenna-Mallikārjuna
of that village two vīsas in every māḍa of the income of the village, that the residents of
Nelamaṟṟi made a similar assignment to the same temple in regard to the income of
Nelamaṟṟi and that agasāla Dēvōja made a gift of a palmyra tope to the same temple.
The gifts were made for the merit of the Mahāsāmanta Māraya-Gaṇapaya-Raḍḍi
during the reign of Pratāpa-Rudradēva.

inscription.head:
No. 490.
(A.R. No. 177 of 1917.)
ON THE FRONT WALL OF THE TEMPLE OF RĀJAGŌPĀLASVĀMI, UNNAVA,
GUNTUR TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1[2]2[3].
inscription.p: Damaged. Refers to gift of land to the temple of Gōpinātha.

edition.p:
Note.—The date is doubtful. The year is adopted from the reading in the Annual Report on Epigraphy,
Madras.

inscription.head:
No. 491.
(A.R. No. 549 of 1909.)
ON A PILLAR OF THE MAṆḌAPA IN THE TEMPLE OF SŪRĒŚVARA, KĀREMPŪḌI,
PALNAD TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1225. (Śōbhakṛit)
inscription.p:
States that one Pōchū-Leṅka got some repairs executed to the temples of
Sūrēśvara and Kēśava-Perumāḷḷu of Kārempūṇḍi during the reign of Kākatīya
Pratāpa-Rudradēva-Mahārāja.

inscription.head:
No. 492.
(A.R. No. 173 of 1917.)
ON A NANDI PILLAR IN THE TEMPLE OF MŪLASTHĀNĒŚVARA, TĀḌIKOṆḌA,
GUNTUR TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1225. (Śōbhakṛit)
inscription.p:
States that, under the orders of Pratāpa-Rudradēva, a certain Gannaya remitted
all taxes (parapaṅgamu) on land belonging to the temple of Kāmēśvara of Tāṇḍikoṇḍa.

inscription.head:
No. 493.
(A.R. No. 561 of 1909.)
ON A PILLAR IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF DOḌḌAMALLĒŚVARA, CHINTAPALLI,
SATTENAPALLI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1226. (Krōdhi)
inscription.p:
States that Pōchaya-Nāyaka gave a puṭṭi of land to god Muktēśvara of Chinta-
palli for the merit of Pratāpa-Rudradēva.

inscription.head:
No. 494.
(A.R. No. 297 of 1924.)
ON A SLAB LYING IN THE ZAMINDAR'S RESIDENCE AT NAḌIGŪḌEM,
NANDIGAMA TALUK, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Ś. 1228. (Plavaṅga)
inscription.p:
Refers to a gift of land to god Chenna-Mallikārjunadēva during the governorship
of Cheṟaku-Jagadāḷu Annaya-Raḍḍi.

inscription.head:
No. 495.
(A.R. No. 387 of 1926.)
ON A SLAB LYING IN THE TEMPLE OF GAṆṬALA RĀMALINGASVĀMI, TAṄGEḌA,
PALNAD TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1230. (Kīlaka)
inscription.p:
States that, while Pratāpa-Rudradēva was ruling the earth and Dēvari-
Nāyaka was his governor at Taṅgeḍa, the eighteen assemblies (samaya) and the
merchants (?) of Taṅgeḍa, assigned to the temple of Rāmanātha, a duty on all sales in
the town at the rate of a vīsam per māḍa of the sale price.

l.te: lakasaṁvatsara caitra śuddha 10 sō | svasti śrī-

inscription.head:
No. 496.
(A.R. No. 616 of 1907.)
ON A SLAB NEAR THE MAIN EASTERN GŌPURA IN THE TEMPLE OF SAUMYANĀTHĒŚVARA,
NANDALŪR, RAJAMPET TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
Ś. 1231(?) (Kilaka)
inscription.p:
States that Pratāpa-Rudradēva remitted the suṅkamu, sādamu and avanāyā till
then levied in the five villages of Nelandalūru (Nandalūr), Āṇḍapūru, Mandaḍamu,
Mannūru and Astyāpuramu.

l.note: Kīlaka corresponds to Ś. 1230 and not to Ś. 1231.

inscription.head:
No. 497.
(A.R. No. 260 of 1905.)
ON A PILLAR SET UP TO THE EAST OF THE DARK ROOM IN THE TEMPLE OF TRIPURĀNTAKĒŚVARA,
TRIPURĀNTAKAM, MARKAPUR TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
Ś. 1230. (Kīlaka)
inscription.p:
States that Vollaya Reḍḍi, brother of Ellaya Reḍḍi the agent of Pratāpa-
Rudradēva, assigned to god Tripurāntakadēva, the income from the sale of goods of all
kinds (which may be conveyed to Tripurāntakamu) by Pedda-Māchana and Pina-
Māchana on their one hundred pack cattle.

inscription.head:
No. 498.
(A.R. No. 506 of 1906.)
ON THE RIGHT AND LEFT DOOR-POSTS OF THE ENTRANCE INTO THE MAṆḌAPA
IN THE TEMPLE OF BHAIRAVA AT MŌPŪRU, PULIVENDLA TALUK,
CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
Ś. 1231. (Saumya)
inscription.p:
States that Bhrammidēva Chōḷa-Mahārāja had a maṇḍapa and gateways (?)
constructed in the temple of Bhairava of Mrōpūru.

inscription.head:
No. 499.
(A.R. No. 679 of 1920.)
ON A STONE IN THE EASTERN GATEWAY OF THE GARBHAGRIHA OF THE TEMPLE OF
YŌGĀNANDA NRISIMHA AT YEḌLAPALLI, TENALI TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1233. (Virōdhikṛit)
inscription.p:
States that Rudradēva, son of Mayidēva-Leṅka, remitted the duties known as
upakshiti, paṅgamu, puṭṭi, māḍalu, kānika and dariśanamu on lands which may thereafter
be granted to god Chennakēśava of Penuṅguduru in the villages belonging to Koṇḍūri-
nāyaṅkara.

inscription.head:
No. 500.
(A.R. No. 564 of 1909.)
ON A SLAB NEAR THE WELL CALLED DĒVATULABHĀVI, OPPICHERLA,
PALNAD TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1233. (Virōdhikṛit)
inscription.p:
Partly illegible. Refers to a gift of land made presumably to a temple for the
merit of Guṇḍaya-Nāyuḍu and Māraya-Sāhiṇi while Māchaya-Sāhiṇi was governing
the Gurindāla, Piṅgali and other provinces as dependent of Pratāpa-Rudradēva.

Perhaps this Māraya-Sāhiṇi is identical with the person of that name to whom a
portion of Bhāskara-Rāmāyaṇam is dedicated.

edition.p: (Rest is illegible.)

inscription.head:
No. 501.
(A.R. No. 398 of 1926.)
ON A PILLAR PLANTED IN THE DOORWAY OF THE RUINED TEMPLE OF BHĪMALINGASVĀMI,
GUḌIPŪḌI, SATTENEPALLI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1234.
inscription.p:
Incomplete and partly unintelligible. Refers to certain gifts of land to Brahmins (?)
made by Rāyapaṭṭa-Sāhiṇi Dāḍi Vīraya-Nāyaka for the prosperity of Pratāpa-Rudra-
dēva.

inscription.head:
No. 502.
(A.R. No. 233 of 1905.)
ON THE EAST WALL OF THE DARK ROOM IN THE TEMPLE OF TRIPURĀNTAKĒŚVARA,
TRIPURĀNTAKAM, MARKAPUR TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
Ś. 1234. (Parīdhāvi)
inscription.p:
States that the worshippers and all other classes of servants attached to the temple
of Tripurāntakadēva entered into a convention agreeing to abide by the orders of the
temple Trustee (sthānapati). The king was Kākatīya Pratāpa-Rudradēva-Mahā-
rāja.

l.te:
virājyamu sēyucuṁḍaṁgānu parīdhāvisaṁvatsara bhādrapada ba 1 śu | śrītripurāṁtaka śrī

galavāramu ||

inscription.head:
No. 503.
(A.R. No. 27 of 1915.)
ON A PILLAR SET UP IN THE EASTERN COURT-YARD OF THE TEMPLE OF
MALLIKĀRJUNA AT ŚRĪŚAILAM, NANDIKOTKUR TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1234. (Parīdhāvi)
inscription.p:
States that Vēpēṭi Kāmayya, the chief minister of Pratāpa-Rudradēva, made a
grant for the midday worship of god Mallikārjuna of Śriparvata.

inscription.head:
No. 504.
(A.R. No. 36 of 1915.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SOUTHERN COURT-YARD OF THE SAME TEMPLE.
Ś. 1235. (Pramādīcha)
inscription.p:
Gives a list of villages which had been given to the temple of Mallikārjuna by
kings etc., from time to time and which were then in the enjoyment of the temple. The
list is said to have been settled at a conference of the disciples of the Śaiva faith held in
the maṇḍapa of the temple of Vīrabhadra.

inscription.head:
No. 505.
(A.R. No. 585 of 1909.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF PŌLĒRAMMA, MĀCHERLA,
PALNĀD TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 12[3]5. (Pramādīcha)
inscription.p:
Damaged. Refers to provision made for worship and naivēdyam in the temple of
goddess Gaṅgā-Paramēśvari of Mahādēvichaṟla, by Dēviri-Nāyaka who was the
governor of the place and son of Māchaya-Nāyaka who had the biruda of establisher of
the kingdom of the Kākatīyas (Kāketarājyasthāpanāchārya).

l.te:
vatsara caitra śu 11 śu | mahādēvicaṟla·abhyu
daya-

edition.p: Note.—Pramādi = Pramādīcha.

inscription.head:
No. 506.
(A.R. No. 328 of 1905.)
ON A BROKEN SLAB LYING IN THE MAṆḌAPA IN FRONT OF THE RUINED TEMPLE OF
ŚIVA CALLED MOBBUDĒVAḶAM NEAR UPPARAPALLE, PRODDATUR TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
Ś. 1235 (?). (Pramādīcha)
inscription.p:
Very much damaged. Seems to refer to the grant of certain village in Muliki-
nāḍu presumably to a temple for the merit of Pratāpa Rudradēva.

l.te: || śrōtraṁ yasya sabha ssamīranivahaśvāsō bhavē ttē-
rūpalasitō viṣṇu ściraṁ va ssuraḥ || tu • •

l.note:
The figures 1236 seem to be an error for 1235. The cyclic year pramādīca misspelt
as pramādi in the inscription, corresponds to the latter year and not to the former. [The date
Śaka 1236 cited in the inscription is correct, as it was the current Śaka year; there are many instances
where the current year is cited. Pramādin found in the record is the correct form of the name while
Pramādīcha, which is also current, is a corruption. The syllable cha added for the sake of metre being taken
with the name of the year.—N.L.R.]

inscription.head:
No. 507.
(A.R. No. 287 of 1924.)
ON A SLAB LYING UNDER A MARGOSA TREE IN FRONT OF THE KARANAM'S
HOUSE IN KOKKIRĒṆI, NANDIGAMA TALUK, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Ś. 1236. (Ānanda)
inscription.p:
Very much damaged. Seems to refer to the grant, presumably to a temple, of a
tax levied on people of several castes.

inscription.head:
No. 508.
(A.R. No. 298 of 1924.)
ON A PILLAR LYING IN THE ZAMINDAR'S RESIDENCE, NAḌIGŪḌEM,
SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Ś. 1236. (Ānanda)
inscription.p:
Incomplete. Refers to grant of land to god Mailāradēva of Tāḍivāya, by
Cheṟuku-Jagadāḷu Annama-Raḍḍi and others during the reign of Pratāpa-Rudradēva.

inscription.head:
No. 509.
(A.R. No. 131 of 1917.)
ON A BROKEN PILLAR IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF GŌKARṆĒŚVARA, PENUMŪLI,
GUNTUR TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1236. (Ānanda)
inscription.p:
States that Eṟṟaya-Leṅka, the gate-keeper of Pratāpa-Rudradēva, remitted
certain customs leviable on lands belonging to temples in 22 villages included in his
nāyaṅkara and also certain dues on lands enjoyed by the mahājanas in the same villages.
Kānika, gaḍḍuga, māḍa, puṭṭi-pahiṇḍi, puṭṭukalupu, upakṛiti, suṅkamu and grazing f are
among the dues remitted.

edition.p:
Note.—A and B are continuations of the main inscription. To their proper left are given the
actual lengths of the cubits in double line.

inscription.head:
No. 510.
(A.R. No. 753 of 1920.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE TEMPLE OF BHĪMĒŚVARA, MŌGALLU, BHIMAVARAM TALUK,
WEST GODAVARI DISTRICT.
Ś. 1237. (Rākshasa)
inscription.p:
Incomplete. States that Attili Peda-Pōti-Raḍḍi put up a stone pillar in the
nandi-maṇḍapa of the temple of Bhīmēśvara of Mōṅgolanu and also gave 5 puṭṭis of land
to Pramathakavi Sirigiri-Ayyaṅgāru.

inscription.head:
No. 511.
(A.R. No. 756 of 1920.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAME TEMPLE.
Ś. 1237. (Rākshasa)
inscription.p:
States that Attili Dēvaya-Raḍḍi put up a stone pillar in the nandi-maṇḍapa of the
temple of Bhīmēśvara of Mōṅgolanu and also presented a perpetual lamp to that temple.

inscription.head:
No. 512.
(A.R. No. 758 of 1920.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAME TEMPLE.
Ś. 1237. (Rākshasa)
inscription.p:
States that Namuṇḍūri Bēti-Reḍḍi's wife Kūnasāni put up a stone pillar in the
nandi-maṇḍapa of the temple of Bhīmīśvara of Mōṅgolanu.

inscription.head:
No. 513.
(A.R. No. 763 of 1920.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAME TEMPLE.
Ś. 1237. (Rākshasa)
inscription.p:
States that Attili Dēvaya-Raḍḍi put up a stone pillar in the nandi-maṇḍapa of
the temple of Bhīmēśvara of Mōṅgolanu and also presented a perpetual lamp to the
temple of Nandikēśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 514.
(A.R. No. 762 of 1920.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAME TEMPLE.
Ś. 1237. (Rākshasa)
inscription.p:
States that Koṇḍūri Kāṭama-Raḍḍi put up a stone pillar in the nandi-maṇḍapa
of the temple of Bhīmīśvara of Mōṅgolanu and also presented a lamp to the said temple.

inscription.head:
No. 515.
(A.R. No. 761 of 1920.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAME TEMPLE.
Ś. 1237. (Rākshasa)
inscription.p:
States that Namuṇḍūri Kūnasāni put up a stone pillar in the nandi-maṇḍapa of
the temple of Bhīmēśvara of Mōṅgolanu.

inscription.head:
No. 516.
(A.R. No. 760 of 1920.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAME TEMPLE.
Ś. 1237. (Rākshasa)
inscription.p:
States that Attili Peda-Pōti-Raḍḍi put up a stone pillar in the nandi-maṇḍapa
of the temple of Bhīmēśvara of Mōṅgolanu.

inscription.head:
No. 517.
(A.R. No. 757 of 1920.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAME TEMPLE.
Ś. 123[7]. (Rākshasa)
Incomplete.

No. 518.
(A.R. No. 759 of 1920.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAME TEMPLE.
Ś. 1237. (Rākshasa)
inscription.p:
States that Koṇḍaya put up a stone pillar in the nandi-maṇḍapa of the temple of
Bhīmēśvara of Mōṅgolanu.

inscription.head:
No. 519.
(A.R. No. 432 of 1911.)
ON A SLAB SET UP NEAR THE RUINED TEMPLE OF SIDDHĒŚVARA,
TANGUṬŪRU, RAJAMPET TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
Ś. 1237. (?) (Rākshasa)
inscription.p:
Damaged. Refers to the assignment of certain customs duties made to the temple
of Siddhanātha during the reign of Pratāpa-Rudradēva.

l.te: • • lunuṁ peddu peṟukaku mu • gānu·i • nu

inscription.head:
No. 520.
(A.R. No. 551 of 1909.)
ON A PILLAR OF THE MAṆḌAPA IN THE TEMPLE OF SŪRĒŚVARA, KĀREMPŪḌI,
PALNAD TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1238. (Anala)
inscription.p:
States that 8 puṭṭis of land was granted to the temple of Sūrēśvara of Kārem-
pūṇḍi free of the paṅga tax. Mentions Pratāpa-Rudradēva as the ruling king and also
one Ālavaṭṭam Peddaya-Leṅka.

edition.p:
Note.—The year 1239 seems to be ·a mistake for 1238 which corresponds to ·ānala. [The
Śaka year cited was current ·and not ·expired.—ṇ.ḷ.ṟ.]

inscription.head:
No. 521.
(A.R. No. 715 of 1920.)
ON A BROKEN RED STONE LYING IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF ĀNANDĒŚVARA,
PEDAKOṆḌŪRU, TENALI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1239. (Piṅgaḷa)
inscription.p:
States that, for the merit of Pratāpa-Rudradēva, Mayidēva-Leṅka remitted
the taxes known as gaḍḍuga, kānika, suṅkam leviable on sales, baṇṭela-āyamu and talāri-
pannu in all the 18 villages of the Koṇḍūru-sthalam.

inscription.head:
No. 522.
(A.R. No. 653 of 1920.)
ON A STONE SET UP IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF AGASTYĒŚVARASVĀMI OF
KOLAKALŪRU, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Ś. 1240. (Kālayukta)
inscription.p:
States that Pōchu-Leṅka, son of Sōmaya-Leṅka, commander-in-chief of all the
forces of Pratāpa-Rudradēva made a gift of 8 pandums of land to god Agastīśvara of
Kolaṁkulūru and of the same extent to god Vīrabhadradēva.

inscription.head:
No. 523.
(A.R. No. 652 of 1920.)
ON THE OUTER SOUTH WALL OF THE TEMPLE OF CHENNAKĒŚAVASVĀMI,
AT THE SAME VILLAGE.
Ś. 1240. (Kālayukti)
inscription.p:
States that Pōchu-Leṅka, son of Sōmaya-Leṅka, commander-in-chief of all the
forces of Pratāpa-Rudradēva presented 5 tūms of land to the temple of Kēśava-Peru-
māḷḷu of Kolaṁkulūru.

inscription.head:
No. 524.
(A.R. No. 690 of 1920.)
ON A STONE BUILT INTO THE WALL OF THE TEMPLE OF ĀNJANĒYASVĀMI,
ZAMPANI, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Ś. 1240. (Kālayukti)
inscription.p:
States that two Mādhava-dāsas gave 5 tūms of land for the upkeep of two sandhya
lamps in the temple of Gōpinātha.

inscription.head:
No. 525.
(A.R. No. 599 of 1909.)
ON A MUTILATED SLAB SET UP IN THE VILLAGE OF PIḌUGURĀḶḶA,
PALNAD TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. [1240.] (Kālayukta)
inscription.p:
Damaged and illegible. Seems to refer to Pratāpa-Rudradēva and a certain
Leṅka.

edition.l:
• • • gārisuputruṁ • • •
(The rest is much damaged and illegible.)

inscription.head:
No. 526.
(A.R. No. 574 of 1909.)
ON A PILLAR SET UP IN FRONT OF ŌṬIGUḶḶU NEAR BUGGA, DURGI,
SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Ś. 1241. (Siddhārthi)
inscription.p:
States that Dēvari-Nāyaka, son of Māchaya-Nāyaka, made a gift of land to the
temples of Rudrēśvara and Bollēśvara of Dāravēmula, that the mahājanas of Dāra-
vēmula granted a certain portion of their income (?) to the said temples and that Vennaya-
Reḍḍi and Avubhaḷanātha purchased the lands of certain Brahmins and gave them to
the temples during the reign of Kākatīya Pratāpa-Rudradēva-Mahārāja.

inscription.head:
No. 527.
(A.R. No. 545 of 1909.)
ON A SLAB IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF VĒṆUGŌPĀLASVĀMI, JONNALAGAḌḌA,
SATTENEPALLI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1241. (Siddhārthi)
inscription.p:
Damaged. Refers to gifts of land made by certain persons to a certain temple of
Jonnalagaḍḍa. This contains two records, the earlier being dated in Ś. 1214 and the
later in Ś. 1241.

l.te: • • • • malnēnikoḍku pōtaya • • • gāru peṭna kṣētramu yūri

inscription.head:
No. 528.
(A.R. No. 163 of 1913.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE COURTYARD OF THE TEMPLE OF VĪRANĀRĀYAṆASVĀMI
AT PĀṆEM, NANDYAL TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
Ś. 1241. (Siddhārthi)
inscription.p:
States that Mummaḍi Juṭṭaya-Leṅka, governor of Peḍakallu and other
Southern districts during the reign of Pratāpa-Rudradēva, made a gift of the fees
(mēras) payable by people of several classes to the temple of Vīranārāyaṇa at Pānyamu
and that he also ordered that a tūmu of grain should be given to the said temple in
respect of grains sold in the markets of certain specified villages.

inscription.head:
No. 529.
(A.R. No. 298 of 1915.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE TEMPLE OF VĒṆUGŌPĀLASVĀMI AT ṞOMPICHEṞLA,
NARASARAOPETA TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1242. (Raudri)
inscription.p:
States that Koṇḍa Peddi-Mañchi, a palace official of Pratāpa-Rudradēva made
a gift of some land to the temple of Ananta-Gōpinātha of Ṟompicheṟla.

inscription.head:
No. 530.
(A.R. No. 178 of 1905.)
ON THE WEST WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE IN THE TEMPLE OF TRIPURĀNTAKĒŚVARA,
TRIPURĀNTAKAM, MARKAPUR TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
Ś. 12[42] (Raudri)
inscription.p:
States that Nēmāni Pina-Vichaya-Pregaḍa made a gift of the suṅkam on a
garden to the temple of Tripurāntakadēva during the reign of Kākatīya Pratāpa-Rudra-
dēva-Mahārāja.

inscription.head:
No. 531.
(A.R. No. 755 of 1920.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE TEMPLE OF BHĪMĒŚVARASVĀMI, MĀGALLU, BHIMAVARAM TALUK,
WEST GODAVARI DISTRICT.
Ś. 1243 (Durmati)
inscription.p:
States that Mōṅgolani Dēvaya-Raḍḍi made a gift of land to the temple of
Bhīmēśvara of Mōṅgolanu.

l.te: vyasanidu na 5 verasi kha 1 na 2 sēnuṁ jēsi kuḍici mō-

inscription.head:
No. 532.
(A.R. No. 604 of 1909.)
ROUND THE BASE OF THE TEMPLE OF KŌDAṆḌARĀMASVĀMI AT MŌṬUPALLI,
BAPATLA TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 124[4] (Dundubhi)
inscription.p:
States that Golla Chūraya gave 20 sheep for a lamp in the temple of Gōpinātha
consecrated by Dharma Rāmaseṭṭi.

l.te:

inscription.head:
No. 533.
(A.R. No. 326 of 1915.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE KALYĀṆA-MAṆḌAPA OF THE TEMPLE OF KŌDAṆḌARĀMASVĀMI
AT KOPPARAM, NARASARAOPETA TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1245. (Rudhirōdgāri)
inscription.p:
States that the inhabitants of Kopparam agreed to give to the temple of Varada-
Gōpinātha and Rāma-Lakshmaṇa a portion of their incomes for the merit of Pratāpa-
Rudradēva.

inscription.head:
No. 534.
(A.R. No. 68 of 1917.)
ON A STONE SET UP NEAR AN OLD WELL AT CHINA KĀKANI,
GUNTUR TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1246 (Raktākshi)
inscription.p:
States that Krāṁja Nāgiseṭṭi and Vallabhiseṭṭi presented a flower and fruit
garden to the temple of Chenna-Mallikārjuna of Kākāṇḍi.

inscription.head:
No. 535.
(A.R. No. 308 of 1915.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN A FIELD TO THE WEST OF SANTAMĀGULŪRU,
NARASARAOPETA TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Kshaya.
inscription.p:
States that Kolani Rudradēvaniṅgāru, minister of Pratāpa-Rudradēva made
a gift of land to the temple of Gōpināthadēva.

l.te:
[svadattāṁ parada]ttāṁ vā yō harēti vasuṁ
dharāṁ ṣaṣṭi¨rvvaru-

inscription.head:
No. 536.
(A.R. No. 329 of 1905.)
ON A BROKEN SLAB LYING IN THE MAṆḌAPA IN FRONT OF THE RUINED ŚIVA TEMPLE
CALLED MOBBUDĒVALAM NEAR UPPARAPALLI, PRODDATUR TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p:
States that Pratāpa-Rudradēva appointed his son Juṭṭaya's servant (leṅka)
Gōṅkaya-Reḍḍi to govern Khaṇḍikōṭa (Gaṇḍikōṭa) and the Mulikināṇḍu country and
that, while so governing, the Reḍḍi gave away the village of Khaṇḍikōṭa (?) rent free to
the residents.

l.te: ni rājyābhiṣiktulaṁ jēśe || tasya rudramahīpasya ju-
kumā[ra]śśakta • raṁ sudhiḥ || rājani tasmin sa[ti]

inscription.head:
No. 537.
(A.R. No. 310 of 1915.)
ON A SLAB LYING IN A FIELD IN THE VILLAGE OF SANTAMĀGULŪRU,
NARASARAOPETA TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p:
States that Pratāpa-Rudradēva's minister [Rudra]dēva made a gift of land to
the temple of [Gō]pinātha of I[ska]palli.

edition.p: The rest is lost.
The rest is damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 538.
(A.R. No. 687 of 1920.)
ON A STONE BUILT INTO THE ROOF OF THE TEMPLE OF AGASTYĒŚVARA, ĪVANI,
TENALI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
The year is missing.
inscription.p:
States that Sōmidēva-Nāyaṅka made a gift of land to the temple of Agastīśvara
of Īvani for the merit of Pratāpa-Rudradēva.

l.note: Themat are inserted below the line.

inscription.head:
No. 539.
(A.R. No. 92 of 1917.)
ON A STONE SET UP IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF MALLĒŚVARASVĀMI, LĀM,
GUNTUR TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Beginning lost.
inscription.p:
Damaged. Refers to the gift of land made by a dependent of Rudradēva-Mahā-
rāja to the Sānis and Mānis of the temple of Bhīmēśvara at Lāmu.

inscription.head:
No. 540.
(A.R. No. 325 of 1915.)
ON A GARUḌA-STAMBHA IN THE TEMPLE OF VĒṆUGŌPĀLASVĀMI AT UPPUMĀGULŪRU,
NARASARAOPETA TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Year missing.
inscription.p:
States that, on his return from a successful campaign against the Pāṇḍya king,
Bolnēniṅgāru remitted tiruviḍayāvamulu (?) as paṅgamānyamu to god Chennakēśava of
Uppuṁbrālūru, for the merit of Pratāpa-Rudradēva.

inscription.head:
No. 541.
(A.R. No. 301 of 1924.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN FRONT OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE IN THE TEMPLE OF MUKTĒŚVARA,
MUKTYĀLA, NANDIGAMA TALUK, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p: Incomplete. Enumerates some of the charities of Chāgi Pōtayarāja.

inscription.head:
No. 542.
(A.R. No. 313 of 1924.)
ON A NĀGA PILLAR LYING NEAR A DILAPIDATED MOSQUE AMONG THE RUINS OF THE
FORT AT GUḌIMEṬṬA, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Year not given.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. States that Chāgi Pōtarāja, whose genealogy is given, installed god
Viśvēśvara in his capital Guḍimeṭṭa. Mentions Pōtarāja's queen Rājāmbā.

edition.p: The rest of this stone is broken.

inscription.head:
No. 543.
(A.R. No. 279 of 1924.)
ON A BROKEN NĀGA PILLAR IN THE HOUSE-SITE OF THE LATE MR. K.V. LAKSHMANA RAO
AT PENUGANCHIPRŌLU, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
The Year is missing.
inscription.p:
States that a certain minister of Dōrapa, son of Bhīmarāja of Nātavāḍi, esta-
blished a temple and made a gift of land to it.

inscription.head:
No. 544.
(A.R. No. 270 of 1924.)
ON A PILLAR SET UP IN THE TEMPLE OF ĀÑJANĒYASVĀMI AT KONAKANCHI,
SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p:
States that, while Pōtarāja was ruling the Nāthavāḍi country with Guḍimeṭla
as his capital, his kāmpu Bīrama's sons Kaśśevu-Seṭṭi and Kuṟivi-Seṭṭi and the latter's
wife Sūrama got the temple of Narēndrēśvara plastered, consecrated the images of
Nārāyaṇadēva and Brahmadēva and also got the temples of attendant gods plastered, and
gave two tanks for the naivēdya and Pātrapāguḍamu in these temples. Also states
that Kuṟṟi-Seṭṭi of the Teliki thousand tribe of Bejevāḍa presented two lamps to
the temples.

inscription.head:
No. 545.
(A.R. No. 589 of 1925.)
ON A STONE LYING NEAR THE TEMPLE OF RĀMALINGĒŚVARA AT VĒLPŪRU,
SATTENEPALLI TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
The year is missing.
inscription.p:
Incomplete and damaged. Mentions Prōla, the minister of Kōṭa Kētarāja of
Amarārāma.

inscription.head:
No. 546.
(A.R. No. 531 of 1925.)
ON A PILLAR LYING NEAR THE TEMPLE OF CHENNAKĒŚAVA AT KŌLAVENNU,
BEZWADA TALUK, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Date lost.
inscription.p: Damaged and unintelligible.

inscription.head:
No. 547.
(A.R. No. 340 of 1915.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF NṚISIṀHASVĀMI, NEKARIKALLU,
NARASARAOPETA TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1254 (Śrīmukha)
inscription.p:
Refers to the gift of eighty puṭṭis of land to god Śānta-Narasiṁha of Nagarakallu
and states that one Siṅgavibhu formed a village on that land and called it Nārasiṁhya-
puram.

inscription.head:
No. 548.
(A.R. No. 261 of 1905.)
ON A NANDI PILLAR IN FRONT OF THE DARK ROOM (CHĪKAṬI-MIDDI) IN THE TEMPLE OF
TRIPURĀNTAKĒŚVARA, TRIPURĀNTAKAM, MARKAPUR TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
Ś. 1[2]5[7](?) (Yuva)
inscription.p:
States that Pōtiraḍḍi Tallayaraḍḍi presented 100 cows and one bull to the
temple of Tripurāntaka-Mahādēva for the merit of his masters Ambadēvarāja and
Lōkināyaka, sons of Chelināyani Komma-Nāyaka, lord of Rēvanūru. A long string
of Komma-Nāyaka's birudas is given.

inscription.head:
No. 549.
(A.R. No. 708 of 1926.)
ON A SLAB NEAR THE DHVAJASTAMBHA IN THE TEMPLE OF RĀMALINGĒŚVARA AT
PINNALI, PALNAD TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 12[63] (?) Vṛisha
inscription.p:
States that Allāḍa-Nāyaka, son of Sabbi-Nāyaka, put up a golden pot on the top
of the temple of Śrī-Rāmanāthadēva of Piṅgali.

inscription.head:
No. 550.
(A.R. No. 230 of 1905.)
ON THE EAST WALL OF THE DARK ROOM IN THE TEMPLE OF TRIPURĀNTAKĒŚVARA,
TRIPURĀNTAKAM, MARKAPUR TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
Ś. 1264 (?) (Chitrabhānu)
inscription.p:
Incomplete. States that a certain Pōtāmbā put up a golden dhvaja in the
temple of Tripurāntakēśvara. From the genealogy given which is not quite intelligible,
it appears that Pōtāmbā was the wife of Sōma-Bhūpāla of the lunar race and Ātrēya-
gōtra and the daughter of Rāma-Bhūpāla, son of Goṅkaṇṇa, who was the son of Peda
Rāma-Bhūpāla, who was the son of Goṅkaṇṇadēva of the Kāśyapa-gōtra, who had the
title of Uttamagaṇḍa.

inscription.head:
No. 551.
(A.R. No. 191 of 1905.)
ON THE EAST WALL OF THE MAṆḌAPA CLOSE TO THE BLOOKED UP DOORWAY
IN THE SAME TEMPLE.
Ś. 1268 (Vyaya)
inscription.p: Refers to Vēmaya-Reḍḍi and Anavōtaya-Reḍḍi.

inscription.head:
No. 552.
(A.R. No. 250 of 1905.)
ON A PILLAR AT THE ENTRANCE INTO THE DARK ROOM (CHĪKAṬI-MIDDI)
IN THE SAME TEMPLE.
Ś. 1269 (Sarvajit)
inscription.p:
States that Iṟuvaprali Paḍālu Immaḍi made a gift of one hundred cows and one
bullock to god Tripurāntakadēva and fifty cows and a bullock to goddess Tripurāpara-
mēśvari for supplying ghee for perpetual lamps, for the merit of Annama-Reḍḍiṅgāru.

inscription.head:
No. 553.
(A.R. No. 184 of 1905.)
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE MAṆḌAPA IN FRONT OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE
IN THE SAME TEMPLE.
Ś. 1275.
inscription.p:
States that Māraya-Nāyaka made a gift of the village of Namilikallu in
Dannālakōṭa (Dharaṇikōṭa)-sthalamu to Tripurāntaka-Mahādēva for the merit of
Kāmi-Nāyaka.

inscription.head:
No. 554.
(A.R. No. 44 of 1912.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE COMPOUND OF THE TEMPLE OF LAKSHMĪNARASIṀHASVĀMI
ON THE HILL AT KŌRUKOṆḌA, RAJAHMUNDRY TALUK,
EAST GODAVARI DISTRICT.
Ś. 1275 (Vijaya)
inscription.p:
States that Bhaṭṭari, the Vaishṇava guru of Mummaḍi-Nāyaka of Kōrukoṇḍa
left his mortal body and reappeared as god Narasiṁha on the top of the Parāśara hill,
that a woman named Lakshmidāsī of that town saw this in a dream and revealed it to
Mummaḍi-Nāyaka, that, with the permission of the Nāyaka, she went about begging
for money and with the money so obtained, she formally consecrated the image of the
god on the hill and that king Mummaḍi-Nāyaka made a gift of two villages and some
lands and gardens to the said temple. The inscription gives a genealogy of Mummaḍi-
Nāyaka and says that he ruled over the country on both sides of the Gōdāvarī consisting
of Pānāra, Kōna, Kuravāṭaka, Cheṅgara etc. districts.

edition.p: After this there is a big blank space and this is followed by the following lines:—

inscription.head:
No. 555.
(A.R. No. 185 of 1905.)
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE MAṆḌAPA IN FRONT OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE
IN THE TEMPLE OF TRIPURĀNTAKĒŚVARA, TRIPURĀNTAKAM,
MARKAPUR TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
Ś. 1278 (Durmukhi)
inscription.p:
States that Aḍapa Vēmana, with the permission of Anavōtaya Reḍḍi, made gifts
of gold, rice, ghee etc., to the temple of Tripurāntaka Gaurīparamēśvari and Vighnēśvara
and also 25 cows for the upkeep of a perpetual lamp.

pautrikamu naḍapaṁgalavāṁḍu maṁgaḷamahāśrīśrī ||

inscription.head:
No. 556.
(A.R. No. 602 of 1909.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE MAṆḌAPA OF THE TEMPLE OF VĪRABHADRA, MŌṬUPALLI,
BAPATLA TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1280 (Viḷambi)
inscription.p:
States that Anavōta's minister Sōmaya issued a proclamation giving certain
assurances to traders resorting to the port of Mōṭupalli for trade purposes, remitting the
customs duties (?) known as aputrikam and kaḍḍāyamu, the duties on gold, one-third of
the duty on gandhamu vaḷubaḍi and agreeing to charge duty on other articles at the old
rates.

inscription.head:
No. 557.
(A.R. No. 368 of 1926.)
ON A SLAB PLANTED BEFORE THE TEMPLE OF VĒṆUGŌPĀLASVĀMI, TANGEḌA,
PALNAD TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1295 (Parīdhāvi)
inscription.p:
States that Avubhaḷanāthuni Dēvu-Leṅka, during the reign of Anavēma-Rēḍḍi,
put up a stone tirumuṭṭamu to the temple of Gōpināthadēva and consecrated the images
of the twelve Āḻvārs and also granted lands to the said temple. It also mentions gifts of
land made by other persons to the same temple.

inscription.head:
No. 558.
(A.R. No. 572 of 1925.)
ON A BLACK GRANITE PILLAR SET UP NEAR THE DHVAJASTAMBHA IN THE TEMPLE OF
RĀMALIṄGĒŚVARA, VĒLPŪRU, SATTENEPALLI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1295.
inscription.p:
States that Vēmāmbikā, wife of Nalla-Nūṅka had the temple of Rāmanātha
plastered with chunam and also put up a gold kalaśa on the top of the temple, besides
making other gifts on the occasion. The donor's genealogy is as follows—Doḍḍa Bhūpa,
Chiṭṭaya, Nūṅkama-Reḍḍi and Nalla-Nūṅka.

inscription.head:
No. 559.
(A.R. No. 20 of 1915.)
ON THE RIGHT AND LEFT PILLARS OF THE MAṆḌAPA ADJOINING THE NANDI-MAṆḌAPA
IN THE TEMPLE OF MALLIKĀRJUNA, ŚRĪŚAILAM, NANDIKOTKUR TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
Ś. 1299 (Piṅgaḷa)
inscription.p:
States that king Anavēmā-Reḍḍi constructed a vīramaṇṭapam in the temple of
Mallikārjuna on the Śrīśaila hill. The Sanskrit portion of this long inscription is com-
posed by Bālasarasvati, the Vidvān attached to the court of Annavēma: and Mallayya
son of Pañchabhiksha Rāmanātha was the promptor (kārayitā) of this dharma. The
inscription gives the full genealogy of the Reḍḍis up to Annavēma and also a complete
list of their birudas. It is stated in this inscription that the Reḍḍis belong to Vellacheri-
gōtra.

inscription.head:
No. 560.
(A.R. No. 46 of 1909.)
ON THE VERANDAH OF THE KITCHEN IN THE ROCK-CUT TEMPLE OF ANANTAŚAYANA,
UṆḌAVALLI, GUNTUR TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 12 • .
inscription.p:
States that Māchama-Reḍḍi, son of Annā-Reḍḍi endowed certain lands (?) for the
performance of various kinds of worship in the temple of Anantaśāyin at Uṇḍavelli.

l.te:
vēdya[mu]naku biyyaṁ | kha 1 na 5 | • • ṇya
• • sana • • •

inscription.head:
No. 561.
(A.R. No. 205 of 1905.)
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE DARK ROOM (CALLED CHĪKAṬI-MIDDI) IN THE TEMPLE OF
TRIPURĀNTAKĒŚVARA, TRIPURĀNTAKAM, MĀRKAPUR TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
Year missing.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. Gives the genealogy of the Reḍḍis down to Mācha, Vēma, Doḍḍa,
Anna and Malla, sons of Kōmaṭi Prōla, the founder of the family, by Anyamāmbā,
daughter of Doḍḍa.

edition.l:
siṁddhō śrīriva doḍḍayā dguṇanidhē jātānyamāṁbā vibhō stasyā sī[t] puruṣōttamasya

patiṁ doḍḍakṣmābhuja maṁnnabhūmiramaṇaṁ śrīmallabhūvallabhaṁ ||
(incomplete.)

inscription.head:
No. 562.
(A.R. No. 754 of 1920.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE TEMPLE OF BHĪMĒŚVARASVĀMI, MOGALLU, BHIMAVARAM
TALUK, WEST GODAVARI DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p:
States that Anavēmāya-Reḍḍi made a gift of eighteen puṭṭis of land consisting
of fields and gardens to Pramathakavi Sirigiri Dēvayya in Mōṅgolanu.

inscription.head:
No. 563.
(A.R. No. 395 of 1926.)
ON A SLAB PLANTED IN FRONT OF THE RUINED TEMPLE OF VASANTARĀYA ON THE HILL, KOTTŪR
BEZWADA TALUK, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Ś. 1312. (Pramādi)
inscription.p:
States that Kumāragiri-Reḍḍi granted certain lands and also a certain quantity
of grain in several villages to the temple of Vasantarāya Narasiṁha.

edition.l: • kṛtāṁ rājabhiḥ kṣētrādīṁ npunaruddharē tsukṛ dadhistasyāṁghriṁ [ ·a-
ti ] śubha[ṁ] dhattē mū¨rdhni || • likānana[sa]thāsēkā¨rthavāḥ khaṁḍḍikākhya[dvi]-
edition.p: Note:—Below this is engraved the head of a male person.

inscription.head:
No. 564.
(A.R. No. 242 of 1924.)
ON TWO FACES OF A PILLAR LYING IN A MOSQUE IN GŪḌŪR, BANDAR TALUK,
KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Ś. 1312. (Pramōda)
inscription.p:
Damaged. Seems to state that some one had regranted certain lands to the
temple of Jaladhīśvara at Ghaṇṭasāla.

inscription.head:
No. 565.
(A.R. No. 380 of 1926.)
ON A SLAB PLANTED IN THE RESERVE FOREST ABOUT A MILE TO THE WEST OF TAṄGEḌA,
PALNAD TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1313. (Prajāpati)
inscription.p:
States that Chokkana Siṅgana, governor of Taṅgeḍa under Komaragiri-Reḍḍi,
son of Anavōtaya-Reḍḍi, established the temples of Narasiṁha, Goddess Lakshmī, the
Āḻvārs and other dependent gods on the Anantagiri hill at Taṅgeḍa and endowed the
temples with certain lands and also the proceeds of certain customs duties, details of
which are given.

inscription.head:
No. 566.
(A.R. No. 367 of 1926.)
ON A WHITE SLAB PLANTED BEFORE THE GARUḌA-STAMBHA IN THE TEMPLE OF
VĒṆUGŌPĀLASVĀMI IN THE SAME VILLAGE.
Ś. 1316. (Bhāva)
inscription.p:
States that some one whose name is lost granted land to god Gōpināthadēva of
Taṅgeḍa for the merit of Komaragiri-Reḍḍi.

inscription.head:
No. 567.
(A.R. No. 370 of 1926.)
ON A SLAB PLANTED NEAR THE COMPOUND OF THE SAME TEMPLE.
Ś. 13 • .
inscription.p:
Damaged. States that, while Komaragiri-Reḍḍi was King and Chokkana
Siṅgana was governor of Taṅgeḍa, the said Siṅgana and certain persons referred to as
chālumūla-vubhaya-nānādēśāla pekkaṇḍru made gifts of land and customs duties to god
Gōpinātha.

l.te: svadattāṁ paradattāṁ vā yō harēta vasu-

inscription.head:
No. 568.
(A.R. No. 695 of 1926.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE TEMPLE OF BHĀVA-NĀRĀYAṆASVĀMI, SARPAVARAM, COCANADA TALUK,
EAST GODAVARI DISTRICT.
Ś. 1319. (Īśvara)
inscription.p:
States that a certain Mummana made a gift of a puṭṭi of land for a garden and
also a house-site and 20 pagodas to god Bhāvanārāyaṇa.

l.te:
rasaṁvatsara caitra śu 15 gu śrībhāvanārāya
ṇa-

inscription.head:
No. 569.
(A.R. No. 580 of 1909.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE MAṆḌAPA IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF CHENNAKĒŚAVASVĀMI AT
MĀCHERLA, PALNAD TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1319. (Īśvara)
inscription.p:
States that a certain Pērūri Muktirāju got certain repairs done to the central
shrine of Chenna-Kēśava at Mahādēvicherla.

inscription.head:
No. 570.
(A.R. No. 406 of 1915.)
ON A GARUḌA-PILLAR SET UP IN THE MAIN STREET OF PURUSHŌTTAPAṬṬAṆAM,
NARASARAOPETA TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1321. (Pramādi)
inscription.p:
Records the planting of the garuḍa-kambham (in front) of the temple of Lakshmī-
Narasiṁhadēva.

inscription.head:
No. 571.
(A.R. No. 90 of 1917.)
ON A GARUḌA-PILLAR SET UP IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF LAKSHMĪNṚISIṀHA,
KUṞNŪTALA, GUNTUR TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1326.
inscription.p:
States that king Peda-Kōmaṭi-Vēma granted the village of Kuṟnūntulūru to
god Dāśarathi of Eṟavīrapuri on the occasion of a lunar eclipse.

inscription.head:
No. 572.
(A.R. Nos. 538, 539 and 540 of 1909.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE RUINED MAṆḌAPA CALLED VARAHĀLAKOṬṬU,
KONḌAVĪḌU, NARASARAOPETA TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1327. (Pārthiva)
inscription.p:
Portions are incomplete. These little inscriptions state that certain stone pillars
(stambhas) were put up by certain persons. They contain no historical information.

Note:—This is a batch of 15 small inscriptions. In the Annual Report on Epigraphy, Madras,
these are treated as constituting three inscriptions but really each one of them is a separate inscription.
Nine of them are illegible owing to the roughness of the surface of the stone. I treat the rest as parts of
one and the same inscription and transcribe them below.

inscription.head:
No. 573.
(A.R. No. 402 of 1915.)
ON A PILLAR SET UP IN FRONT OF THE SHRINE OF ĀÑJANĒYA, APPĀPURAM,
SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Ś. 1327. (Pārthiva)
inscription.p:
States that Kāvūri Vallabhiseṭṭi got excavated a tank called Gōvardhana-
samudramu, consecrated the image of Kēśava-Perumāḷḷu on the bund of that tank and
endowed that temple with four and half puṭṭis of land with the permission of king Peda-
Kōmaṭi-Vēma.

inscription.head:
No. 574.
(A.R. No. 433 of 1911.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE COURTYARD OF THE TEMPLE OF KAILĀSANĀTHA, TANGUṬŪRU,
RAJAMPET TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
Ś. 132[8]. (Vyaya)
inscription.p:
Incomplete. Mentions Annā-Reḍḍi, son of Maḷḷā-Reḍḍi and brother of Vēmā-
Reḍḍi.

inscription.head:
No. 575.
(A.R. No. 422 of 1911.)
ON A SLAB SET UP NEAR THE ENTRANCE INTO THE TEMPLE OF CHENNAKĒŚAVA, LĒBĀKA,
SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Ś. 13[30] (?) (Sarvajit)
inscription.p:
Damaged. Refers to some charity made by one Mallā-Reḍḍi to the temple of
Karavēśvaradēva.

edition.p: Note:—The inscription is much damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 576.
(A.R. No. 599 of 1925.)
ON A STONE LYING NEAR THE HILLOCK CALLED BHAIRAVABŌḌU, VĒLPŪRU,
SATTENEPALLI TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1332. (?) Vikṛiti.
inscription.p:
States that Mādāsi Siṅgamu, the Paḍālu of Vēlpūru, made a gift of a pandumu of
land in Vēlpūru to god Kālabhairava, for the merit of Vēmā-Reḍḍi, son of Māchema-
Reḍḍi.

inscription.head:
No. 577.
(A.R. No. 84 of 1915.)
ON A SLAB SET UP ON THE WAY TO THE PĀMULĒṬI NARASIṀHA TEMPLE, UPPER
AHŌBALAM, SIRVEL TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
Ś. 1332. (Vikṛiti).
inscription.p:
States that Kāṭama-Reḍḍi Vēmā-Reḍḍi made a gift of several villages to temples
and Brahmins renaming those villages Komaragiripuram in honour of King Komara-
giri-Reḍḍi and also ten villages with the name of Vēmavaram. It also states that
Kāṭama-Reḍḍi's wife Mallamma made a gift of a village with the name of Malla-
varam to god Mārkaṇḍēśvara of Rājamahēndravaram.

inscription.head:
No. 578.
(A.R. No. 238 of 1924.)
ON A BOULDER NEAR A WELL CALLED BHĪMUḌIGUṆḌULU, ON THE TALAPUKOṆḌA HILL,
MOLLĒRU, YELLAVARAM TALUK, EAST GODAVARI DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p:
States that a certain Hanuma had the tank Vēmasamudramu excavated for the
merit of Kāṭama-Reḍḍi Vēmā-Reḍḍi.

inscription.head:
No. 579.
(A.R. No. 424 of 1911.)
ON A SLAB SET UP NEAR THE ENTRANCE INTO THE TEMPLE OF CHENNAKĒŚAVA, LĒBĀKA,
RAJAMPET TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
Ś. 1335. (Vijaya)
inscription.p:
States that Sādapalli Mallu built the prākāra (?) of the temple of Kēśava-
Perumāḷḷu of Lēmbāka in the reign of Mallā-Reḍḍi.

inscription.head:
No. 580.
(A.R. No. 582 of 1909.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE MAṆḌAPA IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF CHENNAKĒŚAVASVĀMI AT
MĀCHERLA, PALNĀD TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1336. (Jaya)
inscription.p:
States that Janyāvula Kasavā-Nāyiniṅgāru got some repairs done to the mukha-
maṇḍapa of the temple of Chennakēśava of Mahādēvicherla.

l.te: yasaṁvatsara jēṣṭa śu 5 bu mahādēvice¨rla śrīcennakēśava-
nni tama talli dēvasānikinni puṇyamugānu janyāvula kasa-

inscription.head:
No. 581.
(A.R. No. 696 of 1926.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE TEMPLE OF BHĀVANĀRĀYAṆASVĀMI, SARPAVARAM, COCANADA
TALUK, EAST GODAVARI DISTRICT.
Ś. [13]36. (Jaya)
inscription.p: Damaged and incomplete. Mentions Vēmā-Reḍḍi, son of Kāṭama-Reḍḍi.

inscription.head:
No. 582.
(A.R. No. 543 of 1909.)
ON A BOULDER CALLED PULLARIBŌḌU, AMĪNABĀDA,
SATTENAPALLI TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1337. (Manmatha)
inscription.p:
States that king Rāchavēmā-Reḍḍi had a channel called Jaganobbagaṇḍa-
kālva excavated as a supply channel to the tank Santāna-vārdhi which had been
excavated under the orders of his mother Sūrāmbā. The inscription is said to have
been composed by Śrīnātha.

l.te: yoppu migula mahanīya maina manmadhavatsaraṁbuna makhamāsa-

inscription.head:
No. 583.
(A.R. No. 578 of 1909.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE MAṆḌAPA OF THE TEMPLE OF CHENNAKĒŚAVA, MĀCHERLA,
PALNAD TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1351. (Siddhārthi)
inscription.p:
States that Chenukāli Gōpānāyiniṅgāru got certain repairs done to the
maṇḍapa in front of the temple of Chennakēśava at Mahādēvicherla.

l.te:
ri komāruṁḍu gōpānāyiniṁgāru tama taṁḍri
vīra-

inscription.head:
No. 584.
(A.R. No. 546 of 1925.)
ON A STONE BUILT INTO THE GATEWAY OF THE LOWER FORT AT KOṆḌAPALLI,
BEZWADA TALUK, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Ś. 1358. (Nala)
inscription.p:
States that Māchā-Batta got a temple erected to Pāpavināśanadēva on the
banks of the Kṛishṇā.

l.te: svasti śrī śakava¨rṣaṁbulu 1358 ·agu nēṁṭi nalasa[ṁva]tsara vaiśākha
ba 7 sō bejavāḍa mallēśvaraśrīmanmahādēvara guḍi bhōgaṁpi-

inscription.head:
No. 585.
(A.R. No. 555 of 1909.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE COURT-YARD OF THE TEMPLE OF ANKĀḶAMMA,
KĀREMPŪḌI, PALNAD TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1367. (Raktākshi)
inscription.p:
States that Jīvaraksha Timmana, son of Mācherla Chenna erected a tower at
the place where Chīlama-Nāyiḍu planted (?) his spear. Contains some human figures
bearing the names of Pina-Malidēvarāja, Tomalidēvarāja, Chīlama-Nāyiḍu, Peda-
Malidēvarāja, and Rākshati • • .

l.te: yini koṁtamu vurina cōṭa mēḍa kā-

edition.p: Below some figures are inscribed the following names:—

inscription.head:
No. 586.
(A.R. No. 792 of 1922.)
ON A SLAB BUILT INTO THE WALL TO THE LEFT OF THE ENTRANCE OF THE TEMPLE OF
GŌPĀLASVĀMI AT PARACHŪRU, BAPATLA TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1370. (Vibhava)
inscription.p:
States that Inkolani Tirumala-Reḍḍi remitted all the taxes payable on lands-
held by the temples and Brahmins of Paṟachūru.

l.te: sina pāpānaṁ bō·ināru gaṁga kaṟutanu gaṁgi-
jāḥ satata mujvaladha¨rmmacittāḥ | maddha¨rmma mēna mana-

inscription.head:
No. 587.
(A.R. No. 70 of 1917.)
ON A HANUMĀN-PILLAR SET UP NEAR THE NEW TEMPLE AT
CHINTAPALLI, SATTENAPALLI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1376. (Bhāva)
inscription.p:
Incomplete. Seems to refer to some gift made by one Raghurāya Mahāpātra
to god Chadaluvāḍa Rāghavēśvara of Koṇḍavīḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 588.
(A.R. No. 267 of 1924.)
ON A PILLAR IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF VĒṆUGŌPĀLASVĀMI, MĀGALLU,
NANDIGAMA TALUK, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Date missing.
inscription.p:
Damaged. Seems to state that a certain Muppi-Reḍḍi gave away his daughter
to the temple of Gōpāladēva and also gave two cows for that child.

This inscription is greatly damaged and illegible. The concluding portion is as
follows:—

l.te: mēḍaka komari muppiraḍi tama kūṁtu śrī-

inscription.head:
No. 589.
(A.R. No. 266 of 1924.)
ON A PILLAR IN FRONT OF THE SAME TEMPLE.
Date missing.
inscription.p: Incomplete. Refers to a gift of land by Bachchu Sūrya to god Gōpāladēva.

inscription.head:
No. 590.
(A.R. No. 692 of 1920.)
ON A PILLAR SET UP BEFORE THE TEMPLE OF BHŌGĒŚVARA AT CHILUVŪRU,
TENALI TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p:
Seems to state that Sūranārya gave away to Bhōgaliṅgēśvara the village of
Chiṟuvuli which he had obtained from king Vēma.

inscription.head:
No. 591.
(A.R. No. 372 of 1926.)
ON A SLAB PLANTED OUTSIDE THE TEMPLE OF VĒṆUGŌPĀLASVĀMI, TANGEḌA,
PALNAD TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Year missing.
inscription.p:
Damaged. Refers to gift of a pandumu of land to god Gōpinātha by a certain
Reḍḍi.

inscription.head:
No. 592.
(A.R. No. 249 of 1924.)
ON A HERO-STONE SET UP IN A FIELD TO THE SOUTH-EAST OF NANDIGĀMA,
KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p: Unintelligible. Seems to record the death of a certain Bangāru-Reḍḍi (?).

inscription.head:
No. 593.
(A.R. No. 405 of 1904.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN A FIELD BY THE SIDE OF THE ROAD FROM CHILAMKŪRU
TO MUDDANŪRU, MUDDANŪRU, JAMMALAMADUGU TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p:
States that a certain [Rē]yamayyaru (?) erected a temple of the Sun and endowed
it, on the occasion of a Solar Eclipse, with the permission of Chōḍamahārāja with a
piece of land and an oil-pressing mill.

l.te: tasya tadā phala[ṁ] | svadattāṁ paradattāṁ vā

inscription.head:
No. 594.
(A.R. No. 517 of 1906.)
ON THE DOOR-POST OF THE ENTRANCE INTO THE CENTRAL SHRINE OF THE TEMPLE OF
CHENNAKĒŚAVASVĀMI AT MUTUKŪRU, PULIVENDLA TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p:
States that while Chōḍamahārāja was ruling the Rēnāṇḍu seven thousand
a certain Bhikirāju made a gift of one hundred and twenty maṟutus of land fetching
twelve puṭṭis of paddy to a certain Śaivite mendicant Apimana-Gorava.

inscription.head:
No. 595.
(A.R. No. 466 of 1906.)
ON A PILLAR SET UP NEAR MALLĀ REḌḌI'S HOUSE,
PRODDUṬŪRU, PRODDUTUR TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p:
Damaged. Seems to record that, with the permission of a Chōḻa king (name lost)
who was ruling the Rēnāṇḍu seven thousand, some one made a gift on the occasion
of a Solar Eclipse.

inscription.head:
No. 596.
(A.R. No. 352 of 1905.)
ON A SLAB SET UP TO THE WEST OF THE ŚIVA TEMPLE AT PEDDAMUDYAM,
JAMMALAMADUGU TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
The year is lost.
inscription.p: Damaged. Seems to record that the queen of Chōḻa-mahārāja made a gift of land.

inscription.head:
No. 597.
(A.R. No. 406 of 1904.)
ON THE SAME SLAB AS A.R. NO. 405 OF 1904.
Undated.
inscription.p:
Damaged. Seems to record that during the reign of a certain Chōḻa-[mahārāja]
certain private persons made a gift of land.

inscription.head:
No. 598.
(A.R. No. 408 and 108-A of 1904.)
ON THREE FACES OF A PILLAR LYING NEAR THE THRESHING FLOOR
IN KOSINEPALLI, JAMMALAMADUGU TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p:
States that, while Chōḻa-mahārāja was ruling the Rēnāṇḍu seven thousand, one
Bādeyya Raṭṭōḍlu consecrated a temple and his wife Māraka[ṇa] and son Chāmuṇ-
ḍeya put up the inscribed slab.

edition.p: 408-A, below 408.

inscription.head:
No. 599.
(A.R. Nos. 384, 384-A and 384-B of 1904.)
ON TWO FACES OF A PILLAR SET UP IN THE COURTYARD OF THE TEMPLE OF
RĀMALIṄGĒŚVARA AT RĀMĒŚVARAM, PRODDUTUR TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
5th year of Puṇyakumāra's reign.
inscription.p:
Records the grant by Queen Vasantipōṟi-Chōḻamahādēvi to Vasantīśvara (?)
of three hundred maṟutus of land and two gardens in Viripaṟiti for the merit of
Puṇyakumāra-Pṛithivī-Vallabha-Chōḻamahārāja. It also says that twelve maṟutus
of land were given to a certain Vānapōtula Muchchiya, who was to render service to
the devotees (tapasulu) attached to the temple (?)

inscription.head:
No. 600.
(A.R. Nos. 400, 400-A and 400-B of 1904.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE TEMPLE OF AGASTYĒŚVARA, CHILAMKŪRU,
JAMMALAMADUGU TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p:
Damaged. Records a grant by the Queen of Vikramādityachōḻamahārāja.
The rest is unintelligible.

inscription.head:
No. 601.
(A.R. No. 495 of 1906.)
ON A PILLAR LYING NEAR A WELL CALLED THE KAṬṬABHĀVI ON THE WAY TO THE
MŌPŪRU TEMPLE FROM NALLACHERUVUPALLI, PULIVENDLA TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p: Damaged. Seems to record a grant of land by the Queen of a Chōḻa King.

inscription.head:
No. 602.
(A.R. No. 798 of 1917.)
ON A HERO-STONE SET UP TO THE NORTH-EAST OF THE VILLAGE OF BŪḌIDIGAḌḌAPALLI,
HINDUPUR TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p: Incomplete. Mentions Aravaḷa, son of Chōḻa-mahārāja.

inscription.head:
No. 603.
(A.R. No. 396 of 1904.)
ON A BROKEN PILLAR LYING IN A FIELD ADJOINING CHILAMKŪRU,
JAMMALAMADUGU TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p: Unintelligible. Seems to record the gift of a tank by Chōḻamahādēvi.

inscription.head:
No. 604.
(A.R. No. 474 of 1906.)
ON TWO SIDES OF A PILLAR SET UP IN THE COURT-YARD OF THE VISHṆU TEMPLE AT
ARAKAṬAVĒMULA, PRODDUTUR TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p:
Records the grant of (land ?) to Vinna Śarma, son of Vaṅganūṟla Charuva
Śarma by Bhujangadi Bhūpāditya of Perbāṇa-vaṁśa during the reign of Vallabha-
mahārāja.
