inscription.head:
No. 605.
(A.R. No. 340 of 1905.)
ON A MUTILATED SLAB LYING ON THE BANK OF THE RIVER PENNĒRU IN
DĀNAVULAPĀḌU, JAMMALAMADUGU TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p: Incomplete and unintelligible. Seems to record a gift of land.

inscription.head:
No. 606.
(A.R. No. 392 of 1904.)
ON TWO FACES OF A PILLAR LYING IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF GŌPĀLAKṚISHṆA
AT MĀLEPĀḌU, PRODDUṬUR TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p: Unintelligible. Seems to record the grant of twenty-five maṟutus of land.

inscription.head:
No. 607.
(A.R. No. 380 of 1904.)
ON TWO FACES OF A BROKEN PILLAR LYING IN THE COURT-YARD OF THE TEMPLE OF
CHENNAKĒŚAVASVĀMI AT KALAMAḶḶA, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p: Damaged and unintelligible. Mentions Dhanañjaya and Rēnā[ṇḍu].

inscription.head:
No. 608.
(A.R. No. 378 of 1904.)
ON A SLAB SET UP CLOSE TO THE EAST WALL OF THE
SHRINE OF ĀÑJANĒYA IN THE SAME TEMPLE.
Undated.
inscription.p:
States that this is the temple of Rēva-Śarmma, son of Ṟēchupa-Śarmma of the
Bhāradvāja-gōtra.

inscription.head:
No. 609.
(A.R. No. 484 of 1906.)
ON A PILLAR LYING NEAR THE MĀRIYAMMA TEMPLE IN CHAUḌŪRU,
PRODDUṬUR TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p: Unintelligible.

inscription.head:
No. 610.
(A.R. No. 469 of 1906.)
ON A STONE UNEARTHED IN THE BED OF THE STREAM AT KĀMANŪRU,
SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p: Unintelligible.

inscription.head:
No. 611.
(A.R. No. 351 of 1905.)
ON A BROKEN SLAB LYING TO THE WEST OF THE ŚIVA TEMPLE AT PEDDAMUḌIYAM,
JAMMALAMADUGU TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p:
States that whoever embezzles temple property will incur the sin of killing in
Benares.

inscription.head:
No. 612.
(A.R. No. 351-A of 1905.)
ON THE SAME SLAB.
Undated.
inscription.p:
States that the one hundred and eight (persons) made a convention that whoever
receives or pays a bribe, however small, will be damned.

edition.p:
Note:—In the Departmental Annual Report this inscription is treated as part of No. 351 but they
are two distinct inscriptions.

inscription.head:
No. 613.
(A.R. No. 518 of 1906.)
ON A PILLAR LYING IN FRONT OF THE ŚIVA TEMPLE AT MUTTUKŪRU,
PULIVENDLA TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
Year missing.
inscription.p: Damaged and unintelligible.

inscription.head:
No. 614.
(A.R. No. 515 of 1906.)
ON A PILLAR SET UP IN THE COURT-YARD OF THE TEMPLE OF CHENNAKĒŚAVASVĀMI
AT THE SAME VILLAGE.
Undated.
inscription.p: Unintelligible. Seems to state that some one fought (and fell) in Muttukūru.

inscription.head:
No. 615.
(A.R. No. 497 of 1906.)
ON A STONE LYING BEHIND THE WESTERN GŌPURA OF THE TEMPLE OF BHAIRAVA
AT MŌPŪRU, PULIVENDLA TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p: States that one Miḷḷirāju made a gift of one hundred maṟutus of land.

inscription.head:
No. 616.
(A.R. No. 337 of 1922.)
ON A BROKEN SLAB NEAR GOḌḌALŌḶḶAPALLI, HAMLET OF SŌMPALLI,
MADANAPALLE TALUK, CHITTOOR DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p: Incomplete. Refers to a gift (panasa) made by some one.

inscription.head:
No. 617.
(A.R. No. 403 of 1904.)
ON A SLAB LYING IN THE VERANDAH IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF AGASTYĒŚVARA
AT CHILAMKŪRU, PRODDUTUR TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p:
Damaged. Refers to a gift of land made by the eldest dear son of Vikram-
āditya (?).

inscription.head:
No. 618.
(A.R. No. 400 of 1926.)
ON ONE OF THE STEPS LEADING UP TO THE ŚIVA TEMPLE ON THE BANK OF THE
RIVER CHEYYĒRU, ABOUT TWO MILES TO THE WEST OF PĀTŪRU,
RAJAMPETA TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p: States that some one is a lion of the three worlds.

inscription.head:
No. 619.
(A.R. No. 433 of 1923.)
ON A STONE BUILT INTO THE LEFT SIDE OF THE ENTRANCE TO THE TEMPLE OF
RŪPĀLA SAṄGAMĒŚVARA, SAṄGAMĒŚVARAM, NANDIKOTKŪRU TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p: Unintelligible. Refers to Nakarēśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 620.
(A.R. No. 675 of 1917.)
ON A STONE SET UP IN FRONT OF THE CHĀVAḌI AT KŌṬAPĀḌU,
KOILKUNTLA TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. (?) [836.]
inscription.p: Incomplete. Refers to grant of land.
The earlier portion is entirly damaged. The later portion is as follows:—

inscription.head:
No. 621.
(A.R. No. 374 of 1904.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN A GROVE CALLED THE POLIMĒRATŌPU IN
CHINNATIPPASAMUDRAMU, MADANAPALLE TALUK, CHITTOOR DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p: States that the son of Gaṇḍa-Maykaliyya fell in a battle and went to heaven.

inscription.head:
No. 622.
(A.R. No. 288 of 1905.)
ON A SLAB SET UP NEAR THE ŚIVA TEMPLE WEST OF MADANAPALLE,
SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p:
States that a certain Chandrāditya fell in a battle over certain cattle and
went to heaven.

inscription.head:
No. 623.
(A.R. No. 294 of 1905.)
ON A BROKEN SLAB LYING IN A FIELD AT BASINIKOṆḌA,
SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p: States that some one fell in a battle. Mentions the Ḷōṅkuḷa family.

inscription.head:
No. 624.
(A.R. No. 296 of 1922.)
ON A BROKEN SLAB IN A FIELD NEAR NĀGAVŪṬI-KĀLVA TO THE WEST OF
CHIPPILI, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p: States that some one fell in a battle and went to heaven.

inscription.head:
No. 625.
(A.R. No. 300 of 1922.)
ON A SLAB LYING IN A FIELD TO THE SOUTH OF KURUBALAKŌṬA,
SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p: Unintelligible. Mentions Gaṇḍa-Seṅkali.

inscription.head:
No. 626.
(A.R. No. 301 of 1922.)
ON A SLAB STANDING IN A FIELD TO THE SOUTH OF THE SAME VILLAGE.
Undated.
inscription.p:
States that a certain Bīreya (?) fell in a battle with Sōḍas (?) and went to heaven.
Mentions Gaṇḍa-Saṅkali.

inscription.head:
No. 627.
(A.R. No. 307 of 1922.)
ON A SLAB IN A FIELD NEAR TUPĀKULAPALLI, HAMLET OF GOLLAPALLI,
SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p:
Damaged and unintelligible. Seems to record that some one granted a panasa
(rent free land) to some one.

inscription.head:
No. 628.
(A.R. No. 308 of 1922.)
ON A SLAB IN A FIELD NEAR A TANK IN MUDIVĒḌU,
SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p:
States that Śrī-Meddi (?) ruler of Kuṟṟumaḍuvu fell in the battle of Sōravāḍi
during the reign of Chandragaṇḍa-Triṇētra Vīramahārāja.

inscription.head:
No. 629.
(A.R. No. 310 of 1922.)
ON ONE OF THE THREE SLABS STANDING SIDE BY SIDE NEAR A TANK IN THE SAME VILLAGE.
Undated.
inscription.p: States that certain persons fell fighting in the Sōramaḍi battle.

inscription.head:
No. 630.
(A.R. No. 326 of 1922.)
ON A SLAB IN A FIELD TO THE NORTH OF RĀMANĀYANIKŌṬA, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p:
States that a certain Meṇṭha (?) fell in a battle with the Chōḻas (?) and went to
heaven.

inscription.head:
No. 631.
(A.R. No. 341 of 1922.)
ON A SLAB IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF MADDEMMA IN GAṄGIREḌḌIPALLI,
SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p:
States that, during the reign of Vīramahārāja, a certain Kuḷḷamma fought
with one Toṟṟukoṇṇan at Chēramaṅgala and fell.

inscription.head:
No. 632.
(A.R. No. 797 of 1917.)
ON A HERO-STONE SET UP TO THE NORTH-EAST OF THE VILLAGE OF BŪḌIDIGAḌḌAPALLI,
HAMLET OF BŪDILI, HINDUPUR TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p: States that some one fell in a battle. Mentions Pudali.

inscription.head:
No. 633.
(A.R. No. 811 of 1917.)
ON A STONE LYING IN A FIELD NEAR THE VILLAGE OF BŪḌIDIGAḌḌAPALLI,
HAMLET OF VĀNAVŌLU, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p:
States that Apimana-Gaṅgu Kessarāja of Vaṇavṟōlu constructed a charitable
tank.

inscription.head:
No. 634.
(A.R. No. 327 of 1922.)
ON TWO PIECES OF A BROKEN SLAB LYING IN A FIELD TO THE NORTH OF
RĀMANĀYANIKŌṬA, MADANAPALLE TALUK, CHITTOOR DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p: States that, during the reign of [Vai]dumba-mahārāja, a certain man fell in battle.

inscription.head:
No. 635.
(A.R. No. 338 of 1922.)
ON A SLAB IN A FIELD NEAR GĀLIVĀṆḌLAPALLE, HAMLET OF GŪḌUPALLE,
SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p:
Records that Gaṇḍatriṇētra Baydumahārāja gave a pannasa to a certain
Pāṟachāri (?)

inscription.head:
No. 636.
(A.R. No. 325 of 1905.)
ON A WHITE MARBLE SLAB SET UP BY THE SIDE OF THE CUDDAPAH-PUSHPAGIRI ROAD,
CLOSE TO THE RUINED TEMPLE CALLED THE MABBUDĒVAḶAM NEAR
UPPARAPALLI, CUDDAPAH TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
Ś. 894.
inscription.p:
States that Bhuvanatriṇētra Vaydumba-mahārāja was crowned in Śaka
year 894.

edition.p:
Note:—After the figure 8 in line 6 there is a curved line. It is presumed to be an accidental stroke not
intended to represent any figure.

inscription.head:
No. 637.
(A.R. No. 536 of 1906.)
ON A SLAB LYING IN A FIELD IN PULLAGAṆṬIVĀRIPALLI, HAMLET OF PEDDA-
TIPPASAMUDRAMU, MADANAPALLE TALUK, CHITTOOR DISTRICT.
Date not given.
inscription.p: States that some one fell in a battle.

inscription.head:
No. 638.
(A.R. No. 347 of 1922.)
ON A SLAB IN A FIELD TO THE WEST OF RANGASAMUDRAMU, SAME
TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. Seems to record a grant made on the occasion of a Lunar eclipse.
Mentions Vaidumba Mutturāja.

inscription.head:
No. 639.
(A.R. No. 342 of 1922.)
ON A SLAB TO THE NORTH OF CHENNARĀYANIPALLI, HAMLET OF TSADUMU,
SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p:
States that some one fell in a battle while Vaidumba-mahārāja surnamed
Manuja-Trinētra was ruling the Rēnāṇḍu Seven-thousand.

inscription.head:
No. 640.
(A.R. No. 309 of 1922.)
ON ONE OF THE THREE SLABS IN A FIELD NEAR A TANK IN MUDIVĒḌU
SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p:
States that Gaṇḍara Mutrāja who had been crowned by the Vaidumbas fell in
the battle of Kumuḷu (?) Mentions the Ḷōṅkuḷas.

inscription.head:
No. 641.
(A.R. No. 535 of 1906.)
ON A SLAB IN THE FIELD CALLED YĒNUGURĀTIMAḌI, PEDDA TIPPASAMUDRAMU,
SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p: Incomplete. States that some one fell in a battle.

inscription.head:
No. 642.
(A.R. No. 328 of 1922.)
ON A SLAB IN A FIELD TO THE NORTH OF RĀMANĀYANIKŌṬA, HAMLET OF
PEDDAPĀLEM, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p:
States that, while the Vaidumba king surnamed Manuja-Trinētra was ruling
the earth, a certain Ṟemma (?) fought with Vāṇarāja and fell.

inscription.head:
No. 643.
(A.R. No. 249 of 1908.)
ON A SLAB FROM ERRAKŌṬA NOW AT JAGDĀLPŪR, BASTAR STATE,
CENTRAL PROVINCES.
Ś. 945. (Rudhirōdgāri)
inscription.p: Incomplete. Mentions a certain • • • • • • bhūshaṇa-Mahārāja of the Chinda-kula.

edition.p: Note:—The letters vatsarambu ·in line 4 ·are ·engraved ·in the margin ·at right ·angles to the line.

inscription.head:
No. 644.
(A.R. No. 231 of 1908.)
ON TWO PILLARS SET UP IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF DANTĒŚVARĪ AT
BĀRSŪR, BASTAR STATE, CENTRAL PROVINCES.
Ś. 982. (Śārvari)
inscription.p:
Records the grant of land, by Chandrāditya of Chōḍa-vaṁśa, whose capital was
Bārasūra, to the temple of Chandrādityēśvara established by him on the bund of the
tank Chandrāditya-Samudra, also constructed by him during the reign of the Chinda
king Jagadēkabhūshaṇa.

edition.p: The whole of this side is illegible except the last two lines.
After several illegible lines.
Illegible.

inscription.head:
No. 645.
(A.R. No. 234 of 1908.)
ON A SLAB SET UP NEAR THE POLICE STATION AT BHAIRAMGARH, BASTAR STATE,
CENTRAL PROVINCES; REMOVED FROM JANGLA PŌTĒNĀR.
Ś. 982. (Śārvari)
inscription.p:
The first portion is in the nature of a duplicate of the above inscription. The
next portion states that Chandrāditya also established a garden called Chandrāditya-
nandanavanam. Eight persons are named as witnesses to these charities.

inscription.head:
No. 646.
(A.R. No. 243 of 1908.)
ON A SLAB LYING IN THE TREASURY ROOM IN THE TEMPLE OF DANTĒŚVARĪ,
DANTĒWĀRA, BASTAR STATE, CENTRAL PROVINCES.
Ś. 984.
inscription.p:
Damaged. Seems to state that a certain king of the Chinda family, whose capital
was Dattavāḍa, made a gift of land to a Śiva temple.

inscription.head:
No. 647.
(A.R. No. 250 of 1908.)
ON A SLAB LYING IN THE JUNGLE TO THE WEST OF GADIA, BASTAR STATE,
CENTRAL PROVINCES.
Ś. 1[0]19.
inscription.p:
Very much damaged. Refers to the Chinda king Rājabhū[shaṇa] Sōmēśvara
dēva.

inscription.head:
No. 648.
(A.R. No. 245 of 1908.)
ON A SLAB IN THE TREASURY ROOM IN THE TEMPLE OF DANTĒŚVARĪ,
DANTĒWĀRA, BASTAR STATE, CENTRAL PROVINCES.
Ś. 1147.
inscription.p:
Damaged. Mentions Jagadēkabhūshaṇa-Mahārāja alias Nārasiṁhayadēva-
Mahārāja.

inscription.head:
No. 649.
(A.R. No. 235 of 1908.)
ON A SLAB SET UP NEAR THE POLICE STATION AT BHAIRAMGARH,
BASTAR STATE, CENTRAL PROVINCES.
Undated.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. Mentions Jagadēkabhūshaṇa-Mahārāja who was a worshipper of
the feet of Māṇikyadēvī.

inscription.head:
No. 650.
(A.R. No. 242 of 1908.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE NORTH PRĀKĀRA OF THE TEMPLE OF DANTĒŚVARĪ
AT DANTĒWĀRA, BASTAR STATE, CENTRAL PROVINCES.
Ś. Unknown.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. Records a grant made by Māsakadēvi, younger sister of the
Chinda king Rājabhūshaṇa. Refers to princes (Rāchakoḍukulu) residing at Chakra-
goṭṭamu.

edition.l: vāru tama • • cina • ḷaṁgā • du [ ṭṭi
edition.p: Illegible.

inscription.head:
No. 651.
(A.R. No. 99 of 1909.)
ON A STONE LYING NEAR PARAVASTU RANGĀCHĀRYA'S HOUSE AT
VIZAGAPATAM.
Ś. 101[3] (h) 17th year of Anantavarmadēva.
inscription.p:
Records the gift of “perumbaḍi” (?) by the “city twelve” of Viśākhapaṭṭaṇamu
alias Kulōttuṅga-Chōḍapaṭṭaṇamu to Mātāmana of Malamaṇḍala. The description
of the donee is not quite intelligible.

l.te: ṁddhi dēvāṁḍiyillu mēra paścimasīma saṁddhi ·uppuṭēṟu ·uttarasīma sa-
ṁddhi [da]mmaṟummi velakuṁ goni viḍicina yillu lōpugā nullanyūṭṭuva-
gaṁgga kaṟta vēyu gavilala brāhmaṇula vadhiṁccina pātakamuṁ bo-
ṁdduduru || svadattāṁ paradattā[ṁ] vā yō harēti vasu[ṁ]ddharā[ṁ]

inscription.head:
No. 652.
(A.R. No. 642 of 1926.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE MAṆḌAPA IN FRONT OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE OF THE TEMPLE OF
NĪLAKAṆṬHĒŚVARA, NĀRĀYAṆAPURAM, BOBBILI TALUK, VIZAGAPATAM DISTRICT.
Ś. 101[7].
inscription.p:
Records the gift of a perpetual lamp by Bolli-Seṭṭi, son of Nāmana-Seṭṭi and
Kanya • ma to god Nīlīśvara.

edition.l:
ki • • • • • •
The rest is greatly damaged and illegible•

inscription.head:
No. 653.
(A.R. No. 676 of 1926.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAME MAṆḌAPA.
Ś. 1024.
inscription.p:
Records the grant by Ṟāpaṟti Sūraparāja of 35 cows for the upkeep of a
perpetual lamp in the temple of Nīlēśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 654.
(A.R. No. 641 of 1926.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAME MAṆḌAPA.
Ś. 1024.
inscription.p:
Records the grant by a Sānikāpu woman Nāgava of a piece of wet land for the
upkeep of a perpetual lamp in the temple of Nīlīśvara at Niḍuṁjeṟuvu.

inscription.head:
No. 655.
(A.R. No. 365 of 1905.)
ON A SLAB IN THE MIDDLE OF THE VILLAGE OF BHŌGĀPURAM, ANAKĀPALLI
TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 10[2]7 ([3]1st year of Anantavarmadēva)
inscription.p: Records the gift of a piece of wet land at Bhōgapuram by Lōkimāni-Seṭṭi.

inscription.head:
No. 656.
(A.R. No. 650 of 1926.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE MAṆḌAPA IN FRONT OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE OF THE TEMPLE OF
NĪLAKAṆṬHĒŚVARA, NĀRĀYAṆAPURAM, BOBBILI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
([37]th year of Anantavarmadēva.)
inscription.p:
Records the gift by a certain Permāḍi of a piece of land for the upkeep of a
perpetual lamp in the temple of Nīlīśvara at Niḍuṁjeṟuvu for the prosperity of Chōḍa-
gaṅgadēva.

inscription.head:
No. 657.
(A.R. No. 663 of 1926.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAME MAṆḌAPA.
([3]9th year of Anantavarmadēva.)
inscription.p:
Records the grant of a certain number of māḍas for the upkeep of a perpetual
lamp in the temple of Nīlīśvara of Niḍuṁjeṟvu.

inscription.head:
No. 658.
(A.R. No. 651 of 1926.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAME MAṆḌAPA.
Ś. 1039.
inscription.p:
Records the gift of a certain number of māḍas for the upkeep of perpetual lamps
established by Vīra-Permāḍirāja in the temple of Nīlīśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 659.
(A.R. No. 657 of 1926.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAME MAṆḌAPA.
Ś. 1040.
inscription.p:
Records the gift of five māḍas by Sūrama, wife of Permāḍirāja, for the upkeep
of a perpetual lamp in the temple of Nīlīśvara at Niḻuṁjeṟvu.

inscription.head:
No. 660.
(A.R. No. 658 of 1926.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAME MAṆḌAPA.
Ś. 1040.
inscription.p:
Damaged. Seems to record gift of money for the upkeep of a perpetual lamp in
the temple of Nīlīśvara. Mentions Chōḍagaṅgadēva.

inscription.head:
No. 661.
(A.R. No. 638 of 1926.)
ON A SLAB BUILT INTO THE FRONT WALL OF THE SAME TEMPLE.
Ś. 1040.
inscription.p:
Much damaged. Seems to record a gift by Bhaṭṭamaḍapalli Banāpati to the
temple of Nīlīśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 662.
(A.R. No. 671 of 1926.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE MAṆḌAPA IN FRONT OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE OF THE SAME TEMPLE.
Ś. 1014.
inscription.p:
Records the gift of a perpetual lamp by Baggumudradhāri Nāyaka to the
temple of Nīlīśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 663.
(A.R. No. 679 of 1926.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAME MAṆḌAPA.
Ś. 1044.
inscription.p:
Records the gift of five māḍas by Guṇḍama-Seṭṭi and Eṟapōta-Seṭṭi for the up-
keep of a perpetual lamp in the temple of Nīlīśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 664.
(A.R. No. 681 of 1926.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAME MAṆḌAPA.
Ś. 1044.
inscription.p:
Records the gift of five māḍas by Viṁjama for the upkeep of a perpetual lamp in
the temple of Nīlīśvara for the prosperity of Chōḻagaṅgadēva.

inscription.head:
No. 665.
(A.R. No. 674 of 1926.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAME MAṆḌAPA.
Ś. 1046.
inscription.p:
Records the gift of five māḍas by the daughter (name lost) of Rājamanika-Seṭṭi
for the upkeep of a perpetual lamp in the temple of Nīlīśvara for the prosperity of Chōḻa-
gaṅgadēva.

inscription.head:
No. 666.
(A.R. No. 692 of 1926.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAME MAṆḌAPA.
Ś. 104[6].
inscription.p:
Records the gift of five māḍas by barber Kaṇṭana for the upkeep of a perpetual
lamp in the temple of Nīlēśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 667.
(A.R. No. 682 of 1926.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAME MAṆḌAPA.
Ś. 104[7].
inscription.p: Records the gift of a perpetual lamp to the temple of Nīlīśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 668.
(A.R. No. 664 of 1926.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAME MAṆḌAPA.
Ś. 1049.
inscription.p:
Records the gift of a perpetual lamp to the temple of Nīlīśvara by the wife of
Sūraparāja.

inscription.head:
No. 669.
(A.R. No. 653 of 1926.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAME MAṆḌAPA.
Ś. 1049.
inscription.p:
Records the gift of a perpetual lamp to the temple of Nīlīśvara by a certain Ayta-
Bōya.

inscription.head:
No. 670.
(A.R. No. 678 of 1926.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAME MAṆḌAPA.
Ś. 105[0].
inscription.p: Records the gift of a perpetual lamp by Eṟṟuma-Seṭṭi.

inscription.head:
No. 671.
(A.R. No. 645 of 1926.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAME MAṆḌAPA.
Ś. 10[5]0.
inscription.p: Records the gift of a perpetual lamp to the temple of Nīlīśvara by Puravairi[This perhaps stands for the officer Puravari—C. R. K.]Sōmanātha.

inscription.head:
No. 672.
(A.R. No. 649 of 1926.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAME MAṆḌAPA.
Ś. 1051.
inscription.p:
Records the gift of a perpetual lamp to the temple of Nīlīśvara by Rājēndra-
Chōḍadēva, son of Pedda Permāḍirāja.

inscription.head:
No. 673.
(A.R. No. 667 of 1926.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAME MAṆḌAPA.
Ś. 1052.
inscription.p:
Records the gift of a perpetual lamp to the temple of Nīlīśvara during the reign of
Chōḍagaṅgadēva.

inscription.head:
No. 674.
(A.R. No. 367 of 1905.)
ON A HUGE STONE LYING IN THE MIDDLE OF THE VILLAGE OF RELLIVALASA,
VIZIANAGARAM TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1053. (57th year of Anantavarmadēva.)
inscription.p:
Records the gift of perpetual lamps by Ulaggayagoṇḍapermā[ḍi]This
gaṅga dated in S. 1040 [vide Ep. Rep for 1935-36, p. 61f, para 14—C. R. K.], younger
brother of Chōḍagaṅga, to a certain temple.

inscription.head:
No. 675.
(A.R. No. 681 of 1926.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE MAṆḌAPA IN FRONT OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE IN THE TEMPLE OF
NĪLAKAṆṬHĒŚVARA, NĀRĀYAṆAPURAM, BOBBILI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1053.
inscription.p:
Records gift of a perpetual lamp by Chōḍirājamahādēvi (and another ?) to the
temple of Nīlīśvara for the merit of Chōḍagaṅgadēva.

inscription.head:
No. 676.
(A.R. No. 656 of 1926.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAME MAṆḌAPA.
Ś. 1053.
inscription.p:
Records gift of a perpetual lamp to the temple of Nīlīśvara of Nāvapalli for the
merit of Chōḻagaṅgadēva.

inscription.head:
No. 677.
(A.R. No. 685 of 1926.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAME MAṆḌAPA.
Ś. 10[53].
inscription.p:
Records gift of a perpetual lamp to the temple of Nīlīśvara by Prōli-Seṭṭi and his
“woman” (i.e. wife) Prōlama for the merit of Chōḍagaṅgadēva.

inscription.head:
No. 678.
(A.R. No. 655 of 1926.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAME MAṆḌAPA.
Ś. 1053.
inscription.p:
Records gift of a perpetual lamp to Nīlīśvaradēva by Leṅka Gumuḍana
Gōkana in the village of Kaliṅga-[Nā]vapalli.

inscription.head:
No. 679.
(A.R. No. 688 of 1926.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAME MAṆḌAPA.
Ś. 105[4]. (59th year of Anantavarmadēva.)
inscription.p:
Records gift of a perpetual lamp to Nīlīśvara by Chōḻagaṅgadēva's younger
brother Ulayigaṇḍa-Permāḍi[See for 1 on p. 359 above—C. R., the gift to be protected by the Nāvapalli Thirty.

inscription.head:
No. 680.
(A.R. No. 661 of 1926.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAME MAṆḌAPA.
Ś. 10[55].
inscription.p: Records gift of a perpetual lamp to the temple of Nīlīśvaradēva.

inscription.head:
No. 681.
(A.R. No. 669 of 1926.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAME MAṆḌAPA.
Ś. 1055.
inscription.p: Records gift of a perpetual lamp to the temple of Nīlīśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 682.
(A.R. No. 668 of 1926.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAME MAṆḌAPA.
Ś. 1055.
inscription.p:
Records gift of a perpetual lamp to the temple of Nīlīśvaradēva by Koṟaḍlapeṇṭi
Brami-Seṭṭi for the merit of Chōḍagaṅgadēva.

inscription.head:
No. 683.
(A.R. No. 686 of 1926.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAME MAṆḌAPA.
Ś. 1055.
inscription.p:
Records gift of a perpetual lamp to the temple of Nīlīśvara at Nīḍajeṟuvu by
Sōma-Seṭṭi, son of Eṟapōta-Seṭṭi, for the merit of Chōḍagaṅgadēva.

inscription.head:
No. 684.
(A.R. No. 647 of 1926.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAME MAṆḌAPA.
Ś. 10[55].
inscription.p:
Records gift of a perpetual lamp to the temple of Nīlīśvara at Nīṟujeṟuvu by
Eṟakama and her “man” (i.e., husband) Brammana-peggaḍa.

inscription.head:
No. 685.
(A.R. No. 670 of 1926.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAME MAṆḌAPA.
Ś. 105[7].
inscription.p:
Records gift of a perpetual lamp to the temple of Nīlīśvara by Maddūripeṇṭa-
talaṟa Sōmi-Seṭṭi.

inscription.head:
No. 686.
(A.R. No. 684 of 1926.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAME MAṆḌAPA.
Ś. 105[7].
inscription.p: Records gift of a perpetual lamp to the temple of Nīlīśvaradēva.

inscription.head:
No. 687.
(A.R. No. 355 of 1905.)
ON A MUTILATED SLAB IN THE COMPOUND OF THE MAHĀRĀJA'S COLLEGE, VIZIANAGARAM,
SAME DISTRICT.
Śaka year lost ([6]1st year of Ananta[varmadēva].)
inscription.p: Damaged. Seems to record grant of land for food offering to God [Cha]ṇḍīśvara.

edition.l:
ḻusthāna • • • •
ṣome lines seem to have been lost here•

inscription.head:
No. 688.
(A.R. No. 675 of 1926.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE MAṆḌAPA IN FRONT OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE IN THE TEMPLE OF
NĪLAKAṆṬHĒŚVARA, NĀRĀYAṆAPURAM, BOBBILI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 105 • .
inscription.p:
Records gift of a perpetual lamp to the temple of Nīlīśvara by a son of a certain
Madhurāntaka-Seṭṭi.

inscription.head:
No. 689.
(A.R. No. 673 of 1926.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAME MAṆḌAPA.
Ś. 1059 (6[3]rd year of Chōḻagaṅgadēva.)
inscription.p: Records gift of a perpetual lamp to the temple of Nīlēśvara by a certain woman.

inscription.head:
No. 690.
(A.R. No. 357 of 1905.)
ON A SLAB LYING IN THE COMPOUND OF THE MAHĀRĀJA'S COLLEGE, VIZIANAGARAM,
SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1061. (65th year of Anantavarmadēva.)
inscription.p:
Records gift of 4 puṭṭis of land irrigated under a certain tank for two perpetual
lamps, for food offering and for āchārya-bhōgam in the temple of Sōmēśvara in Maṟu-
cheṟvu in Sārakināṇḍu, by Chōḍagaṅgadēva's son-in-law, with the permission of
that king.

inscription.head:
No. 691.
(A.R. No. 354 of 1905.)
ON A SLAB LYING IN THE COMPOUND OF THE MAHĀRĀJA'S COLLEGE, VIZIANAGARAM,
SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1061. (65th year of Anantavarmadēva.)
inscription.p:
Records gift of land to god Sōmēśvara in Maṟacheṟvu in Sāraki-nāṇḍu with the
permission of Chōḻagaṅga-Rāvutarāja.

inscription.head:
No. 692.
(A.R. No. 690 of 1926.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE MAṆḌAPA IN FRONT OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE IN THE TEMPLE OF
NĪLAKAṆṬHĒŚVARA, NĀRĀYAṆAPURAM, BOBBILI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1061.
inscription.p:
Records the gift of a perpetual lamp to the temple of Nīlīśvara at Niḍuṁjeṟuvu
by Rājēndra-Chōḍadēva, son of Pedda-Permā[ḍi]rāju.

inscription.head:
No. 693.
(A.R. No. 687 of 1926.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAME MAṆḌAPA.
Ś. 1063.
inscription.p: Records the gift of a perpetual lamp to the temple of Nīlīśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 694.
(A.R. No. 665 of 1926.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAME MAṆḌAPA.
Ś. 1065.
inscription.p: Records the gift of a perpetual lamp to the same temple.

inscription.head:
No. 695.
(A.R. No. 644 of 1926.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAME MAṆḌAPA.
Ś. 1066.
inscription.p:
The inscription records the grant of two perpetual lamps to the temple of
Nīlēśvara (called also Nīlīśvara) one by Mantripōta, brother of Taṇḍali Bhīmanātha,
of the Kāyastha family and the other by Bachchana, also of the Kāyastha family,
both grants being made in the same year.

inscription.head:
No. 696.
(A.R. No. 691-A of 1926.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAME MAṆḌAPA.
Ś. 1066.
inscription.p:
Records gift of a perpetual lamp to the temple of Nīlīśvara at Nāvapalli by
Mattana Eṟku-Maṇḍalika.

edition.p:
Note:—This inscription bears no number in the Annual Report. As it appears to be engraved on
the same pillar as No. 691, it is numbered here as 691-A.

inscription.head:
No. 697.
(A.R. No. 691 of 1926.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAME MAṆḌAPA.
Ś. 1066.
inscription.p: Records gift of five māḍas (?) to the temple of Nīlīśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 698.
(A.R. No. 652 of 1926.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAME MAṆḌAPA.
Ś. 1067.
inscription.p:
Records gift of a perpetual lamp to the temple of Nīlīśvara at Niḍuṁjeṟuvu by
Pritividēvi.

inscription.head:
No. 699.
(A.R. No. 636 of 1926.)
ON A SLAB FROM AKKAVARAM PRESERVED IN THE ZAMINDAR'S PALACE,
TEKKALI, TEKKALI TALUK, GANJAM DISTRICT.
Ś. 10[67].
inscription.p:
Records the construction of a temple by the Queen of Chōḻagaṅgadēva and
the gift of a perpetual lamp to that temple.

l.note:
[TheEp. Rep. 1924-25, App. A. Nos. 6 and
7. C.R.K.)].

inscription.head:
No. 700.
(A.R. No. 654 of 1926.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE MAṆḌAPA IN FRONT OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE OF THE TEMPLE OF
NĪLAKAṆṬHĒŚVARA, NĀRĀYAṆAPURAM, BOBBILI TALUK, VIZAGAPATAM DISTRICT.
Ś. 1069.
inscription.p:
Records gift of a perpetual lamp to the temple of Nīlīśvara at Nāvapalli by a
certain Pōtana.

inscription.head:
No. 701.
(A.R. No. 646 of 1926.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAME MAṆḌAPA.
Ś. 106.
inscription.p:
Records gift of a perpetual lamp to the temple of Nīlīśvara at Niḍuṁcheṟvu
by Brommari Prōlāṇḍi-Seṭṭi.

inscription.head:
No. 702.
(A.R. No. 366 of 1905.)
ON A SLAB SET UP ON A MOUND CALLED GUḌIPĀḌUDIBBA ON THE BANK OF THE RIVER
IN DVĀRAPUREḌḌIPĀḶEM, VIZIANAGARAM TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1071.
inscription.p:
Records gift of land for the upkeep of a perpetual lamp in the temple of
Chōḻagaṅgīśvara in Dharmavoramu in the Tālāṇḍi-Twelve.

l.te: yu niṁdula paṁḍreṇṭi kāṁpulu nai peṭṭiri ·ī-

inscription.head:
No. 703.
(A.R. No. 662 of 1926.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE MAṆḌAPA IN FRONT OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE IN THE TEMPLE OF
NĪLAKAṆṬHĒŚVARA, NĀRĀYAṆAPURAM, BOBBILI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 107[3].
inscription.p: Records gift of a perpetual lamp to the temple of Nīḷēśvara at Nāvapalli.

inscription.head:
No. 704.
(A.R. No. 660 of 1926.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAME MAṆḌAPA.
Ś. 10[73].
inscription.p:
Records gift of wet land for the upkeep of a perpetual lamp in the temple of
Nīlīśvara at Nāvapalli by Chōḻagaṅgadēvara Sebbana-Sēnāpati.

inscription.head:
No. 705.
(A.R. No. 659 of 1926.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAME MAṆḌAPA.
Ś. 1073.
inscription.p: Records gift of a perpetual lamp to the temple of Nīlēśvara at Nāvapalli.

inscription.head:
No. 706.
(A.R. No. 683 of 1926.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAME MAṆḌAPA.
Ś. 10[7]5.
inscription.p: Records gift of a perpetual lamp to the temple of Nīlīśvara at Niḍujeṟuvu.

inscription.head:
No. 707.
(A.R. No. 368 of 1905.)
ON A PILLAR LYING NEAR A PRIVATE THRESHING FLOOR SOUTH-WEST OF RELLIVALASA,
VIZIANAGARAM TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1075. (8th year of Anantavarmadēva.)
inscription.p:
Records gift of land, with all emoluments, in the village of Taṭaṟam in the
Tālaṇḍa-Twelve for the worship of the god, maintenance of dancing girls etc., in the
temple of Chōḻagaṅga-Mādhavadēva. The gift was made by Arusagonddalarāvuta,
brother-in-law of the king, in the presence of god Vīrabhadrēśvara, on the bank of the
river Vaṁśadhārā for the prosperity of Kāmārnavadēva.

inscription.head:
No. 708.
(A.R. No. 370 of 1905.)
ON THE SAME PILLAR.
Ś. 1075. (8th year of Anantavarmadēva.)
inscription.p:
Records gift of a perpetual lamp to the temple of Chōḻagaṅga-Mādhavadēva, by
Eṟapōta-Nāyaka, son of Nāgama-Nāyaka.

inscription.head:
No. 709.
(A.R. No. 371 of 1905.)
ON THE SAME PILLAR.
Ś. 108[1]. ([5]th year of Anantavarmadēva.)
inscription.p:
Records the conferment by Eṟapōta-Nāyaka of the office of Sthānapati of the
temple of Chōḻagaṅga-Mādhavadēva of Tālāṇḍa on Kaḍapāka Purushōttama-Bhaṭṭa
and his son Vidvajjanapriya-Brahmamārāya.

inscription.head:
No. 710.
(A.R. No. 363 of 1905.)
ON A SLAB LYING IN THE MIDDLE OF THE VILLAGE OF BHŌGĀPURAM,
BIMLIPATAM TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 110[0]. (11th year of Anantavarmadēva.)
inscription.p:
Records the construction of Rājarāja-Jinālaya in Bhōgāpuram alias Vikrama-
gaṅgavīrapēṇṭa and grant of land to the said temple by Kannama-Nāyaka.

inscription.head:
No. 711.
(A.R. No. 369 of 1905.)
ON A PILLAR LYING NEAR A PRIVATE THRESHING FLOOR SOUTH-WEST OF RELLIVALASA,
VIZIANAGARAM TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1105.
inscription.p:
Records gift of land for the upkeep of a perpetual lamp in the temple of
Chōḻagaṅga-Mādhavadēva of Tālāḍi, by Pōti-Seṭṭi.

inscription.head:
No. 712.
(A.R. No. 689 of 1926.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE MAṆḌAPA IN FRONT OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE IN THE TEMPLE OF
NĪLAKAṆṬHĒŚVARA, NĀRĀYAṆAPURAM, BOBBILI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1105.
inscription.p: Records gift of a perpetual lamp and land to the temple of Nīlīśvara at Nāvapalli.

inscription.head:
No. 713.
(A.R. No. 634 of 1926.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE TEMPLE OF HĀṬAKĒŚVARA AT SINGAVARAM,
CHICACOLE TALUK, GANJAM (NOW VIZAGAPATAM) DISTRICT.
Ś. 1127.
inscription.p: Records gift of a perpetual lamp to the temple of Koṇḍapōtidēvi, by Pura. .maṁchi-Peggaḍa.[This perhaps stands for puravari.—C. R. K.]

inscription.head:
No. 714.
(A.R. No. 359 of 1905.)
ON TWO FACES OF A PILLAR IN THE ĀSTHĀNA-MAṆḌAPA OF THE TEMPLE OF
AMARĒŚVARA, BŌṆI, BIMLIPATAM TALUK, VIZAGAPATAM DISTRICT.
Ś. 1168.
inscription.p: Records gift of two perpetual lamps to the temple of Śiva by Mēru-Mādhavārya.

inscription.head:
No. 715.
(A.R. No. 666 of 1926.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE MAṆḌAPA IN FRONT OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE IN THE TEMPLE OF
NĪLAKAṆṬHĒŚVARA, NĀRĀYAṆAPURAM, BOBBILI TALUK, VIZAGAPATAM DISTRICT.
Ś. [1]173.
inscription.p:
Records gift of perpetual lamps and land to the temple of Nīlīśvara at Niduṁ-
jeṟuvu by Ṟāpaṟiti Sūraparāju.

inscription.head:
No. 716.
(A.R. No. 648 of 1926.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAME MAṆḌAPA.
Ś. 1[1]7.
inscription.p:
Records gift of land for the up-keep of food offering to god Nīlēśvara by the
Mahāsandhivigrahi (name lost) of Chōḻagaṅgadēva.

inscription.head:
No. 717.
(A.R. No. 358 of 1905.)
ON A BEAM IN THE ĀSTHĀNA-MAṆḌAPA OF THE TEMPLE OF
AMARĒŚVARA AT BŌṆI, BIMLIPATAM TALUK, VIZAGAPATAM DISTRICT.
Ś. 1193 (?).
inscription.p:
Records gift of a perpetual lamp, a garden and a well to the temple of Amarēśvara
by General Umāpati during the reign of Bhānudēva, son of Narasiṅghadēva and
grandson of Bhīma.

inscription.head:
No. 718.
(A.R. No. 356 of 1905.)
ON A SLAB LYING IN THE COMPOUND OF THE MAHĀRĀJAH'S COLLEGE,
VIZIANAGARAM, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1204.
inscription.p: Records the consecration of the image of Gōpinātha.

inscription.head:
No. 719.
(A.R. No. 364 of 1905.)
ON A SLAB LYING IN THE MIDDLE OF THE VILLAGE OF BHŌGĀPURAM,
BIMLIPATAM TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1205. (7th year of Vīra-Naranarasiṁha.)
inscription.p:
Records gift of land to the temple of Durgādēvi of Bo[ḍḍ]apuramu during the reign
of Vīra-Naranarasiṁhya • • • • rāvutudēva.

inscription.head:
No. 720.
(A.R. No. 362 of 1905.)
ON THE RIGHT AND LEFT SIDES OF THE DOOR-POST OF THE ENTRANCE INTO THE
ĀSTHĀNA-MAṆḌAPA OF THE TEMPLE OF AMARĒŚVARA, BŌṆI, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Ś. 124[8].
inscription.p:
Records gift of sheep for the up-keep of a perpetual lamp in the temple of
Amarēśvara of Bōṇi.

inscription.head:
No. 721.
(A.R. No. 828 of 1917.)
ON A SLAB SET UP NEAR THE OUTER ENTRANCE TO THE TEMPLE OF
VAṬĒŚVARA, PĀLUR, PĀLUR ZAMINDARI, GANJAM DISTRICT.
(2nd year of Aniyaṅka-Bhīma.)
inscription.p:
Records gift of perpetual lamps and she-buffaloes to the temple of Baṭēśvara and
of land to god Bīrēśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 722.
(A.R. No. 639 of 1926.)
ON A SLAB BUILT INTO THE FRONT WALL OF THE TEMPLE OF NĪLAKAṆṬHĒŚVARA
AT NĀRĀYAṆAPURAM, BOBBILI TALUK, VIZAGAPATAM DISTRICT.
Date Lost.
inscription.p: Records gift of a lamp to the temple of Nīlīśvara at Nāvapalli.

inscription.head:
No. 723.
(A.R. No. 544 of 1925.)
ON A STONE LYING IN A STREET OF REḌḌIPĀLEM (NEAR POṬNŪRU)
BIMLIPATAM TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Year missing.
inscription.p: Damaged. Mentions the king Anantavarma Vīra-Vara-Nārasi[ṁha]dēva.

inscription.head:
No. 724.
(A.R. No. 360 of 1905.)
ON A MUTILATED SLAB IN THE ĀSTHĀNA-MAṆḌAPA OF THE TEMPLE OF AMARĒŚVARA AT
BŌṆI, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p:
Seems to record the gift of something to god A[marēśvara] of Brōṇi by
Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Vīra-Savaranāthadēva of Jantaṟunāṇḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 725.
(A.R. No. 677 of 1926.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE MAṆḌAPA IN FRONT OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE IN THE TEMPLE OF
NĪLAKAṆṬHĒŚVARA, NĀRĀYAṆAPURAM, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Ś. 1 • • .
inscription.p:
Records gift of a perpetual lamp to the temple of Nīlīśvara at Nāvapalli by the
wife of Kalva-Bhīmarāja.

inscription.head:
No. 726.
(A.R. No. 361 of 1905.)
ON A BROKEN SLAB IN THE TEMPLE OF AMARĒŚVARA AT
BŌṆI, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Year missing.
inscription.p: Damaged. Mentions the Nāga-kula.

inscription.head:
No. 727.
(A.R. No. 827 of 1917.)
ON A STONE LYING AT THE ENTRANCE INTO THE TEMPLE OF TUMBĒŚVARA,
PRATĀPUR, CHATRAPUR TALUK, GANJAM DISTRICT.
Year missing.
inscription.p: Incomplete. Mentions Anantava[rma].

edition.p:
The rest of the inscription is entirely damaged.
GAJAPATI.

inscription.head:
No. 728.
(A.R. No. 148 of 1913.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN A FIELD IN ZAKKAMPŪḌI,
BEZWADA TALUK, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p:
Records the gift of (the village) Jakkireḍḍipalle by Āmbidēvarāja, son of
Pratāpa-Kapilēśvaradēva for the up-keep of worship etc., in the temples of Pāpa-
vināśanadēva and Rudradēva, the Jaṅgam choultry and the Brahmin choultry, all at
Bejavāḍa.

inscription.head:
No. 729.
(A.R. No. 156 of 1913.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN A FIELD TO THE NORTH OF THE ROAD LEADING TO KOṆḌAPALLI,
KAVALŪRU, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p:
Records gift of land for some charity. Mentions King Purushōttamadēva and
Hamivīra.

inscription.head:
No. 730.
(A.R. No. 471 of 1915.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE VERANDAH OF THE TEMPLE OF CHENNAKĒŚAVASVĀMI,
SANTARĀVŪRU, BAPATLA TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1418 (Anala)
inscription.p:
Records that Gande Pōtaya consecrated the images of Lakshmīdēvi and
Bhūmidēvi in the temple of Chennakēśava of Rāvūru.

inscription.head:
No. 731.
(A.R. No. 469 of 1915.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAME VERANDAH.
Ś. 1418 (Nala)
inscription.p:
Records gift of land in Rāvūru by a certain Hindūrāvu-Surathāṇi Timmā-Reḍḍi
for food-offerings to goddesses Lakshmīdēvi and Bhūmidēvi in the temple of
Chennakēśava. Mentions Purushōttamadēva Gajapati as the overlord of the donor.

inscription.head:
No. 732.
(A.R. No. 802 of 1922.)
ON THE FOUR FACES OF THE GARUḌA-PILLAR PLANTED NEAR THE DHVAJASTAMBHA
IN THE TEMPLE OF CHENNAKĒŚAVA, ĪḌUPULAPĀḌU, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
Ś. 1422. (Raudri)
inscription.p:
Records the gift by Pratāpa-Rudra of the village Iḍvulapāḍu to the east of
Vinikoṇḍa, to Mādhava-Mantrin of the Bhāradvāja-gōtra and the Yājñyavalkya-śākhā.
Gives a genealogy of the Gajapatis and of the donee.

l.te: yuṁ ḍīta • ḍanaṁgaṁ danaru koṁḍaya ma-

inscription.head:
No. 733.
(A.R. No. 375 of 1926.)
ON A STONE BUILT INTO A GATE OF THE FORT AT
TANGEḌA, PALNAD TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 14[3]1 (Śukla)
inscription.p:
Damaged. Unintelligible; mentions some Khān. States that Pratāpa-Rudradēva
Gajapati was ruling.

inscription.head:
No. 734.
(A.R. No. 377 of 1905.)
ON A ROUND PILLAR IN THE RAISED VERANDAH ROUND THE CENTRAL SHRINE
IN THE TEMPLE OF LAKSHMĪ-NARASIṀHASVĀMI, SIṀHĀCHALAM, VIZAGAPATAM DISTRICT.
Ś. 1432. (Pramōda)
inscription.p:
Records the gift of two lamps and the village of Anantakapalli to the temple of
Narasiṁha at Siṁhādri by the chief Timma, son of Rāvu Nṛisiṁha, under the orders of
(Pratāpa)-Rudra-Gajapati.

inscription.head:
No. 735.
(A.R. No. 573 of 1925.)
ON A BLACK GRANITE PILLAR SET UP NEAR THE DHVAJASTAMBHA IN THE TEMPLE OF
RĀMALIṄGĒŚVARA, VĒLPŪRU, SATTENEPALLI TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1443 (Vṛisha)
inscription.p:
Records the gift of land by Chedalavāḍa Rāmānāyaniṅgāru to Kāśa Liṅgā-
battuḍu who constructed the temple of Śrī-Rāmēśvara at Vēlupunūru.

inscription.head:
No. 736.
(A.R. No. 577 of 1909.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE MAṆḌAPA OF THE TEMPLE OF CHENNAKĒŚAVASVĀMI,
MĀCHERLA, PALNAD TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1443 (Vṛisha)
inscription.p:
Records the execution of certain repairs to the temple of Chennakēśava of
Mahādēvicherla under the orders of Buyyana-Preggaḍa Gōvindarāju-Mahāpātra
the Karaṇam of Koṇḍapalli-sthala. Mentions the artisan Nāgārjunikoṇḍa Rāmōju.

l.te: gāriki(ṁ)nni tama talli ciṭṭamaṁgārikinni tama pe-

inscription.head:
No. 737.
(A.R. No. 47 of 1909.)
ON A PILLAR SET UP IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF BHĀSKARĒŚVARA,
UṆḌAVALLI, GUNTUR TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 144[8] (Pārthiva)
inscription.p:
Records that Pāpiśeṭṭi, Tippiśeṭṭi, Malliśeṭṭi and Kṛishṇamaśeṭṭi of the
Mūrukula or Murukula-gōtra built the temple of Bhāskarēśvara at Uṇḍavalli on the
banks of the Kṛishṇā and endowed that temple with a garden, a tank and some land
under that tank. This was done during the reign of Pratāparudra-Gajapati.

inscription.head:
No. 738.
(A.R. No. 822 of 1922.)
ON A SLAB LEANING AGAINST THE NORTH WALL OF THE TEMPLE OF AGASTYĒŚVARA,
KOMMŪRU, BAPATLA TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Date lost.
inscription.p:
Records the gift by Beharā-Mahāpātra Bāhubalēndra-Mahāpātra, of the entire
parīksha-basāṇam in the villages of Kommūru, Vorugāṇḍi, Rāvipāḍu and Gōṅgulamūṇḍi
in the Durgāprasāda-Koṇḍavīḍu daṇḍapāṭa for the offerings of god Mallikārjuna at
Śrīparvata. The gift was made with the consent of Pratāparudradēva-Gajapati.

inscription.head:
No. 739.
(A.R. No. 335 of 1919.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE MAṆḌAPA IN FRONT OF THE WESTERN GŌPURA IN THE TEMPLE OF
BHĪMĒŚVARA, DRĀKSHĀRĀMA, RAMACHANDRAPURAM TALUK, EAST GODAVARI DISTRICT.
Aṅka year 10 of Mukundadēva.
inscription.p:
Records that king Mukundadēva remitted the marriage and pānu duties payable
by the Balija-pekkaṇḍru and others in twenty-one places in the Rājamahēndrāvara-daṇḍa-
pāṭa. The remission was made after the king's conquest of Gauḍa-dēśa and performing
the pearl-tulāpurusha.

l.te: kinni tama taṁḍri sarvarājuṁgārikinni tama ta-

inscription.head:
No. 740.
(A.R. No. 157 of 1913.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE VILLAGE OF YENIKEPĀḌU,
MASULIPATAM TALUK, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p:
Records gift, by Komāra Hambīradēva-Mahāpātrulu to the servants of god
Rājanārāyaṇadēva of Bejavāḍa, of the village of Venakepāḍu with all sources of enjoy-
ment. Mentions the previous gift of the village to the same god by Kulōttuṅgachōḍaya-
dēva-Mahārāja.

inscription.head:
No. 741.
(A.R. No. 54 of 1912.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE TEMPLE OF KĒŚAVASVĀMI AT
CHŌḌAVARAM, VIRAVALLI TALUK, VIZAGAPATAM DISTRICT.
Śaka year not given (Kāḷayukti).
inscription.p:
Records the consecration of the image of Garutmanta by Boṇḍu Mallayya for the
prosperity etc. of Bhūpatirāvu Vallabharāja-Mahāpātra.

inscription.head:
No. 742.
(A.R. No. 55 of 1912.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE SAME TEMPLE.
Ś. 1 • • .
inscription.p:
Incomplete and unintelligible. Seems to record the grant of land for the up-keep
of a perpetual lamp in the temple of Vallabharāya.

edition.l:
kēśavasvāmiki rāmēśvaruni • • • •
(incomplete.)
Qūṭ2B ṣḥā1ḥī ḍYṇāṣṭY ·ōF ṅō1ḷKōṇ2ḍ2ā•

inscription.head:
No. 743.
(A.R. No. 549 of 1925.)
ON A STONE LYING BY THE SIDE OF THE MALKĀPURAM ROAD AT KOṆḌAPALLI,
BEZWADA TALUK, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Ś. 1452. (Khara.)
inscription.p:
Records the gift of two villages to the south of Koṇḍapalli for the up-keep of a
choultry for the poor established by Masanadaēli Quṭb-ul-Mulk, favourite of
Sultān Mahamud-Shāh.

inscription.head:
No. 744.
(A.R. No. 152 of 1913.)
ON A PILLAR LYING NEAR THE MUSSALMAN CHĀVAḌI, MALKĀPURAM,
GUNTUR TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1452.
inscription.p: An exact copy of the above inscription, No. 743.

inscription.head:
No. 745.
(A.R. No. 547 of 1925.)
ON A STONE SET UP NEAR A ERUKULA HOUSE AT KOṆḌAPALLI, BEZWADA TALUK,
KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Ś. 14[5]2 (Vikṛiti)
inscription.p:
Records that Masanadaēli Quṭb-ul-Mulk remitted the ānddiśānta-suṅkam
in the Koṇḍapalli-sīma.

inscription.head:
No. 746.
(A.R. No. 379 of 1915.)
ON A BROKEN PILLAR IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF CHENNAKĒŚAVASVĀMI
AT KŌLALAPŪḌI, NARASARAOPETA TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1487 (Akshaya).
inscription.p:
Records that Kōllapūṇḍi Śiṅgarāju consecrated the central shrine and antarāḷa
of Ādi-Kēśava, a Garuḍa-pillar and the temple of Nīlakaṇṭhēśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 747.
(A.R. No. 534 of 1913.)
ON A PILLAR LYING IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF GŌPĀLASVĀMI
AT ĪPŪRU, VINUKONDA TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1496 (Śrīmukha)
inscription.p:
Records the grant of a piece of land by Rāvūri Chennappa, son of Timmā-
Nāyuḍu to Tirumala Narasiṅgaya for the purpose of utilizing the proceeds of the land
for the daśamī services in the temple of Allāḍanātha of Īpūru.

inscription.head:
No. 748.
(A.R. No. 93 of 1917.)
ON A STONE SET UP NEAR THE KARAṆAM'S HOUSE AT MALKĀPURAM, GUNTUR TALUK,
SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1499 (Dhātu)
inscription.p:
Records the extent of land in each of the 19 villages included in the Rāvipūṇḍi-sthalam
as measured by the standard rope of thirty-eight cubits, the measurement having been
made by the villagers themselves, by mutual agreement, under the orders of Ibrāhim
Shāh. It further states that, on the guḍikat so arrived at, the Kāpus should pay full
assessment, Muhammadans half the assessment and Brahmins no assessment. Brahmins
and Muhammadans are exempt from the grazing tax.

l.te: māla prajalu samyāsaurucula tamalōṁ dāmu voppi-
cēsukonna polaṁ kolanirnnayaṁ | yīpiḍi mūra kammini
ki kuṁṭalu 360 lekhanu yiṁduku dhāsyaṁ na 3 lekhānu

inscription.head:
No. 749.
(A.R. No. 750 of 1920.)
ON A NANDI-PILLAR IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF SŌMĒŚVARA, JUTTIGA,
TANUKU TALUK, WEST GODAVARI DISTRICT.
Ś. 1505. (Svabhānu)
inscription.p:
Records that, under the orders of king Muhammand Quli Pādshāh, son of
Ibrāhīm Shāh, Sattirāju Sōmarāju's son Peddirāju and his son Sōmēśvara
consecrated certain temples etc.

inscription.head:
No. 750.
(A.R. No. 187 of 1913.)
ON A ROCK NEAR A HILL AT BODAGULO, ON THE BOYRĀṆI-KALLIKŌṬA ROAD,
GANJAM DISTRICT.
Ś. 1512. (Virōdhi)
inscription.p:
Seems to record that, while Muhammad Quli Qutb Nizam Pādshāh was ruling,
Siṅgabhūpāla of the family of Bāhubalēndra Chandra, who was ruling the Utkala
country constructed a tank called Siṅgasāgara to the west of Lāṅgalavēṇi, to the west
of Asikā-Lāṅgalavēṇi (road ?)

l.te: vēṇipaścimataṭē sthitvā taṭā[kō][tta*]mē || siṁggasāgaranā mōyaṁ taṭākō
nabhūpasya vilikhyaṁtē samāḥ | sahasrōrdhvaṁ paṁccaśataṁ tadō¨rdhvaṁ
dvādaśaṁ śarat | virōdhau caitrādipakṣē daśamyāṁ guruvāsarē cakrē laṁgga-

edition.p: The rest of the 16th line and the following 11 lines are in Uriya characters.

inscription.head:
No. 751.
(A.R. No. 541 of 1909.)
ON A SLAB NEAR THE DURGA TEMPLE ON THE HILL, AMĪNABĀD,
SATTENAPALLI TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1514. (Nandana)
inscription.p:
Records that, during the reign of Muhammad Quli Pādshāh, certain persons
rebelled against the king and that his general Malka Amī[n] suppressed the rebellion and
restored order.

inscription.head:
No. 752.
(A.R. No. 649 of 1920.)
ON A BROKEN STONE LYING IN THE TEMPLE OF SAṄGAMĒŚVARA, JĀGARLAMŪḌI,
TENALI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1520.
inscription.p:
Records the grant of some land to a certain temple in the reign of Muhammad
Quli Qutb Shāh.

edition.l:
• • • nānu gaṁga • • • •
(The rest of the stone is broken.)

inscription.head:
No. 753.
(A.R. No. 841 of 1922.)
ON A SLAB PLANTED OPPOSITE TO THE TEMPLE OF SĪTĀRĀMASVĀMI, DHARMĀVARAM,
ONGOLE TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1522. (Śārvari)
inscription.p:
Records the terms of the revenue settlement of the village of Eklāskhānapuram
near Dharmāvaram in the Addaṁki-sīma, founded by Eklāskhān, the trusted general
of Muhammad Pādshāh, son of Ibrāhim Pādshāh of Gōlkoṇḍa. Eklāskhān
was then governor of the Koṇḍavīḍu and Addanki districts.

inscription.head:
No. 754.
(A.R. No. 650 of 1920.)
ON A BROKEN STONE LYING IN THE TEMPLE OF SAṄGAMĒŚVARA, JĀGARLAMŪḌI,
TENALI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1524(?) (Śubhakṛit)
inscription.p:
Records the planting of a mango tope in the village of Chāmarlamūṇḍi in
Mṛittijānagara-sīma during the reign of Muhammad Quli Pādshāh.

inscription.head:
No. 755.
(A.R. No. 373 of 1905.)
ON A ROUND PILLAR IN THE ĀSTHĀNA-MAṆḌAPA OF THE TEMPLE OF LAKSHMĪNARASIṀHA,
SIṀHĀCHALAM, VIZAGAPATAM TALUK, VIZAGAPATAM DISTRICT.
Ś. 1526. (Krōdhi)
inscription.p:
Records the grant of the village of Narava with all its belongings to the temple of
god Nṛisiṁha at Siṁhādri, by Sarvappa Aśvarāya, son of Kamaḷaya of Vipparla-gōtra
and Padmanāyaka-kula. Aśvarāya is stated to have subdued the country consisting of
Koppulavāni-koṭṭam, Vīragoṭṭam and Yaṟajerla and repelled Mukunda Bāhubalindra
under the orders of Muhammad Quli Qutb Pādśhāh.

inscription.head:
No. 756.
(A.R. No. 579 of 1909.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE MAṆḌAPA IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF CHENNAKĒŚAVASVĀMI
AT MĀCHERLA, PALNAD TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1541. (Siddhārthi)
inscription.p:
Records gift of land for the upkeep of a perpetual lamp in the temple of Chenna-
kēśava of Mahādēvicherla by a person of the Viśvakarma-kula.

inscription.head:
No. 757.
(A.R. No. 375 of 1920.)
ON A STONE IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF ĀÑJANĒYASVĀMI, ANUMPALLI, GOOTY TALUK,
ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
Ś. [1572.]
inscription.p:
Records the scale of money payments agreed to be paid by the merchants and
other people of Anuppalli, to the temple of Gōpālasvāmi and Hanumantarāya and to
their priests. The village is stated to be situated in Jagadāpi-Gutti-sīma.

inscription.head:
No. 758.
(A.R. No. 323 of 1922.)
ON A ROCK ON THE BANK OF THE STREAM TO THE WEST OF DĒVULACHERUVU,
MADANAPALLE TALUK, CHITTOOR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1580. (Ānanda)
inscription.p:
Records the measurement of certain land in Dēvulachervu about which there
was a dispute. At this time, Ajarati was governing Gōlkoṇḍa, Chandragiri, Ghaṇḍikōṭa
etc. provinces after Rāmadēvarāya's rule at Ānegondi.

l.te: ṁḍa caṁdragiri ghaṁḍikōṭa modalaina rājyālu cēstuṁḍi [ma]khā·ālaṁ-

inscription.head:
No. 759.
(A.R. No. 128 of 1917.)
ON A PILLAR LYING IN A DONKA TO THE SOUTH OF PEDAPARIMI,
GUNTUR TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1585. (Krōdhi)
inscription.p:
Records the settlement of a land dispute between the people of Tāṁḍikoṇḍa, on
the one hand and those of Parimi, on the other.

l.te: pu tarvāta kasubē tāṁḍikoṁḍḍi tā || śya-
lēni garakasā cēstunnā rani mālmu cēśiri ·a-

inscription.head:
No. 760.
(A.R. No. 648 of 1920.)
ON A ROUGH BLACK STONE LYING IN A STREET AT ĪPŪRU, TENALI TALUK,
SAME DISTRICT.
Ś. 1590(?) (Kīlaka)
inscription.p: Records the construction of a well by Āgā Śiramā-Sāhēbulagāru.

inscription.head:
No. 761.
(A.R. No. 161 of 1913.)
ON A SLAB LYING IN THE GŌKULAMMA-CHERUVU IN KUDARAVALLI,
GUDIVADA TALUK, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
Ś. 1591 (?) (Saumya)
inscription.p: Seems to record that Abdul Husain had a tank constructed at [Dūḍapalli].

inscription.head:
No. 762.
(A.R. No. 403 of 1905.)
ON A SLAB IN FRONT OF THE MASJID AT KĀVĒRIPPĀKKAM,
ARKONAM TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
Ś. 1591. (Kīlaka)
inscription.p: Records the construction of a mosque at Kāvēripāka.

inscription.head:
No. 763.
(A.R. No. 350 of 1909.)
ON A SLAB NEAR THE TEMPLE TANK AT KŪVAM,
TIRUVALLORE TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
Ś. 1[5]9[2]. (Virōdhikṛit)
inscription.p:
Records that Pemmasāni Kumāra Timmānāyanivāru planted a plantain tope-
on the tank bund in Kuvvam which was under his government and dedicated it to
Tervikkōlēśvarasvāmi, in the reign of Qutb Shāh of Gōlkoṇḍa.

inscription.head:
No. 764.
(A.R. No. 790 of 1917.)
ON A SLAB NEAR THE ĪŚVARA TEMPLE, AT CHĀGALĒRU, HINDUPUR TALUK,
ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1599. (Piṅgaḷa)
inscription.p: Incomplete and unintelligible.

inscription.head:
No. 765.
(A.R. No. 359 of 1915.)
ON A SLAB SET UP NEAR THE TANK AT MUKTĒŚVARAM, NARASARAOPETA TALUK,
GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1600. (Kāḷayukti)
inscription.p: Records that Tēju Khān Khāvandudār had sluices constructed to a tank.

inscription.head:
No. 766.
(A.R. No. 582 of 1912.)
ON THE EAST WALL LEFT OF THE ENTRANCE INTO THE TEMPLE OF ŚIVA AT OLD
LĒPĀKSHI, HINDUPUR TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1600 (1602?). (Raudri)
inscription.p:
Records the gift of land to the temple of Śānta-Nañjuṇḍēśvara at Lēpākshi by
the Reḍḍi and Karaṇam of the Old Lēpākshi-sthalam during the reign of Sultān Abdul
Hasan.

l.te: sthānaṁ kiṁdi kōnēraṁcumaḍilō 2 yiddumu dēvaḷānaku putta-
raṁ nāgalakaṭṭakiṁda bhaṁḍaṁcumaḍilō 2 yiddumu vubhayaṁ ma-
ḍi nalutumu tōṭalu dēvaḷānaku paḍamara vallaṁbhaṭlamaḍikiṁda

edition.p: Note.—Raudri corresponds to Ś. 1602 expired and not to 1600.

inscription.head:
No. 767.
(A.R. No. 681 of 1920.)
ON A STONE LYING AT THE THRESHOLD OF THE TEMPLE OF YŌGĀNARASIṀHA,
YEḌLAPALLI, TENALI TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1602. (Raudri)
inscription.p:
Records the settlement and demarcation of the boundary between Yeḍlapalli and
Penugudurupāḍu made by Māṇikyarāvu under the orders of the king.

l.te: ma pharmānu sādiru lai vuṁḍḍaṁgānu durga-

inscription.head:
No. 768.
(A.R. No. 34 of 1912.)
ON STONES BUILT INTO A WELL IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF MĀTAṄGĒŚVARA AT
SATYAVĒḌU, PONNERI TALUK, CHINGELPUT DISTRICT.
Ś. 1608. (Kshaya)
inscription.p:
Seems to record the construction of a well to the south of the village Satyapurī
in the kingdom of Abul Hāsan of Gōlkoṇḍa by Pēṭa Kṛishṇa-Bhūpa.

l.te: svasti śrī gōlakoṁḍḍābuluhāsanuvibhōḥ kṣmātalē satyapuryāṁ ·āśāyāṁ dakṣiṇasyā
makalayadasaghō dī¨rghikāṁ pū¨rṇatōyāṁ | śrāvaṇyaṁ saurivārē kṣaya-

inscription.head:
No. 769.
(A.R. No. 299 of 1922.)
ON A BIG BOULDER TO THE NORTH OF SĀNICHERUVU IN KOṆḌAMAṞṞIPALLE,
MADANAPALLE TALUK, CHITTOOR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1613. (Prajōtpatti)
inscription.p:
Records the grant of the daśavandha rights under the tank Sānicheruvu to the
Brahmans, Kāpus and merchants who repaired it.

l.te: lu 1613 ·agu nēṭi prajōtpattisaṁvatsara mā¨rggaśira śuddha 15 lu || • •

inscription.head:
No. 770.
(A.R. No. 494 of 1906.)
ON A SLAB NEAR GOVINDA RAO'S HOUSE IN PULIVENDLA, PULLIVENDLA TALUK,
CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
Ś. 1613. (Prajōtpatti)
inscription.p:
Records the terms offered by the order of Muhammad Jāfar to induce merchants
from other places to come and settle down in Pulviṁddula which had been deserted.

l.te: ṭa pāḍai va¨rtakulu cāraka vuṁḍiri ganaka

inscription.head:
No. 771.
(A.R. No. 303 of 1922.)
ON A HUGE ROCK TO THE EAST OF THE ISUKANUTIPALLI TANK, BASINIKOṆḌA,
MADANAPALLE TALUK, CHITTOOR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1614. (Prajōtpatti)
inscription.p:
Registers the terms offered to certain persons for repairing the irrigation tank at
Baśvinakoṇḍa which had breached.

śi yiccina kavulū
ṁōṅḥūḷ ḍYṇāṣṭY ·ōF ḍēḷḥī•

inscription.head:
No. 772.
(A.R. No. 322 of 1922.)
ON A ROCK TO THE WEST OF THE VILLAGE OF BURRAKĀYALAKŌṬA, SAME TALUK
AND DISTRICT.
Ś. 16[1]8. (Īśvara)
inscription.p:
States that, on the representation of the Reḍḍi and Karaṇam of Burakāyalakōṭa,
that the old measuremet of the lands of their village was incorrect, Ināyat-Khān, the
Fauzdār of Gurramkoṇḍa, had the lands remeasured with a two-feet pole at the rate of
30 guṇṭas per tūmu. Aurangazēb was the emperor and Hazarat Akā-Khān(?) was
Subēdār of Hyderābād.

l.note:
āt the top is engraved the line:
julu e[kāri] khānubahadarasāhēbu pāccājaha [danahā]avaraṁgajēbu alaṁgiru

l.te: ḍagāruṁnnu sthalakaraṇaṁ | raṁganna modalayina peddavāruṁ śī-
malō cellē burakāyalakōṭa reḍḍikaraṇālu tama vūri maḍi
mulku reṁḍḍu ·aḍugula ghaḍanu tūmu muvai guṁṭalu

inscription.head:
No. 773.
(A.R. No. 427 of 1923.)
ON A STONE BUILT INTO THE BACK WALL OF THE RUINED TEMPLE OF PRASANNAYYA,
BOLLAVARAM, NANDIKOTKURU TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
Ś. 1619. (Īśvara)
inscription.p:
Records the gift of land by Gōparāju Prasannayya, Sthala-karaṇam of
Bollavaram, to Kṛishṇayya, Karaṇam of Prōlukallu while Aurang Pādshā was
ruling at Āmudānagaram, another name for Hastināpuram (Delhi).

l.te: 1619 ·agu nēṭi yīśvarasaṁvatsara | kārtika śu 12 lu śrīmanmahārāja rājādhi-

maṁdu ratnasiṁhvāsanārūḍhu lai pṛthivi sāmrājyaṁ cēyucuṁḍagānu kāśyapagōtra
·āpastaṁbasū-

inscription.head:
No. 774.
(A.R. No. 426 of 1923.)
ON A STONE BUILT INTO THE SAME WALL.
Ś. 1619. (Īśvara)
inscription.p:
Records that Gōparāju Prasannayya, Sthala-karaṇam of Bollavaram, granted
two tūmus of land to the architect Musalaya, on the occasion of the consecration of the
temple of Kāśī-Viśvanātha, while Aurang Pādshā was ruling at Āmudānagaram
which was another name for Hastināpuram.

l.te: yīśvarasaṁvatsa[ra] kā¨rtika śu 12 lu śrīmanmahārāja rājādhirāja śrīavaraṁgapā-
ratna siṁhvāsanārūḍhu lai pṛthivi sāmrājyaṁ cēyucuṁḍagānu | kāśyapagōtra ·āpasta-
ṁbasūtra yajuśākhādhyāyu laina bolavaraṁ sthaḷakaraṇaṁ gōparājuvāri vaṁśōdbhavu

laina prasannayagāri pautṛ laina pāparāju putṛ ḍaina prasanayagāru ·āpastaṁbasūtra
mayānagō-

tṛlaina diṁccanāla liṁgābattuni pautṛ ḍai[na] vīrābattuni putṛ ḍaina musalayaku
yiccina bhūdānadharma-
śilāśāsanaṁ nēmu kāśīviśvenādhuni pratiṣṭhacēsi dēvālayaṁ kūpapratiṣṭha nā-
ḍu māku cellivaccē bolavaraṁ grāmāna daṁḍumānyaṁ dāmagaṭla polamēranu kō-
[la]pāṭi rēgaḍa[na] 2 yidmu mīku dhārabōsi yistimi trikaraṇatrivācakatriśuddhigā dhāra-
svadattā dviguṇaṁ puṇyaṁ paradattānupālanaṁ paradattāpahārēṇa svadat[ta]ṁ niṣpha-

laṁ bhavēt || svadattā putrikā dhātrī pitṛdattā ca sōdarī paradattā gurō[ḥ]patnī vipra
dattā vasuṁdharā ||

inscription.head:
No. 775.
(A.R. No. 130 of 1923.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN A FIELD IN THE VILLAGE OF PUTTĒRI, CONJEEVARAM TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p:
Seems to record that the Kāpus and the Karaṇam of Putēri gave a piece of land
to one Timmābhaṭṭa while Ālamgīr Pādshā Avarangzēb was the emperor and
Razab Khān was Fauzdār.

inscription.head:
No. 776.
(A.R. No. 56 of 1912.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE TEMPLE OF KĒŚAVASVĀMI AT CHŌḌAVARAM, VIRAVALLI TALUK,
VIZAGAPATAM DISTRICT.
Ś. 1658. (Akshaya)
inscription.p:
Incomplete. Says that Pūsapāṭi Vijayarāmarāju, son of Śītārāmarāju was
ruling the land and mentions the god Kēśavasvāmi and the village Chōḍavaram.

inscription.head:
No. 777.
(A.R. No. 77 of 1912.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF BHŌGĪŚVARA, KONAKOṆḌLA,
GOOTY TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
Ś. 1662. (Siddhārthi)
inscription.p:
Records that, on the occasion of the consecration of the temple of Kēśavanāthasvāmi
at Konakoṇḍla, Molgu Timmāji made a gift of certain lands to one Koṇḍamayya,
presumably a trustee of the temple. The grant is said to be made for the merit of Sultān
Abdul Hasan Qutb Shāh who was, however, not king at the time.

l.te: pautṛ laina kamaḷājigāri putṛ laina timmāji-
bhaginī lōkē | sarvēṣā mēva bhūbhujāṁ | na bhō-

jyā na karagrāhyā | vipradattā vasuṁdharā || ma
dvaṁ[śa]-

edition.p: Note.—In Ś. 1662 the country was ruled not by Abdul Hasan; but by the kings of Delhi.

inscription.head:
No. 778.
(A.R. No. 360 of 1915.)
ON A SLAB SET UP NEAR THE TEMPLE OF RĀMASVĀMI IN MUKTĒŚVARAM,
NARASARAOPETA TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
Undated.
inscription.p: Simply states “the mānyam (land) of Tējōkhān's mosque.”

inscription.head:
No. 779.
(A.R. No. 362 of 1915.)
ON A STONE LYING IN A FIELD IN CHENNUPALLI AGRAHĀRAM, SAME TALUK
AND DISTRICT.
Śaka year not given (Siddhārthi)
inscription.p: States that a certain Ballulā Khān gave land to a certain mosque.

inscription.head:
No. 780.
(A.R. No. 55 of 1909.)
ON A SLAB SET UP NEAR A WELL IN THE VILLAGE OF OLD PALLĀVARAM, NEAR MADRAS.
Undated.
inscription.p:
States that one Sheik Musā Miyya Sāhib built a pēṭa, established a market, had
wells dug, formed a garden called Ibhurām Bāg after Syed Ibrāhīm Sāhib and con-
structed chāvaḍis, all at Pallāvaram.

inscription.head:
No. 781.
(A.R. No. 147 of 1921.)
ON A ROCK IN THE MAṆIYAGĀR-MĀNYAM TO THE WEST OF PARAVAKKAL,
GUDIYATTAM TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
Śaka year not given (Bahudhānya)
inscription.p:
Unintelligible. Seems to record that the agent of the Navāb Sāhib made a
money payment to one Veṅgana with the consent of the Karaṇam, etc.
